/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7827:### Finding 1: Engram splits session closure into two competing summary channels
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7829:- **What it does**: `mem_session_summary` creates an `observations` row of type `session_summary`, but it does not call `EndSession()` or update `sessions.summary`. Separately, `mem_session_end` updates `ended_at` and `sessions.summary`. `FormatContext()` renders `Recent Sessions` from the `sessions` table and `Recent Observations` from the observations table, so the mandatory summary path and the session-row summary can diverge.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7830:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest lifecycle flaw in Engram. If we add explicit session mutation APIs in Public, we should not copy a model where “summary saved” and “session closed” can silently land in different authorities.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7831:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7832:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7834:### Finding 2: Engram’s session creation is deliberately late-bound, idempotent, and reconnect-safe
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7836:- **What it does**: The OpenCode plugin calls `ensureSession()` before prompt saves, tool-hook writes, and compaction handling. `createSessionTx()` uses an upsert that fills project/directory only when they are still blank, so late registration after reconnect/reload is safe. The plugin also suppresses sub-agent sessions to avoid session inflation.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7837:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has stronger recovery than Engram, but this mutation primitive is worth studying. A lightweight session ledger with idempotent start/checkpoint semantics could harden our continuity edges without replacing `session_bootstrap`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7838:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7839:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7841:### Finding 3: User prompts are a first-class continuity stream in Engram, not just summary material
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7843:- **What it does**: Every non-trivial user message is captured through `chat.message` into `user_prompts`; MCP also exposes `mem_save_prompt`; `FormatContext()` includes recent prompts as a separate section. This means Engram preserves user intent as its own lifecycle artifact instead of expecting future sessions to infer it from a final summary.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7844:- **Why it matters for us**: In the Public code examined here, prompt preservation is centered on JSON-primary `generate-context` payloads and resume synthesis, not a dedicated always-on prompt ledger. If we want stronger “what was the user actually asking for?” continuity, this is one of Engram’s more useful ideas.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7845:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7846:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7848:### Finding 4: Engram’s resume surface is intentionally lightweight, but too shallow to be our authoritative recovery model
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7850:- **What it does**: Engram’s `mem_context` is just formatted output of recent sessions, recent prompts, recent observations, plus stats. It has no spec-folder contract, no structural readiness, no semantic-index status, and no bounded next-action guidance. It is a convenience context packet, not a composite recovery contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7851:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has the stronger pattern: `session_resume` merges resume memory, code-graph state, CocoIndex availability, and additive cached continuity; `session_bootstrap` adds health and recommended actions. Engram’s context format is fine as cheap startup seasoning, but it would be a regression as a primary recovery surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7852:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7853:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7855:### Finding 5: Engram’s compaction survival remains host-driven and ungated at the point of reuse
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7857:- **What it does**: During compaction, Engram fetches `/context` and appends it directly into the compaction payload, then injects prose telling the next agent to call `mem_session_summary`. Setup writes the same instruction family into Codex and Gemini install surfaces. There is no store-native checkpoint written at compaction time, and the reused continuity packet is not checked against scope/freshness/fingerprint gates before injection.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7858:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the lifecycle fragility behind Engram’s otherwise useful session APIs. Public’s safer pattern is already visible in the code here: autosave flows through `generate-context`, while cached continuity is additive only after explicit scope and freshness checks in `session_resume`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7859:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7860:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7906:### Finding 1: Engram splits session closure into two competing summary channels
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7908:- **What it does**: `mem_session_summary` creates an `observations` row of type `session_summary`, but it does not call `EndSession()` or update `sessions.summary`. Separately, `mem_session_end` updates `ended_at` and `sessions.summary`. `FormatContext()` renders `Recent Sessions` from the `sessions` table and `Recent Observations` from the observations table, so the mandatory summary path and the session-row summary can diverge.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7909:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest lifecycle flaw in Engram. If we add explicit session mutation APIs in Public, we should not copy a model where “summary saved” and “session closed” can silently land in different authorities.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7910:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7911:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7913:### Finding 2: Engram’s session creation is deliberately late-bound, idempotent, and reconnect-safe
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7915:- **What it does**: The OpenCode plugin calls `ensureSession()` before prompt saves, tool-hook writes, and compaction handling. `createSessionTx()` uses an upsert that fills project/directory only when they are still blank, so late registration after reconnect/reload is safe. The plugin also suppresses sub-agent sessions to avoid session inflation.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7916:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has stronger recovery than Engram, but this mutation primitive is worth studying. A lightweight session ledger with idempotent start/checkpoint semantics could harden our continuity edges without replacing `session_bootstrap`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7917:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7918:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7920:### Finding 3: User prompts are a first-class continuity stream in Engram, not just summary material
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7922:- **What it does**: Every non-trivial user message is captured through `chat.message` into `user_prompts`; MCP also exposes `mem_save_prompt`; `FormatContext()` includes recent prompts as a separate section. This means Engram preserves user intent as its own lifecycle artifact instead of expecting future sessions to infer it from a final summary.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7923:- **Why it matters for us**: In the Public code examined here, prompt preservation is centered on JSON-primary `generate-context` payloads and resume synthesis, not a dedicated always-on prompt ledger. If we want stronger “what was the user actually asking for?” continuity, this is one of Engram’s more useful ideas.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7924:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7925:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7927:### Finding 4: Engram’s resume surface is intentionally lightweight, but too shallow to be our authoritative recovery model
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7929:- **What it does**: Engram’s `mem_context` is just formatted output of recent sessions, recent prompts, recent observations, plus stats. It has no spec-folder contract, no structural readiness, no semantic-index status, and no bounded next-action guidance. It is a convenience context packet, not a composite recovery contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7930:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has the stronger pattern: `session_resume` merges resume memory, code-graph state, CocoIndex availability, and additive cached continuity; `session_bootstrap` adds health and recommended actions. Engram’s context format is fine as cheap startup seasoning, but it would be a regression as a primary recovery surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7931:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7932:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7934:### Finding 5: Engram’s compaction survival remains host-driven and ungated at the point of reuse
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7936:- **What it does**: During compaction, Engram fetches `/context` and appends it directly into the compaction payload, then injects prose telling the next agent to call `mem_session_summary`. Setup writes the same instruction family into Codex and Gemini install surfaces. There is no store-native checkpoint written at compaction time, and the reused continuity packet is not checked against scope/freshness/fingerprint gates before injection.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7937:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the lifecycle fragility behind Engram’s otherwise useful session APIs. Public’s safer pattern is already visible in the code here: autosave flows through `generate-context`, while cached continuity is additive only after explicit scope and freshness checks in `session_resume`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7938:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7939:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:1:### Finding 3: Engram’s recovery path is simple and deterministic because `FormatContext()` assembles a bounded session package
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:3:- **What it does**: `FormatContext()` always renders the same structure: recent sessions first, then recent prompts, then recent observations, with fixed windows. The OpenCode compaction hook injects `/context` output and explicitly tells the next agent to call `mem_session_summary`. Public instead recovers through `session_resume` and `session-prime`, where cached continuity is accepted only if transcript fingerprint, producer metadata, freshness window, and spec-folder scope all validate.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:4:- **Why it matters for us**: Engram’s model is easier to port across runtimes because the lifecycle contract is obvious and lightweight. Public’s recovery is more trustworthy, but it is also more runtime-aware and less universal.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:5:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:6:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8:### Finding 4: Engram treats session rows, prompts, and summaries as sync/export entities, so cross-session continuity is portable rather than purely local
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:10:- **What it does**: `CreateSession()`, `EndSession()`, and `AddPrompt()` all enqueue sync mutations; `Export()` and `Import()` include `sessions`, `observations`, and `prompts`; the HTTP surface exposes `/sessions`, `/prompts`, and `/context` as first-class lifecycle resources. Public’s strongest continuity artifacts today are local hook state, session-state SQLite rows, and memory files produced by `generate-context.js`, which are powerful but less obviously portable as a live session model.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:11:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a real architectural difference, not just packaging. Engram’s session continuity is designed to move across machines and clients as data. Public’s continuity is stronger on trust and richer synthesis, but more local in shape.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:12:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:13:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:15:### Finding 5: Public is stronger on trustworthy recovery, while Engram is stronger on explicit, agent-agnostic session lifecycle APIs
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:17:- **What it does**: Engram gives agents obvious lifecycle verbs: `mem_session_start`, `mem_session_end`, `mem_session_summary`, `mem_context`. Public instead centers on trusted sessions, `memory_context` mode resolution, `session_resume`, `session_bootstrap`, interrupted-session recovery, and cached-summary validation. In practice, Engram is easier to explain to any MCP client; Public is harder to misuse and better at rejecting stale or untrusted continuity.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:18:- **Why it matters for us**: The right move is not to replace Public’s lifecycle core. The gap is the missing thin API layer that makes our stronger internals feel as explicit as Engram’s lifecycle surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:19:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:20:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:60:Research Engram's persistent memory architecture, MCP tool design, and session lifecycle patterns to identify practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially for Spec Kit Memory. Focus on how Engram separates agent tools from admin tools, persists session history, stabilizes evolving topics with `topic_key`, and ranks or shortcuts search using SQLite + FTS5. Compare Engram's choices against current Public capabilities and classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`. Prioritize improvements that strengthen session continuity, memory hygiene, search usefulness, and agent-agnostic integration without duplicating systems Public already has.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:83:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with semantic retrieval via `memory_search`, session continuity via `memory_context`, trigger-based recall via `memory_match_triggers`, causal links, health/status tooling, and context save flows through `generate-context.js`. It also has CocoIndex for semantic code search and code-graph tooling for structural queries. What it does **not** currently have is a first-class session lifecycle API comparable to `mem_session_start` / `mem_session_end`, nor a lightweight MCP tool-profile split equivalent to Engram's `ProfileAgent` versus `ProfileAdmin`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:106:14. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and make sure the report explicitly distinguishes `adopt now`, `prototype later`, and `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:152:- What it does: `ProfileAgent` keeps proactive save/search/context/session tools available to agents, while `ProfileAdmin` isolates deletion, stats, timeline, and project-merge operations for curation workflows.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:153:- Why it matters: Public currently exposes many memory capabilities without a comparable profile layer, so a narrower agent-safe surface could reduce prompt noise and misuse risk.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:154:- Recommended action: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:164:- What it does: `AddObservation()` upserts by `topic_key` within project+scope, increments `revision_count`, and pairs that with `mem_session_summary` / `mem_session_end` so evolving work stays threadable across sessions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:165:- Why it matters: Public has strong retrieval, but it does not yet expose a similarly lightweight thread-key plus explicit lifecycle model for durable session narratives.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:166:- Recommended action: adopt now or prototype later, depending on implementation scope
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:218:- Recommendations explicitly say `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:241:### Finding N: [Title]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:243:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8167:   742	            console.warn(`[stage1-candidate-gen] Deep variant branch ${idx} rejected: ${result.reason}`);
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8686:  1082	- user required “log warning and continue instead of returning rejected” for warn-only Gates 1-3 and “do not modify ... memory-index.ts” -> keep scope tightly in `processPreparedMemory()` [Task 1]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8939:### Finding 1: Engram gives `topic_key` queries a deterministic fast path that bypasses normal FTS ranking
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8941:- **What it does**: If the query contains `/`, `Search()` first checks `topic_key = ?`, orders exact matches by `updated_at DESC`, assigns them a sentinel `Rank = -1000`, prepends them ahead of FTS results, and deduplicates the final list. `SuggestTopicKey()` creates stable families like `architecture/...`, `bug/...`, `decision/...`, and `session/...`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8942:- **Why it matters for us**: Public has trigger phrases and hybrid retrieval, but it does not have a comparable exact thread-key retrieval shortcut for evolving decisions, incidents, or recurring session narratives.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8943:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8944:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8946:### Finding 2: Engram’s FTS query sanitization is intentionally blunt and safer than raw MATCH, but narrower than Public’s lexical lane
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8948:- **What it does**: Engram turns `fix auth bug` into `"fix" "auth" "bug"` before `MATCH`, which prevents syntax blowups but keeps query logic very simple. Public normalizes lexical tokens, wraps them, joins them with `OR`, and runs weighted BM25 FTS5 over `title`, `trigger_phrases`, `file_path`, and `content_text`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8949:- **Why it matters for us**: Engram proves the value of always sanitizing user text before FTS, but Public already has a stronger lexical implementation for recall and field weighting.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8950:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8951:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8953:### Finding 3: Engram’s search stack is easy to reason about because it is basically one lexical path, while Public’s ranking power comes from a much deeper fusion pipeline
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8955:- **What it does**: Engram runs exact topic-key match plus FTS and formats preview results. Public runs Stage 1 candidate generation, hybrid/vector/lexical collection, Stage 2 fusion with session/causal/feedback signals, then Stage 3 reranking with cross-encoder, MMR, and MPAB aggregation.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8956:- **Why it matters for us**: Engram is weaker on recall and ranking quality, but much stronger on explainability. Public’s search is more capable, but much harder to debug mentally when a result looks wrong.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8957:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8958:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8960:### Finding 4: Public’s query-shape routing is a real advantage; Engram applies the same lexical recipe to every query
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8962:- **What it does**: Public classifies queries as simple/moderate/complex, can suppress channels, uses deep-mode query expansion only when warranted, and falls back gracefully. Engram always runs the same `topic_key` shortcut plus FTS flow regardless of query shape.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8963:- **Why it matters for us**: We should not simplify Public down to Engram’s fixed route. Our corpus, retrieval goals, and failure-handling needs justify route-aware search.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8964:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8965:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8967:### Finding 5: Engram’s search quality depends heavily on write-time hygiene: revisions, duplicates, and soft deletes are first-class search signals
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8969:- **What it does**: `AddObservation()` upserts by `topic_key`, increments `revision_count`, suppresses near-duplicate inserts via `normalized_hash` + time window by incrementing `duplicate_count`, and `Search()` excludes `deleted_at IS NOT NULL` in both exact and FTS paths. Those counters are exposed back to callers.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8970:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has dedup and archival filters, but it does not expose a similarly compact “this result is the latest revision of a stable thread” signal in retrieval results.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8971:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8972:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8974:### Finding 6: Public is operationally safer than Engram when the lexical lane is unhealthy
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8976:- **What it does**: Engram treats FTS as required and returns a search error if the lexical query fails. Public probes compile flags and table presence, records whether the lexical path is `fts5`, `like`, or unavailable, and fails open by returning empty lexical-lane results while preserving the rest of the retrieval pipeline.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8977:- **Why it matters for us**: This is one area where Engram’s simplicity is a liability. Public’s fallback behavior is better suited to an always-on MCP memory surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8978:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8979:- **Impact**: medium

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:9048:- **What it does**: `AddObservation()` upserts by `topic_key`, increments `revision_count`, suppresses near-duplicate inserts via `normalized_hash` + time window by incrementing `duplicate_count`, and `Search()` excludes `deleted_at IS NOT NULL` in both exact and FTS paths. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:9055:- **What it does**: Engram treats FTS as required and returns a search error if the lexical query fails. Public probes compile flags and table presence, records whether the lexical path is `fts5`, `like`, or unavailable, and fails open by returning empty lexical-lane results while preserving the rest of the retrieval pipeline.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:9056:- **Why it matters for us**: This is one area where Engram’s simplicity is a liability. Public’s fallback behavior is better suited to an always-on MCP memory surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:9057:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:9058:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:3:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:4:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6:### Finding 2: Engram’s memory hygiene is row-level and overwrite-first; Public’s is lineage-first and append-first
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8:- **What it does**: In Engram, `AddObservation()` checks `topic_key` first and updates the same row in place with `revision_count++`; only if that misses does it do normalized-hash duplicate suppression via `duplicate_count++`. Public instead creates a new append-only row when content changes and wires it into lineage/projection state.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:9:- **Why it matters for us**: The useful part is the stable thread identity, not the overwrite semantics. Engram optimizes for compactness inside one row; Public optimizes for provenance and recoverable version history.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:10:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:11:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:13:### Finding 3: Engram makes session and prompt history first-class durable data, which gives it a simpler continuity story than our split recovery path
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:15:- **What it does**: Engram stores `sessions.summary`, saves `session_summary` as a searchable observation, stores prompts in `user_prompts`, and builds startup context directly from recent sessions, prompts, and observations. Public recovery is more composite: `session_resume` merges memory context, code-graph state, CocoIndex status, cached hook summaries, and file-backed memories generated through `generate-context`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:16:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is stronger overall, but continuity is split across more layers. A small durable session/prompt ledger could reduce resume fragility after compaction or hook loss without replacing the current file-first save model.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:17:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:18:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:20:### Finding 4: Passive capture in Engram is intentionally lightweight and storage-native
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:22:- **What it does**: Engram looks only for the last `## Key Learnings`-style section, normalizes each item, dedupes by normalized hash, and saves each surviving learning as a `passive` observation. It does not run a separate review pipeline or build a special passive-memory schema.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:23:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a good example of a low-complexity adjunct rather than a new subsystem. Public could add a narrow passive-ingest helper for subagent outputs or closeout text, but it should feed into the existing `generate-context` and quality-governed save path instead of bypassing it.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:24:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:25:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:27:### Finding 5: Engram bakes replication intent directly into the storage layer through `sync_id` and mutation queues
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:29:- **What it does**: Sessions, observations, and prompts all get stable `sync_id` values; `sync_state`, `sync_mutations`, `sync_chunks`, and `sync_enrolled_projects` track outbound/inbound replication state as part of the same SQLite database. That makes export/import/sync a storage concern, not just a CLI concern.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:30:- **Why it matters for us**: Public has governance, checkpoints, and mutation/history helpers, but not this kind of explicit cross-machine mutation queue in the primary memory DB. If we ever want durable local-first sync, Engram’s queue tables are a concrete starting shape.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:31:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:32:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:73:Research Engram's persistent memory architecture, MCP tool design, and session lifecycle patterns to identify practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially for Spec Kit Memory. Focus on how Engram separates agent tools from admin tools, persists session history, stabilizes evolving topics with `topic_key`, and ranks or shortcuts search using SQLite + FTS5. Compare Engram's choices against current Public capabilities and classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`. Prioritize improvements that strengthen session continuity, memory hygiene, search usefulness, and agent-agnostic integration without duplicating systems Public already has.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:96:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with semantic retrieval via `memory_search`, session continuity via `memory_context`, trigger-based recall via `memory_match_triggers`, causal links, health/status tooling, and context save flows through `generate-context.js`. It also has CocoIndex for semantic code search and code-graph tooling for structural queries. What it does **not** currently have is a first-class session lifecycle API comparable to `mem_session_start` / `mem_session_end`, nor a lightweight MCP tool-profile split equivalent to Engram's `ProfileAgent` versus `ProfileAdmin`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:119:14. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and make sure the report explicitly distinguishes `adopt now`, `prototype later`, and `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:165:- What it does: `ProfileAgent` keeps proactive save/search/context/session tools available to agents, while `ProfileAdmin` isolates deletion, stats, timeline, and project-merge operations for curation workflows.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:166:- Why it matters: Public currently exposes many memory capabilities without a comparable profile layer, so a narrower agent-safe surface could reduce prompt noise and misuse risk.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:167:- Recommended action: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:177:- What it does: `AddObservation()` upserts by `topic_key` within project+scope, increments `revision_count`, and pairs that with `mem_session_summary` / `mem_session_end` so evolving work stays threadable across sessions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:178:- Why it matters: Public has strong retrieval, but it does not yet expose a similarly lightweight thread-key plus explicit lifecycle model for durable session narratives.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:179:- Recommended action: adopt now or prototype later, depending on implementation scope
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:231:- Recommendations explicitly say `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12579:### Finding 1: Engram’s profile split is enforced at registration time, while Public’s split is descriptive rather than executable
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12581:- **What it does**: Engram resolves `agent` and `admin` profiles into an allowlist with `ResolveTools()`, then only registers matching tools with `shouldRegister()`. Within that set, it further marks non-core tools with `WithDeferLoading(true)`, so “profile” and “load timing” are both runtime behavior, not just docs. Public already has strong L1-L7 metadata and ordered tool definitions, but it still exposes one canonical server surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12582:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest tool-surface gap. Public has the semantics needed for safe subsets, but not the runtime filtering. We could derive install-time or launch-time profiles from existing layer metadata instead of hand-maintaining separate lists.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12583:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12584:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12586:### Finding 2: Engram is not MCP-only in practice; its OpenCode integration is HTTP-first and sidecar-driven
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12588:- **What it does**: One binary exposes CLI, MCP stdio, HTTP REST, and TUI. But the OpenCode plugin does not consume MCP; it calls localhost HTTP, auto-starts `engram serve`, posts sessions/prompts/passive captures, and hits `/context` during compaction.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12589:- **Why it matters for us**: This is operationally flexible, but it creates a second integration contract beside MCP. Public’s memory system is already MCP-centric plus file-backed `generate-context`; copying Engram’s sidecar HTTP path would split authority and expand drift risk.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12590:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12591:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12593:### Finding 3: Engram’s continuity contract lives partly in APIs, but heavily in duplicated setup-time prompt text
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12595:- **What it does**: Engram writes large memory-protocol and compaction instructions into runtime-specific setup outputs, then the OpenCode plugin appends those instructions to system prompts and compaction context. The APIs exist, but host-specific instruction injection is doing a lot of the real continuity work. Public instead centralizes recovery in `session_resume` and `session_bootstrap`, and preserves save authority in JSON-primary `generate-context`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12596:- **Why it matters for us**: Engram’s approach is portable, but brittle: the behavior depends on duplicated prose across setup layers. Public’s recovery surface is already stronger than this. The only useful borrowing here is small host-specific nudges that point back to canonical recovery tools.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12597:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12598:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12600:### Finding 4: Engram treats project identity as a first-class API boundary, with normalization, drift detection, migration, and merge tooling
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12602:- **What it does**: Engram auto-detects project from git/cwd, normalizes project names, warns on similar names, exposes `POST /projects/migrate`, and offers `mem_merge_projects` for canonicalization. Project naming is the main isolation and cleanup surface above the store.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12603:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already goes further with `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, `sharedSpaceId`, and session-scoped retrieval. So Engram’s project model is weaker as a security or governance boundary. Still, its normalization and “similar project” detection are useful ergonomic patterns for local-only workflows.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12604:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12605:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12607:### Finding 5: Engram’s explicit session lifecycle APIs are good mutation primitives, but not a full recovery contract
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12609:- **What it does**: Engram has narrow session mutation surfaces: start, end, summary, plus idempotent `ensureSession()` in the plugin. That is clean. But startup recovery is still basically `mem_context` plus prompt injection, rather than one authoritative “resume everything” tool. Public already has that composite recovery contract in `session_resume` and `session_bootstrap`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12610:- **Why it matters for us**: The part worth borrowing is the durable session mutation layer, not the overall recovery model. A lightweight session ledger or explicit checkpoint write could harden Public’s continuity without replacing `session_bootstrap`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12611:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12612:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12614:### Finding 6: Engram proves install-time agent-safe profiles are operationally valuable, and Public can implement them from metadata it already has
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12616:- **What it does**: Engram setup consistently installs `engram mcp --tools=agent` into Codex and other runtimes, and even preloads Claude permissions for just that narrowed toolset. Public already has richer categorization than Engram, but it does not yet turn that metadata into installation-time or startup-time profiles.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12617:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the strongest concrete adoption candidate from the tool-surface pass. Public can expose derived profiles such as `agent-core`, `analysis`, `maintenance`, and `admin` using existing layer metadata, instead of cloning Engram’s exact two-profile maps.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12618:- **Recommendation**: adopt now

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12699:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12700:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:9:Research Engram's persistent memory architecture, MCP tool design, and session lifecycle patterns to identify practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially for Spec Kit Memory. Focus on how Engram separates agent tools from admin tools, persists session history, stabilizes evolving topics with `topic_key`, and ranks or shortcuts search using SQLite + FTS5. Compare Engram's choices against current Public capabilities and classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`. Prioritize improvements that strengthen session continuity, memory hygiene, search usefulness, and agent-agnostic integration without duplicating systems Public already has.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:32:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with semantic retrieval via `memory_search`, session continuity via `memory_context`, trigger-based recall via `memory_match_triggers`, causal links, health/status tooling, and context save flows through `generate-context.js`. It also has CocoIndex for semantic code search and code-graph tooling for structural queries. What it does **not** currently have is a first-class session lifecycle API comparable to `mem_session_start` / `mem_session_end`, nor a lightweight MCP tool-profile split equivalent to Engram's `ProfileAgent` versus `ProfileAdmin`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:55:14. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and make sure the report explicitly distinguishes `adopt now`, `prototype later`, and `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:101:- What it does: `ProfileAgent` keeps proactive save/search/context/session tools available to agents, while `ProfileAdmin` isolates deletion, stats, timeline, and project-merge operations for curation workflows.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:102:- Why it matters: Public currently exposes many memory capabilities without a comparable profile layer, so a narrower agent-safe surface could reduce prompt noise and misuse risk.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:103:- Recommended action: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:113:- What it does: `AddObservation()` upserts by `topic_key` within project+scope, increments `revision_count`, and pairs that with `mem_session_summary` / `mem_session_end` so evolving work stays threadable across sessions.
