/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5810:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/022-hybrid-rag-fusion/009-perfect-session-capturing/scratch/legacy-memory-quarantine/15-03-26_12-27__i-m-starting-with-the-repo-s-own-runbook-and.md:1009:PLEASE IMPLEMENT THIS PLAN: # Fix Plan for `009-perfect-session-capturing` Workspace Identity and Manual Validation Drift ## Summary Fix `009-perfect-session-capturing` by making the repo-local `.opencode` folder the canonical workspace identity for native session capture, then align backend matching, stateless validation, and manual docs to that contract. This is not a simple “change the root path” patch. The implementation should: - normalize backend-native paths into a shared `.opencode` workspace identity, - stop relying on raw absolute-path equality across CLIs, - prevent false `V7`/`V8` aborts caused by incomplete stateless normalization, - re-baseline `M-007` so it reflects actual shipped behavior instead of stale assumptions. ## Key Changes ### 1. Canonical workspace identity - Add a shared workspace-identity helper that derives the canonical target from the nearest repo-local `.opencode` directory. - The helper should produce an equivalence set for the active workspace, including: - the canonical `.opencode` path, - the enclosing repo root when `.opencode` is nested inside it, - normalized absolute forms used by CLI artifacts. - Replace direct project-root equality checks in native extractors with “same workspace identity” matching. - Keep `.opencode` as the source of truth; repo-root and git-root matches are accepted only when they resolve to the same workspace identity. ### 2. Backend matcher updates - Update `OpenCode`, `Claude`, `Codex`, `Copilot`, and `Gemini` capture discovery to use the shared identity helper before accepting a transcript/workspace/session. - Preserve existing backend-specific safety rules: - exact session selection preference where available, - reasoning/thought stripping, - in-project file filtering, - no cross-workspace opportunistic matching. - Make loader precedence unchanged conceptually, but evaluated after canonical identity normalization. ### 3. Stateless normalization and validation fixes - Fix stateless `TOOL_COUNT` derivation so `V7` is based on actual structured tool evidence, not just `FILES.length`. - Extend normalized capture shaping so read/search/bash-heavy sessions do not render execution artifacts while still reporting `tool_count: 0`. - Tighten prompt/exchange relevance fallback so lack of keyword match does not blindly re-include foreign-spec prompt text and trip `V8`. - Keep contamination blocking strict, but ensure valid captures for the active `.opencode` workspace are not rejected due to fallback pollution. ### 4. Manual validation and documentation truth - Update `M-007` so it explicitly states that native capture targets the repo-local `.opencode` workspace identity. - Rewrite `M-007b`, `M-007c`, and `M-007e` through `M-007i` expectations to match the corrected pipeline: - thin JSON fixture must be defined so it truly falls below threshold if abort is still intended, - alignment scenario must account for earlier gate precedence, - precedence means “first matching backend chosen,” not “first backend always yields a successful save.” - Refresh the feature catalog entry so “exact project match” is restated as “exact canonical workspace identity match via `.opencode` normalization.” - Update spec `010` evidence/acceptance text to separate: - discovery precedence, - capture normalization, - final save/index success. ## Public Interfaces / Types - Add a shared internal workspace-identity utility used by extractors and loader-facing matching. - No CLI signature changes for `generate-context.js`. - No change to JSON-mode authority semantics. - `DataSource` values remain the same. - Matching semantics change internally from raw absolute-path equality to canonical `.opencode` workspace equivalence. ## Test Plan 1. Add focused unit coverage for the workspace-identity helper. - `.opencode` path matches itself - repo root matches the same `.opencode` - git root matches the same `.opencode` - unrelated repo with a different `.opencode` is rejected - normalized symlink/trailing-slash variants remain equivalent 2. Expand extractor tests for each backend. - session accepted when artifact stores repo root but active canonical workspace is `.opencode` - session accepted when artifact stores `.opencode` - unrelated workspace rejected even if basename is similar - thought/reasoning stripping remains intact 3. Expand stateless normalization/validation tests. - `V7` no longer fires for tool-rich captures with sparse `FILES` - `V8` no longer fires because of fallback-to-all-prompt contamination when spec relevance is missing - thin JSON fixture either: - truly aborts below threshold, or - is reclassified as allowed and the docs are updated accordingly 4. Re-run the full closure stack. - `npm run lint` - `npm run build` - targeted Vitest suite for session capture, runtime memory inputs, stateless enrichment, and scorer calibration - JS regression suites - alignment drift - `spec/validate.sh` for `009-perfect-session-capturing` 5. Re-run `M-007` manually against the corrected runtime with no in-memory root override. - `M-007a` rich JSON authority - `M-007b` corrected thin-fixture expectation - `M-007c` mis-scoped stateless scenario - `M-007d` provenance-tagged enrichment - `M-007e` through `M-007i` backend precedence under canonical `.opencode` identity - `M-007j` empty-home `NO_DATA_AVAILABLE` ## Assumptions and Defaults - The user-approved contract is that repo-local `.opencode` is the only canonical workspace identity. - Repo root, git root, and backend-native stored paths are compatibility forms, not primary identifiers. - Documentation should be updated in place in the existing feature catalog entry and `M-007`, not split into new parallel docs. - If thin JSON still scores above threshold after the identity fixes, the playbook must be corrected rather than forcing an artificial abort rule that conflicts with shipped scorer behavior.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5812:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/022-hybrid-rag-fusion/009-perfect-session-capturing/scratch/legacy-memory-quarantine/15-03-26_12-27__i-m-starting-with-the-repo-s-own-runbook-and.md:1135:PLEASE IMPLEMENT THIS PLAN: # Cross-Platform Memory Sufficiency Hardening ## Summary Add one shared “is this enough context for a proper memory?” gate across the entire save surface, not just native session capture. The new rule is: - good memories save - off-spec memories still fail alignment/contamination as they do now - thin, under-evidenced, or metadata-only memories fail with a new explicit `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT` - this hard-block applies everywhere: native capture, explicit JSON, and MCP `memory_save` This plan keeps `.opencode` workspace identity and the existing fallback order, but stops treating a spec anchor, long rendered output, or auto-generated metadata as sufficient proof of memory quality. ## Key Changes ### 1. Add one shared memory-sufficiency evaluator - Introduce a shared internal module that evaluates a normalized evidence snapshot instead of relying on backend-specific heuristics. - The evaluator should return: - `pass` - `rejectionCode: 'INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT'` - `reasons` - `evidenceCounts` - `score` or normalized sufficiency value for diagnostics - Use the same pass contract across all platforms: - specific title is required - at least 1 primary evidence item is required - at least 2 total spec-relevant evidence items are required - semantic substance is required: minimum aligned text floor such as `>=250` semantic chars or `>=40` unique words - Count as primary evidence only: - meaningful file role with non-placeholder description - meaningful tool result tied to the target task/spec - concrete decision / next action / blocker / outcome - substantive observation with a non-generic title and useful narrative - Count as support evidence only: - additional aligned prompt or exchange - anchored section structure - trigger phrases that are not generic - recent context with specific request/learning - Do not count: - workspace match by itself - generic titles like `session`, `development session`, `update` - placeholder file descriptions - single anchored prompts with no other substance - synthetic/provenance enrichment unless it is clearly target-spec-specific ### 2. Harden `generate-context.js` and the native/JSON pipeline - Run the new sufficiency gate after stateless enrichment and relevance filtering, before file writing or indexing. - Apply it to: - `OpenCode` - `Claude Code` - `Codex CLI` - `Copilot CLI` - `Gemini CLI` - explicit JSON input - Keep failure types distinct: - off-spec or same-workspace wrong task: `ALIGNMENT_BLOCK` - foreign-spec dominated content: existing contamination/quality failure path (`V8`) - not enough real context even though aligned: `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT` - Tighten normalization so backend evidence survives when it is real: - preserve meaningful tool outputs - preserve file-role hints - preserve decision/next-step evidence - keep single-message anchored sessions from being inflated into valid memories - Keep the legacy 0-100 scorer for diagnostics only. - Keep v2 `quality_score` metadata, but make it reflect insufficiency strongly so thin memories score low instead of looking healthy. ### 3. Apply the same rule to MCP `memory_save` - Add the shared sufficiency gate to the server-side save path in the common handler flow, not as a session-capture-only rule. - Run order should be: 1. parse/validate 2. quality-loop auto-fixes for recoverable formatting only 3. shared sufficiency gate 4. embedding / dedup / persistence - The quality loop must not try to “repair” semantic insufficiency by inventing context. - `force: true` must not override insufficient-context rejection. - `dryRun: true` must surface the insufficiency result with reasons and evidence counts, with no write/index side effects. - The new insufficiency rejection must hard-block immediately and must not respect the older warn-only rollout behavior from the save-quality gate. ### 4. Align scoring, responses, and docs - Add a stable explicit failure contract everywhere: - CLI/runtime errors include `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT` - MCP save responses include `rejectionCode` and insufficiency details - Update server response builders and recovery hints so the user/agent sees “not enough context for a proper memory” instead of a vague generic quality failure. - Refresh the canonical docs in place: - `009-perfect-session-capturing` for capture-entry behavior - `NEW-139` feature-catalog entry - pre-storage quality gate feature doc - quality-loop feature doc - dry-run preflight feature doc - manual playbook scenarios for both capture and `memory_save` ## Public Interfaces / Types - New shared internal type such as `MemorySufficiencyResult` / `MemoryEvidenceSnapshot`. - New stable failure code: `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT`. - `generate-context.js` CLI signature does not change, but its error contract expands to include the new failure code. - `memory_save` response should add explicit insufficiency reporting, including `rejectionCode` and structured reasons/evidence. - No backend-specific save thresholds; all platforms use the same sufficiency contract after their evidence is normalized. ## Test Plan - Add direct unit coverage for the shared sufficiency evaluator: - rich specific evidence passes - single anchored prompt fails - generic title plus long content fails - placeholder file descriptions do not count - spec-specific tool output can satisfy primary evidence - Expand session-capture tests for: - same-workspace off-spec OpenCode still `ALIGNMENT_BLOCK` - rich Codex capture passes and no longer false-fails - foreign-spec Claude capture still fails the existing contamination path - thin but anchored Copilot and Gemini captures fail `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT` - thin explicit JSON fails `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT` - rich explicit JSON still passes - Expand MCP save tests for: - `memory_save({ dryRun: true })` returns explicit insufficiency result - normal save rejects insufficient memories before embedding/storage - `force: true` does not bypass the new gate - quality-loop fixes formatting issues but cannot rescue semantic insufficiency - warn-only quality-gate behavior does not bypass the new insufficiency hard block - Re-run the relevant automated stacks: - scripts lint/build/Vitest/JS suites - MCP server quality-gate, quality-loop, memory-save, and preflight suites - alignment drift - spec validation - Rework manual testing so it proves: - rich JSON/native saves still pass honestly - thin aligned saves fail explicitly - all five native backends behave consistently - `memory_save` dry-run and real save both notice insufficient context ## Assumptions And Defaults - “All platforms” means all five native CLI backends, explicit JSON input, and MCP `memory_save`. - The new `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT` is immediate and authoritative everywhere. - `force` can bypass dedup/update constraints when already supported, but not alignment, contamination, or insufficient-context gates. - The implementation should reuse existing docs where possible instead of creating parallel feature-catalog entries unless a validator or taxonomy rule requires one.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5813:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/022-hybrid-rag-fusion/009-perfect-session-capturing/scratch/legacy-memory-quarantine/15-03-26_12-27__i-m-starting-with-the-repo-s-own-runbook-and.md:1177:Analyze this issue spotted by plan Plan: Fix Memory File Generation Quality (Root Cause) Spec folder:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/022-hybrid-rag-fusion/009-perfect-session-capturing Context Generated memory files (output of generate-context.js) have systemic quality issues: missing ANCHOR tags, generic frontmatter trigger phrases, and cross-session contamination. The user's most recent memory save produced an 893-line file with zero ANCHOR tags despite the template containing 26 of them, and frontmatter trigger_phrases that say "memory dashboard" / "session summary" instead of session-specific content. Additionally, when saving from Claude Code (or any external CLI), the script's session capture detects the active OpenCode editor session instead of the calling session, leading to ALIGNMENT_BLOCK rejections or saving the wrong session's context. Root cause investigation traced these to bugs in scripts/core/workflow.ts and templates/context_template.md. --- Root Cause Analysis BUG 1: ANCHOR tags destroyed by HTML comment stripping (CRITICAL) Location: workflow.ts:108,162-179,1360-1364 Chain of events: 1. Template context_template.md contains 26 ANCHOR tags: &lt;!-- ANCHOR:id --&gt; / &lt;!-- /ANCHOR:id --&gt; 2. Step 8 (populateTemplate) renders the template correctly, anchors intact 3. Step 8.5 calls stripWorkflowHtmlOutsideCodeFences() on the rendered output 4. That function uses WORKFLOW_HTML_COMMENT_RE = //g (line 108) 5. This regex matches all HTML comments, including every ANCHOR tag 6. Line 171:.replace(WORKFLOW_HTML_COMMENT_RE, '') — deletes them all 7. Result: 0 ANCHOR tags in output file Irony: Line 538 has extractAnchorIds() that parses ANCHOR tags — but it runs after they've been destroyed. Fix: Modify the HTML comment regex to exclude ANCHOR comments: // Before (line 108): const WORKFLOW_HTML_COMMENT_RE = //g; // After: const WORKFLOW_HTML_COMMENT_RE = //g; The negative lookahead (?!\s*\/?ANCHOR:) preserves &lt;!-- ANCHOR:id --&gt; and &lt;!-- /ANCHOR:id --&gt; while still stripping all other HTML comments. BUG 2: Frontmatter trigger_phrases are hardcoded (HIGH) Location: templates/context_template.md:4-7 vs templates/context_template.md:807-811 Chain of events: 1. Template frontmatter (lines 4-7) has static values: trigger_phrases: - "memory dashboard" - "session summary" - "context template" 2. The  system spec kit/022 hybrid rag fusion/010 perfect session capturing, dry run, rejection code, no data available, memory_save, render quality, re reading, m 007, json mode, repo local, save path, target spec, tool call, current spec, auto fixes, warn only, save quality, under evidenced, hard blocks, post render, home directory, negative path, user selected, tree thinning, implementation summary, malformed generated memory files, system, spec, kit/022, hybrid, rag, fusion/010, perfect, session, capturing mustache variable IS used — but only in the YAML metadata block at line 807-811 (inside the trailing ```yaml code fence) 3. The workflow DOES extract session-specific triggers (preExtractedTriggers, line 1231-1269) and passes them as TRIGGER_PHRASES to the template (line 1315) 4. But the frontmatter section never references  system spec kit/022 hybrid rag fusion/010 perfect session capturing, dry run, rejection code, no data available, memory_save, render quality, re reading, m 007, json mode, repo local, save path, target spec, tool call, current spec, auto fixes, warn only, save quality, under evidenced, hard blocks, post render, home directory, negative path, user selected, tree thinning, implementation summary, malformed generated memory files, system, spec, kit/022, hybrid, rag, fusion/010, perfect, session, capturing — it uses hardcoded strings Fix: Replace the static frontmatter trigger_phrases with the template variable: # Before (lines 4-7): trigger_phrases: - "memory dashboard" - "session summary" - "context template" # After: trigger_phrases: {{#TRIGGER_PHRASES}} - "" {{/TRIGGER_PHRASES}}  BUG 3: Cross-session contamination in direct-path mode (HIGH) Location: workflow.ts — collectSessionData() call path in direct-path mode Chain of events: 1. User runs generate-context.js specs/015-skill-alignment from Claude Code CLI 2. The script's direct-path mode calls collectSessionData() which captures the active OpenCode editor session (e.g., "Media Editor: Convert images to webp") 3. The alignment check compares this unrelated session against the target spec folder 4. Result: 0 file matches, 0 phrase matches → ALIGNMENT_BLOCK error 5. Workaround: user must fall back to JSON file mode (/tmp/save-context-data.json) The JSON fallback produces lower quality because it lacks the rich session data (file diffs, observations, tool calls) that automatic capture provides. This creates a two-tier quality problem: sessions saved from external CLIs are always worse. Fix: When JSON data is provided (Mode 1), skip collectSessionData() entirely — the caller has already provided the session context. The alignment check should only run when using direct-path mode (Mode 2) with auto-captured session data. --- Files to Modify ┌─────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────┐ │ # │ File │ Change │ ├─────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤ │ │ │ Fix HTML comment │ │ 1 │.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts │ regex to preserve │ │ │ │ ANCHOR tags (line │ │ │ │ 108) │ ├─────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤ │ │ │ Replace hardcoded │ │ 2 │.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/templates/context_template.md │ frontmatter │ │ │ │ trigger_phrases with │ │ │ │  system spec kit/022 hybrid rag fusion/010 perfect session capturing, dry run, rejection code, no data available, memory_save, render quality, re reading, m 007, json mode, repo local, save path, target spec, tool call, current spec, auto fixes, warn only, save quality, under evidenced, hard blocks, post render, home directory, negative path, user selected, tree thinning, implementation summary, malformed generated memory files, system, spec, kit/022, hybrid, rag, fusion/010, perfect, session, capturing │ ├─────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤ │ │ │ Skip session capture │ │ 3 │.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts │ when JSON data is │ │ │ │ provided (Bug 3) │ └─────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────┘ --- Verification 1. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5840:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/022-hybrid-rag-fusion/009-perfect-session-capturing/scratch/legacy-memory-quarantine/15-03-26_12-25__how-is-used-later-in-the-template-to-match-th.md:649:Implement the following plan: # Plan: Fix Memory File Generation Quality (Root Cause) **Spec folder:** `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/022-hybrid-rag-fusion/009-perfect-session-capturing` ## Context Generated memory files (output of `generate-context.js`) have systemic quality issues: missing ANCHOR tags, generic frontmatter trigger phrases, and cross-session contamination. The user's most recent memory save produced an 893-line file with zero ANCHOR tags despite the template containing 26 of them, and frontmatter trigger_phrases that say "memory dashboard" / "session summary" instead of session-specific content. Additionally, when saving from Claude Code (or any external CLI), the script's session capture detects the active OpenCode editor session instead of the calling session, leading to ALIGNMENT_BLOCK rejections or saving the wrong session's context. Root cause investigation traced these to bugs in `scripts/core/workflow.ts` and `templates/context_template.md`. --- ## Root Cause Analysis ### BUG 1: ANCHOR tags destroyed by HTML comment stripping (CRITICAL) **Location:** `workflow.ts:108,162-179,1360-1364` **Chain of events:** 1. Template `context_template.md` contains 26 ANCHOR tags: `<!-- ANCHOR:id -->` / `<!-- /ANCHOR:id -->` 2. Step 8 (`populateTemplate`) renders the template correctly, anchors intact 3. Step 8.5 calls `stripWorkflowHtmlOutsideCodeFences()` on the rendered output 4. That function uses `WORKFLOW_HTML_COMMENT_RE = //g` (line 108) 5. This regex matches **all** HTML comments, including every ANCHOR tag 6. Line 171: `.replace(WORKFLOW_HTML_COMMENT_RE, '')` — deletes them all 7. Result: 0 ANCHOR tags in output file **Irony:** Line 538 has `extractAnchorIds()` that parses ANCHOR tags — but it runs after they've been destroyed. **Fix:** Modify the HTML comment regex to exclude ANCHOR comments: ```typescript // Before (line 108): const WORKFLOW_HTML_COMMENT_RE = /<!--[\s\S]*?-->/g; // After: const WORKFLOW_HTML_COMMENT_RE = /<!--(?!\s*\/?ANCHOR:)[\s\S]*?-->/g; ``` The negative lookahead `(?!\s*\/?ANCHOR:)` preserves `<!-- ANCHOR:id -->` and `<!-- /ANCHOR:id -->` while still stripping all other HTML comments. ### BUG 2: Frontmatter trigger_phrases are hardcoded (HIGH) **Location:** `templates/context_template.md:4-7` vs `templates/context_template.md:807-811` **Chain of events:** 1. Template frontmatter (lines 4-7) has static values: ```yaml trigger_phrases: - "memory dashboard" - "session summary" - "context template" ``` 2. The ` system spec kit/022 hybrid rag fusion/010 perfect session capturing, dry run, rejection code, no data available, memory_save, json mode, repo local, save path, target spec, tool call, current spec, auto fixes, warn only, save quality, under evidenced, hard blocks, post render, tree thinning, implementation summary, merged-small-files tree-thinning merged small, tree-thinning merged small files, one shared semantic sufficiency, evidence the workflow, cleanup the workflow, keep one ordered native, one ordered native fallback, system, spec, kit/022, hybrid, rag, fusion/010, perfect, session, capturing` mustache variable IS used — but only in the YAML metadata block at line 807-811 (inside the trailing ```` ```yaml ```` code fence) 3. The workflow DOES extract session-specific triggers (`preExtractedTriggers`, line 1231-1269) and passes them as `TRIGGER_PHRASES` to the template (line 1315) 4. But the frontmatter section never references ` system spec kit/022 hybrid rag fusion/010 perfect session capturing, dry run, rejection code, no data available, memory_save, json mode, repo local, save path, target spec, tool call, current spec, auto fixes, warn only, save quality, under evidenced, hard blocks, post render, tree thinning, implementation summary, merged-small-files tree-thinning merged small, tree-thinning merged small files, one shared semantic sufficiency, evidence the workflow, cleanup the workflow, keep one ordered native, one ordered native fallback, system, spec, kit/022, hybrid, rag, fusion/010, perfect, session, capturing` — it uses hardcoded strings **Fix:** Replace the static frontmatter trigger_phrases with the template variable: ```yaml # Before (lines 4-7): trigger_phrases: - "memory dashboard" - "session summary" - "context template" # After: trigger_phrases: {{#TRIGGER_PHRASES}} - "" {{/TRIGGER_PHRASES}}  ``` ### BUG 3: Cross-session contamination — ALREADY FIXED by 010 Spec `009-perfect-session-capturing` (completed 2026-03-15) already addresses this via: - Canonical `.opencode` workspace identity normalization (`workspace-identity.ts`) - Target-spec affinity gate (`spec-affinity.ts`) requiring anchor match beyond workspace - `ALIGNMENT_BLOCK` hard-fail for same-workspace off-spec sessions - `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT` for under-evidenced memories - Safe prompt fallback that drops foreign-spec content **No additional fix needed.** The remaining quality gap (JSON fallback producing lower-quality saves) is by design — JSON mode lacks native session data. The 010 work correctly documents this as an expected limitation. --- ## Files to Modify | # | File | Change | |---|------|--------| | 1 | `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts` | Fix HTML comment regex to preserve ANCHOR tags (line 108) | | 2 | `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/templates/context_template.md` | Replace hardcoded frontmatter trigger_phrases with ` system spec kit/022 hybrid rag fusion/010 perfect session capturing, dry run, rejection code, no data available, memory_save, json mode, repo local, save path, target spec, tool call, current spec, auto fixes, warn only, save quality, under evidenced, hard blocks, post render, tree thinning, implementation summary, merged-small-files tree-thinning merged small, tree-thinning merged small files, one shared semantic sufficiency, evidence the workflow, cleanup the workflow, keep one ordered native, one ordered native fallback, system, spec, kit/022, hybrid, rag, fusion/010, perfect, session, capturing` | --- ## Verification 1. **ANCHOR preservation:** Run `generate-context.js` on a spec folder and grep output for `ANCHOR`: ```bash grep -c 'ANCHOR' .md # Should be >0 (expect ~26 pairs) ``` 2. **Trigger phrase replacement:** Check frontmatter of generated file: ```bash head -10 .md # Should NOT contain "memory dashboard", "session summary", "context template" # Should contain session-specific phrases ``` 3. **HTML stripping still works:** Verify non-ANCHOR HTML comments are still stripped: ```bash grep -c '<!-- Template Configuration' .md # Should be 0 (template config comments still stripped) ``` 4. **Existing tests pass:** ```bash cd.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit && npm run test --workspace=scripts ``` 5. **Quality score impact:** The quality scorer at line 1434 checks `html` (noLeakedTags). /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5863:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/022-hybrid-rag-fusion/009-perfect-session-capturing/scratch/agent-output-iter-004-codex-A2.md:137:        "content": "# Outsourced agent handback protocol\n\n## 1. OVERVIEW\n\nOutsourced agent handback keeps external CLI save payloads aligned with the current `generate-context.js` contract. It enforces hard-fail validation for explicit JSON data files, persists `nextSteps` fields into memory observations, and now documents the post-010 save gates that can still reject a valid handback after normalization.\n\nWhen work is delegated to an external helper (like a different AI tool), the results need to come back in a clean format the memory system can understand. This feature makes sure that incoming data files are properly validated and that follow-up actions are captured, so nothing important gets lost when work passes between different tools.\n\n---\n\n## 2. CURRENT REALITY\n\nOutsourced-agent handback is now implemented and aligned across runtime behavior, regression tests, the 8 CLI handback docs, and the feature catalog.\n\nCurrent behavior is enforced in four slices:",

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15592:### Finding 1: Engram is built as a store-centered hub with thin delivery surfaces
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15594:- **What it does**: `cmd/engram/main.go` dispatches every surface from one binary: `serve`, `mcp`, `tui`, `search`, `save`, `context`, `sync`, `setup`. The architecture doc matches that by making `internal/store/store.go` the core, with MCP, HTTP, sync, setup, and TUI as adapters around it.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15595:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has richer retrieval than Engram, but its memory system spans many MCP tools, scripts, hooks, and supporting subsystems. Engram’s clearer “one core store, many thin surfaces” boundary is a useful architecture lens for reducing wrapper drift.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15596:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15597:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15599:### Finding 2: Engram explicitly separates agent-safe MCP tools from admin/curation tools
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15601:- **What it does**: Engram defines `ProfileAgent` and `ProfileAdmin`, resolves them through `ResolveTools()`, and pairs that with “core” versus “deferred” tool instructions. Public currently exposes a flat `TOOL_DEFINITIONS` list from the server without an equivalent profile gate.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15602:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a concrete answer to the “tool clutter” problem. Public has a much broader MCP surface, so profile-aware listing or wrapper-level profiles could reduce prompt noise and lower misuse risk without removing power-user tools.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15603:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15604:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15606:### Finding 3: Engram treats sessions, prompts, and summaries as first-class persisted objects
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15608:- **What it does**: Engram persists `sessions`, `observations`, and `user_prompts` as separate tables, exposes `mem_session_start`, `mem_session_end`, and `mem_session_summary`, then composes startup context by formatting recent sessions, prompts, and observations together. Public has strong resume/bootstrap orchestration, but it is centered on recovery composition rather than a lightweight explicit session-start/session-end API.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15609:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is already stronger at startup recovery and structural readiness, but Engram’s explicit lifecycle tools would add a simpler durable narrative boundary around a session. That is especially useful for compaction survival and for cleaner prompt/session/history analytics.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15610:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15611:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15613:### Finding 4: Engram’s memory hygiene revolves around stable topic threads plus duplicate suppression
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15615:- **What it does**: `AddObservation()` first upserts on `topic_key + project + scope`, incrementing `revision_count`; if no topic match exists, it checks a rolling duplicate window and increments `duplicate_count` instead of inserting a new row. Public’s save path is file-first and quality-gated through `generate-context.ts` and `memory_save`, but it does not expose a comparable first-class thread-key convention for evolving topics.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15616:- **Why it matters for us**: This is one of Engram’s strongest durable ideas. Public already preserves richer markdown memory artifacts, but long-running decisions and repeated refinements would benefit from an optional stable thread key so related saves revise one conceptual thread instead of fragmenting across many files.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15617:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15618:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15620:### Finding 5: Engram’s search is intentionally simple: direct topic-key shortcut first, sanitized FTS5 second
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15622:- **What it does**: If the query contains `/`, Engram tries direct `topic_key` lookup first and assigns those hits a dominant rank, then falls back to quoted FTS5 `MATCH` ordered by `fts.rank`. Public’s `memory_search` is much broader: vector + lexical + rerank + intent routing + session/causal boosts, while `memory_context` adds orchestration and graph-aware routing.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15623:- **Why it matters for us**: Engram’s lexical path is not better than Public’s hybrid stack overall, but its exact-thread shortcut is useful. The idea to copy is the deterministic “if this looks like a thread key, bypass normal ranking,” not the FTS-only architecture.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15624:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15625:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15627:### Finding 6: Agent-agnostic adoption is driven by setup/install code and hook-level protocol injection, not by core-store complexity
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15629:- **What it does**: `setup.go` writes/install-configures agent-specific MCP integrations for OpenCode, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Codex. The OpenCode adapter is explicitly “thin,” and the Claude session-start hook starts the server, creates a session, imports sync state, fetches context, and injects protocol text on startup.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15630:- **Why it matters for us**: The strong pattern here is distribution discipline: keep setup friction low, keep hooks small, and keep behavioral protocol near the runtime seam. Public already supports more advanced runtime recovery, but Engram’s packaging is cleaner and easier to reason about.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15631:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15632:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15667:Deep-dive the implementation semantics inside Engram’s `AddObservation()`, `SuggestTopicKey()`, `ExtractLearnings()`, and the MCP handlers for `mem_session_summary` and `mem_capture_passive`, then map those directly against Public’s `memory_save`, trigger/cognitive recall, and session-resume/bootstrap paths to separate `adopt now` from `prototype later` more decisively.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15678:### Finding 1: Engram is built as a store-centered hub with thin delivery surfaces
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15680:- **What it does**: `cmd/engram/main.go` dispatches every surface from one binary: `serve`, `mcp`, `tui`, `search`, `save`, `context`, `sync`, `setup`. The architecture doc matches that by making `internal/store/store.go` the core, with MCP, HTTP, sync, setup, and TUI as adapters around it.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15681:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has richer retrieval than Engram, but its memory system spans many MCP tools, scripts, hooks, and supporting subsystems. Engram’s clearer “one core store, many thin surfaces” boundary is a useful architecture lens for reducing wrapper drift.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15682:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15683:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15685:### Finding 2: Engram explicitly separates agent-safe MCP tools from admin/curation tools
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15687:- **What it does**: Engram defines `ProfileAgent` and `ProfileAdmin`, resolves them through `ResolveTools()`, and pairs that with “core” versus “deferred” tool instructions. Public currently exposes a flat `TOOL_DEFINITIONS` list from the server without an equivalent profile gate.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15688:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a concrete answer to the “tool clutter” problem. Public has a much broader MCP surface, so profile-aware listing or wrapper-level profiles could reduce prompt noise and lower misuse risk without removing power-user tools.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15689:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15690:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15692:### Finding 3: Engram treats sessions, prompts, and summaries as first-class persisted objects
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15694:- **What it does**: Engram persists `sessions`, `observations`, and `user_prompts` as separate tables, exposes `mem_session_start`, `mem_session_end`, and `mem_session_summary`, then composes startup context by formatting recent sessions, prompts, and observations together. Public has strong resume/bootstrap orchestration, but it is centered on recovery composition rather than a lightweight explicit session-start/session-end API.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15695:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is already stronger at startup recovery and structural readiness, but Engram’s explicit lifecycle tools would add a simpler durable narrative boundary around a session. That is especially useful for compaction survival and for cleaner prompt/session/history analytics.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15696:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15697:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15699:### Finding 4: Engram’s memory hygiene revolves around stable topic threads plus duplicate suppression
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15701:- **What it does**: `AddObservation()` first upserts on `topic_key + project + scope`, incrementing `revision_count`; if no topic match exists, it checks a rolling duplicate window and increments `duplicate_count` instead of inserting a new row. Public’s save path is file-first and quality-gated through `generate-context.ts` and `memory_save`, but it does not expose a comparable first-class thread-key convention for evolving topics.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15702:- **Why it matters for us**: This is one of Engram’s strongest durable ideas. Public already preserves richer markdown memory artifacts, but long-running decisions and repeated refinements would benefit from an optional stable thread key so related saves revise one conceptual thread instead of fragmenting across many files.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15703:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15704:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15706:### Finding 5: Engram’s search is intentionally simple: direct topic-key shortcut first, sanitized FTS5 second
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15708:- **What it does**: If the query contains `/`, Engram tries direct `topic_key` lookup first and assigns those hits a dominant rank, then falls back to quoted FTS5 `MATCH` ordered by `fts.rank`. Public’s `memory_search` is much broader: vector + lexical + rerank + intent routing + session/causal boosts, while `memory_context` adds orchestration and graph-aware routing.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15709:- **Why it matters for us**: Engram’s lexical path is not better than Public’s hybrid stack overall, but its exact-thread shortcut is useful. The idea to copy is the deterministic “if this looks like a thread key, bypass normal ranking,” not the FTS-only architecture.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15710:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15711:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15713:### Finding 6: Agent-agnostic adoption is driven by setup/install code and hook-level protocol injection, not by core-store complexity
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15715:- **What it does**: `setup.go` writes/install-configures agent-specific MCP integrations for OpenCode, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Codex. The OpenCode adapter is explicitly “thin,” and the Claude session-start hook starts the server, creates a session, imports sync state, fetches context, and injects protocol text on startup.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15716:- **Why it matters for us**: The strong pattern here is distribution discipline: keep setup friction low, keep hooks small, and keep behavioral protocol near the runtime seam. Public already supports more advanced runtime recovery, but Engram’s packaging is cleaner and easier to reason about.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15717:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15718:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15753:Deep-dive the implementation semantics inside Engram’s `AddObservation()`, `SuggestTopicKey()`, `ExtractLearnings()`, and the MCP handlers for `mem_session_summary` and `mem_capture_passive`, then map those directly against Public’s `memory_save`, trigger/cognitive recall, and session-resume/bootstrap paths to separate `adopt now` from `prototype later` more decisively.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:1:### Finding 6: Agent-agnostic adoption is driven by setup/install code and hook-level protocol injection, not by core-store complexity
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:3:- **What it does**: `setup.go` writes/install-configures agent-specific MCP integrations for OpenCode, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Codex. The OpenCode adapter is explicitly “thin,” and the Claude session-start hook starts the server, creates a session, imports sync state, fetches context, and injects protocol text on startup.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:4:- **Why it matters for us**: The strong pattern here is distribution discipline: keep setup friction low, keep hooks small, and keep behavioral protocol near the runtime seam. Public already supports more advanced runtime recovery, but Engram’s packaging is cleaner and easier to reason about.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:5:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:6:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:41:Deep-dive the implementation semantics inside Engram’s `AddObservation()`, `SuggestTopicKey()`, `ExtractLearnings()`, and the MCP handlers for `mem_session_summary` and `mem_capture_passive`, then map those directly against Public’s `memory_save`, trigger/cognitive recall, and session-resume/bootstrap paths to separate `adopt now` from `prototype later` more decisively.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:52:Research Engram's persistent memory architecture, MCP tool design, and session lifecycle patterns to identify practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially for Spec Kit Memory. Focus on how Engram separates agent tools from admin tools, persists session history, stabilizes evolving topics with `topic_key`, and ranks or shortcuts search using SQLite + FTS5. Compare Engram's choices against current Public capabilities and classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`. Prioritize improvements that strengthen session continuity, memory hygiene, search usefulness, and agent-agnostic integration without duplicating systems Public already has.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:75:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with semantic retrieval via `memory_search`, session continuity via `memory_context`, trigger-based recall via `memory_match_triggers`, causal links, health/status tooling, and context save flows through `generate-context.js`. It also has CocoIndex for semantic code search and code-graph tooling for structural queries. What it does **not** currently have is a first-class session lifecycle API comparable to `mem_session_start` / `mem_session_end`, nor a lightweight MCP tool-profile split equivalent to Engram's `ProfileAgent` versus `ProfileAdmin`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:98:14. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and make sure the report explicitly distinguishes `adopt now`, `prototype later`, and `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:144:- What it does: `ProfileAgent` keeps proactive save/search/context/session tools available to agents, while `ProfileAdmin` isolates deletion, stats, timeline, and project-merge operations for curation workflows.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:145:- Why it matters: Public currently exposes many memory capabilities without a comparable profile layer, so a narrower agent-safe surface could reduce prompt noise and misuse risk.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:146:- Recommended action: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:156:- What it does: `AddObservation()` upserts by `topic_key` within project+scope, increments `revision_count`, and pairs that with `mem_session_summary` / `mem_session_end` so evolving work stays threadable across sessions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:157:- Why it matters: Public has strong retrieval, but it does not yet expose a similarly lightweight thread-key plus explicit lifecycle model for durable session narratives.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:158:- Recommended action: adopt now or prototype later, depending on implementation scope

Management**: sqlite-vec schema v15 (current) with document-type fields, event-based decay and phase-aware columns\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:58:|---> Vector (sqlite-vec)       -> Semantic matches\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:93:| Vector | `vector-index.ts` | Semantic similarity via sqlite-vec through the split vector-index modules |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:95:| FTS5 | `sqlite-fts.ts` | SQLite FTS5 BM25 weighted scoring |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:107:4. Graph signals (N2a + N2b) — momentum + causal depth\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:111:8. Feedback signals — learned trigger boosts (0.7x weight) + negative demotions\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:141:- **MMR Diversity**: Maximal Marginal Relevance reranking reduces near-duplicate results\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:143:- **Co-activation Boost**: Graph-neighbor score propagation via 2-hop causal traversal\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:175:Packet `026-graph-and-context-optimization/010-fts-capability-cascade-floor` freezes the lexical capability contract that packet `002-implement-cache-warning-hooks` now consumes. `memory_search` responses expose:\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:177:- `lexicalPath`: the lane that actually ran for lexical retrieval. Current packet-owned values are `fts5` and `unavailable`. The wider response schema also allows `like`, but packet `010` does not claim that lane for this runtime seam.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:184:| `ok` | FTS5 compile probe passed, `memory_fts` exists, and BM25 ranking executed normally | `fts5` |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:187:| `no_such_module_fts5` | The SQLite engine rejects FTS5 usage with `no such module: fts5` | `unavailable` |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:297:| `causal-boost.ts`          | -      | TypeScript | Causal-neighbor score boosting for graph traversal  |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:298:| `session-boost.ts`         | -      | TypeScript | Session-attention score boosting                    |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:311:| `sqlite-fts.ts`            | -      | TypeScript | SQLite FTS5 BM25 weighted scoring, extracted from hybrid-search for independent use |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:319:| `learned-feedback.ts`      | -      | TypeScript | Selection-based relevance feedback with learned triggers (R11) |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:347:| `session-state.ts`         | -      | TypeScript | Tracks per-session search state and retrieval context |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:348:| `session-transition.ts`    | -      | TypeScript | Handles session-state transition rules during retrieval flows |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:383:   MMR diversity reranking -> Reduce near-duplicate results\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:427:| `SPECKIT_GRAPH_SIGNALS`   | `true`  | Enable N2a momentum + N2b causal depth scoring |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:451:| v3      | Add `related_memories` column to `memory_index`                              |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:456:| v8      | `causal_edges` table with 6 relationship types (REQ-012, T043-T047)          |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:475:// Float32Array -> Buffer conversion for sqlite-vec\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:612:**Purpose**: Create causal edges between memories that share extracted entities across spec folders. Gated via `SPECKIT_ENTITY_LINKING`. Requires R10 (`SPECKIT_AUTO_ENTITIES`) to also be enabled.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:620:| **Edge Creation** | Creates `supports` causal edges between memories sharing entities |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:631:| `createEntityLinks` | `(db, matches) => EntityLinkResult` | Create causal edges for cross-doc matches |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:646:| `caused` | 1.0 | Strongest causal signal |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:650:| `supersedes` | 0.6 | Version supersession |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:656:Top-ranked results seed a 2-hop BFS traversal over causal edges. Discovered neighbors receive a score boost with `SEED_FRACTION=0.25` strength. A `1/sqrt(neighbor_count)` fan-effect divisor (R17) prevents hub nodes from appearing in every co-activation result.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:662:The causal graph is condensed into strongly connected components, then longest-path depth is computed across the resulting DAG. Shortcut edges do not collapse deeper chains; cycle members share one bounded depth layer, and the final depth is normalized by the deepest reachable component chain (`maxDepth`). Score bonus: `normalizedDepth * 0.05` — rewards structurally deep nodes in the causal chain.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:673:Processing steps applied during `memory_save` before a memory is persisted.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:687:| 2. Content Quality | 5-dimension weighted signal density | Dimensions: title (0.25), triggers (0.20), length (0.20), anchors (0.15), metadata (0.20) |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:688:| 3. Semantic Dedup | Cosine similarity against existing memories | Threshold: 0.92 rejects near-duplicates |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:698:| 0.75 - 0.88 | **Conflict** | Supersede: deprecate existing, create `supersedes` causal edge |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:719:`buildBm25DocumentText()` builds the canonical lexical document from title, content, trigger phrases, and folder metadata. `normalizeLexicalQueryTokens()` is shared by BM25 and SQLite FTS flows so lexical matching stays aligned across search channels.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:722:Watcher- and ingest-triggered reindex paths now use the normal synchronous embedding cache-miss flow. Deferred embeddings remain opt-in via `asyncEmbedding` or failure fallback.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:725:`memory_index_scan` now runs the broader post-mutation invalidation hook behavior whenever a scan indexes, updates, or stale-deletes rows.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:737:Typed-degree ranking is cached per database connection and invalidated through `clearDegreeCache()` / `clearDegreeCacheForDb()` after causal-edge and mutation flows, so the degree channel stays in sync with recent graph writes.\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:757:Selection tracking writes to a separate `learned_triggers` column (NOT FTS5 index). 10 safeguards: separate column, 30-day TTL, 100+ stop words denylist, rate cap (3 terms/selection, 8 terms/memory), top-3 exclusion, 1-week shadow period, <72h memory exclusion, sprint gate review, rollback mechanism, provenance audit log. Query weight: **0.7x** of organic triggers. Gated via `SPECKIT_LEARN_FROM_SELECTION` (default ON; set to `false` to disable).\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:772:import Database from 'better-sqlite3';\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:914:| `better-sqlite3` | SQLite database driver  |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:915:| `sqlite-vec`     | Vector search extension |\\n.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:921:|
