/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:36:### Finding 5: Identity is path-derived, not stable, so renames are semantic changes
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:38:- **What it does**: Search hits and facts are identified by relative path; `MemFact.ID` is the relative markdown path, dedup uses `Path`, and `memory_reinforce` takes a path. New facts are slugged from `subject-predicate` and collision-suffixed. There is no Modus-side equivalent of Public’s `memory_lineage` + `active_memory_projection` stable logical identity model.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:39:- **Why it matters for us**: This is fine for a small personal vault, but path-based identity makes renames and moves double as identity changes, which weakens continuity for reinforcement history, links, and long-lived references. Public’s lineage tables are substantially stronger for evolving memories.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:40:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:41:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:43:### Finding 6: Public already has the richer persisted storage graph; Modus is a portability reference, not a schema upgrade
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:45:- **What it does**: Public persists a unified `memory_index` row with provenance, governance scope, FSRS fields, chunking, quality, and `content_text`, then derives `memory_fts`, `vec_memories`, `causal_edges`, `memory_summaries`, `memory_entities`, `entity_catalog`, `memory_lineage`, and `active_memory_projection`. DB consumers can rebind when the backing SQLite handle changes, and FSRS strengthening on retrieval is opt-in via `trackAccess`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:46:- **Why it matters for us**: The storage-layer gap is architectural, not incremental. Modus’s value is in transparent file-native ergonomics and understandable portability, but its underlying persistence model is much weaker than what Public already has.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:47:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:48:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:7:### Finding 1: Modus boots a vault process, not a resumable session lifecycle
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9:- **What it does**: Modus resolves one vault root from flag/env/default, creates core directories, builds one in-memory index, registers 11 tools, and starts a stdio MCP loop. Its MCP initialize path only announces the tool surface, and its only built-in lifecycle/status tool is `vault_status`, which returns vault counts rather than any session/task recovery contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:10:- **Why it matters for us**: Modus is process-and-vault scoped; Public is session-and-task scoped. Modus has no real analogue for `memory_context`, `session_resume`, or `session_bootstrap`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:11:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:12:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:14:### Finding 2: Modus’s only session field is decorative, not an enforced partition
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:16:- **What it does**: Indexed facts may carry `session_tag`, defaulting to `session-1` when absent, but the fact store indexes only subject/predicate/value and scores only term overlap, confidence, and recency. `memory_store` does not write `session_tag` at all.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:17:- **Why it matters for us**: There is no true session boundary in retrieval or storage. Public already does far more by threading `sessionId` through retrieval, dedup, and continuity logic.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:18:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:19:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:21:### Finding 3: Public’s trusted session lifecycle is a real control plane; Modus has no equivalent
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:23:- **What it does**: Public resolves only server-managed session IDs, rejects mismatched callers, persists `session_state` with `spec_folder` and `current_task`, tracks inferred mode, and can inject attended working-memory items into resumed prompt context. Modus exposes no session token, no identity binding, and no resumed prompt-context surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:24:- **Why it matters for us**: Public’s continuity model is explicit and guarded; Modus’s continuity is implicit in whatever markdown already exists in the vault.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:25:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:26:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:28:### Finding 4: Public’s resume/bootstrap path is compositional and fail-closed; Modus only offers raw vault health
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:30:- **What it does**: `session_resume` composes `memory_context(resume)`, code-graph status, CocoIndex availability, structural context, and cached continuity only when summary text, transcript identity, freshness, and scope all validate. `session_bootstrap` adds health, hints, and next actions. Modus has no comparable resume packet or cached-summary validator; `vault_status` is just counts.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:31:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is already materially ahead on recovery lifecycle. Modus does not contribute a reusable bootstrap pattern here.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:32:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:33:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:35:### Finding 5: Modus’s write lifecycle is immediate markdown mutation, but it is not same-session freshness-safe
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:37:- **What it does**: In Modus, `vault_write` and `memory_store` write markdown directly, but the searchable index is built at startup and queried in memory with no post-write refresh in the write path. Public’s `generate-context` flow is structured-input, path-validated, atomically written, post-reviewed, and immediately indexed.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:38:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a lifecycle correctness gap, not just a style difference. Modus is simpler, but same-process writes can lag behind search until rebuild/restart. Public’s heavier save pipeline is justified for authoritative memory artifacts.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:39:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:40:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:42:### Finding 6: Modus treats recall as an implicit global write; Public’s two-timescale lifecycle is safer
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:44:- **What it does**: Modus `memory_search` asynchronously reinforces every returned fact, mutating confidence, stability, difficulty, `last_accessed`, and `access_count` on read. Public intentionally separates ephemeral session attention from long-term memory decay: resume retrieval disables long-term decay, while working-memory attention drives bounded prompt-context injection.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:45:- **Why it matters for us**: The transferable idea is that recall events matter, but Modus implements that as implicit global mutation. For Public, any equivalent should be explicit and scoped.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:46:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:47:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8:### Finding 1: Modus exposes a very thin MCP transport: stdio JSON-RPC with only `initialize`, `tools/list`, and `tools/call`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:10:- **What it does**: `Server.Run()` reads newline-delimited JSON from stdin and `handle()` only recognizes `initialize`, `notifications/initialized`, `tools/list`, and `tools/call`. The binary starts this server directly from `main.go` after building the in-memory index and registering tools. There is no inbound HTTP listener, no resource/prompt surface, no session bootstrap tool, and no orchestration layer comparable to Public’s `memory_context`, `session_bootstrap`, `code_graph_query`, or `code_graph_context`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:11:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a portability win for a single-purpose local binary, but it is a surface-area downgrade relative to Public’s retrieval/orchestration server. It is useful as a minimal transport reference, not as a model for Public’s primary MCP surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:13:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:15:### Finding 2: Modus has hidden profile separation in code, but the shipped binary hard-wires a single 11-tool profile
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:17:- **What it does**: `RegisterVaultTools()` defines a much larger tool family (`atlas_*`, `qm_*`, `distill_status`, plus memory/vault tools). `RegisterMemoryTools()` first calls `RegisterVaultTools()` and then destructively deletes every tool not in its 11-tool allowlist. `RegisterLearningsTools()` defines a separate `modus_learnings_*` family, but `main.go` never wires it. Separately, `docs/librarian.md` recommends a 6-tool “librarian” subset in agent config, but that subset is documentation-only, not a negotiated runtime profile.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:18:- **Why it matters for us**: The codebase clearly wants multiple role-specific surfaces, but the implementation is manual, non-discoverable, and not caller-selectable. Public could benefit from explicit tool-profile projections, but Modus’s current approach is too brittle to copy directly.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:19:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:20:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:22:### Finding 3: The only real API endpoints are outbound sidecar calls to the local Librarian model, not Modus-owned service endpoints
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:24:- **What it does**: Modus hard-codes `http://127.0.0.1:8090` as the Librarian endpoint, probes `/health` with `/v1/models` fallback, and posts chat requests to `/v1/chat/completions`. `ExpandQuery()`, `RankResults()`, `ExtractFacts()`, `ClassifyIntent()`, and `ProduceBriefing()` all run through this local OpenAI-compatible sidecar, but none are exposed as MCP tools themselves. MCP callers only see higher-level tools whose behavior changes if `librarian.Available()` returns true.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:25:- **Why it matters for us**: The useful pattern is “optional local lexical-expansion/rerank sidecar,” not the hard-coded localhost contract. Public could prototype an internal expander/reranker channel, but should keep it capability-checked, configurable, and traceable rather than ambient and endpoint-bound.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:26:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:27:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:29:### Finding 4: Tool registration and argument validation are loose enough that adopting Modus’s MCP schema pattern would weaken Public
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:31:- **What it does**: Modus stores `InputSchema` as generic `interface{}` and passes raw `map[string]interface{}` into handlers. Validation is decentralized and uneven: many handlers use loose type assertions and defaults, while filesystem safety is enforced mostly by `vault.safePath()` on read/write/list operations. Public, by contrast, has centralized schema definitions, strict Zod validation, explicit `additionalProperties: false`, safe path validators, and governance/session fields baked into tool schemas.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:32:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a material safety and operability gap. Public’s current tool layer is already stronger on input validation, scope governance, and explicit contracts; replacing it with Modus’s pattern would be regression, not simplification.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:33:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:34:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:36:### Finding 5: Modus search tools hide side effects and capability changes behind ambient conditions rather than explicit flags
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:38:- **What it does**: `vault_search` silently switches to librarian-expanded multi-query retrieval when the sidecar is reachable, otherwise falls back to direct search. `memory_search` silently spawns async `ReinforceFact()` calls for every returned fact. There are no per-call controls for disabling reinforcement, forcing plain lexical search, asking for provenance, selecting a presentation profile, or scoping session behavior. Public exposes those controls explicitly through `trackAccess`, `includeTrace`, `profile`, `sessionId`, intent routing, and governed scope fields.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:39:- **Why it matters for us**: Search-as-read with hidden write side effects is risky for operators and harder to reason about in evaluations. Public’s explicit opt-in `trackAccess` model is materially safer and easier to benchmark than Modus’s always-on recall reinforcement.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:40:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:41:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:43:### Finding 6: Modus’s CLI is a real second operator surface, separate from MCP, and that split is one of the cleaner parts of the design
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:45:- **What it does**: The binary has a tiny command surface: `version`, `health`, `doctor`, and `import khoj`, with `--vault` or `MODUS_VAULT_DIR` selecting the vault. `doctor` performs structural diagnostics over markdown files and `import khoj` handles migration into vault folders. This is separate from the MCP tool plane and clearly aimed at human operators or one-shot maintenance tasks.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:46:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has rich MCP health/status/index tools, but a thin human-facing CLI wrapper for common operator flows—health, validate, reindex, diagnose—could improve ergonomics for local maintenance and CI without changing core retrieval architecture.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:47:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:48:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:8:### Finding 1: **Modus’s “degraded mode” is not actually survivable**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:10:- **What it does**: Startup logs `index build failed` and claims it can continue, but `main.go` still calls `idx.DocCount()` even when `index.Build()` returned `nil, err`. The MCP layer also assumes an index exists: `memory_search` calls `v.Index.SearchFacts(...)` directly, while only the lower-level `Vault.SearchFacts()` method contains a no-index fallback. `vault_connected` also just returns `"Index not loaded."` rather than recovering.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a brittle failure contract: one bad scan can turn “start degraded” into a startup panic or nil-deref path. Public is already stricter here: DB refresh/rebind is mutexed, refuses empty-db drift, and returns explicit degraded/error responses instead of pretending recovery succeeded.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:12:- **Recommendation**: **reject**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:13:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:15:### Finding 2: **Parser corruption is silently normalized instead of surfaced**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:17:- **What it does**: If YAML frontmatter fails to parse, `Parse()` silently treats the frontmatter block as body text and returns success. `ScanDir()` also suppresses filesystem walk errors and parse failures (`return nil // skip errors`, `return nil // skip unparseable files`). Then `doctor` operates on that already-sanitized view, so malformed frontmatter is reduced to softer symptoms like “documents without frontmatter” or missing fact fields rather than a hard corruption report.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:18:- **Why it matters for us**: Modus optimizes for “keep indexing something,” but the cost is silent data quality loss and weaker operator trust. Public’s `memory_save` path is materially safer: preflight, dry-run, validation, and atomic save all give us a better place to reject malformed state before it becomes indexed ambiguity.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:19:- **Recommendation**: **reject**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:20:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:22:### Finding 3: **The plain-file write and migration path is non-atomic, partial, and lossy**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:24:- **What it does**: Core writes go straight to the final path with `os.WriteFile`; Khoj import uses `os.Create` directly, continues after per-file failures, skips already-existing files, truncates imported context over 2000 chars, deduplicates context by the first 200 chars, and falls back to `time.Now()` when timestamps cannot be parsed. There is no temp-file promotion, rollback, checkpoint, manifest, or resume journal.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:25:- **Why it matters for us**: This is fine for a lightweight local tool, but it is the wrong mutation model for governed or high-value memory. Public already has the stronger pattern: checkpointed bulk operations, rollback-aware atomic saves, and post-insert cleanup when metadata application fails. If we ever add importer workflows or operator batch jobs, they should follow Public’s mutation model, not Modus’s write-through importer.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:26:- **Recommendation**: **reject**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:27:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:29:### Finding 4: **A timestamp edge case can disable the intended memory lifecycle**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:31:- **What it does**: `StoreFact()` writes `created: "now"` as a literal string. But recency boosting, tier labeling, staleness warnings, and decay all expect parseable dates or RFC3339 timestamps. That means newly stored facts can miss age-aware behavior until some later successful recall writes `last_accessed`. Separately, Khoj import converts bad timestamps to the current wall clock, which silently rewrites chronology.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:32:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a good example of how lightweight markdown persistence can quietly undermine lifecycle logic if timestamps are not normalized at write time. Public should keep lifecycle timestamps machine-parseable and validation-gated at ingest; our current preflight/save architecture already gives us the right enforcement point.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:33:- **Recommendation**: **reject**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:34:- **Impact**: **medium**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:36:### Finding 5: **Scaling is “rebuild everything in memory,” with a few latent concurrency footguns**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:38:- **What it does**: Every build/open rescans markdown, reparses documents, rebuilds BM25, cross-refs, and the fact store entirely in RAM. Prefix fallback linearly scans the full term map. `memory_search` launches a goroutine per returned fact for asynchronous reinforcement writes. The cache uses `RWMutex`, but `get()` mutates `hits` while holding only `RLock`, which is unsafe if request handling ever becomes concurrent. The current stdio loop is serial, so that race is partly masked today.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:39:- **Why it matters for us**: Modus’s architecture is intentionally simple, but it does not transfer well to Public’s larger, longer-lived memory surface. Public’s incremental DB-backed indexing, guarded rebind path, and post-restore rebuild hooks are the right direction; the Modus lesson is mostly where the scaling cliffs are, not a backend we should copy.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:40:- **Recommendation**: **reject**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:41:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:43:### Finding 6: **The strongest transferable pattern here is the operator-facing “doctor,” not Modus’s repair depth**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:45:- **What it does**: Modus gives operators a very readable `health`/`doctor` surface: counts, contradictions, duplicates, empty docs, directory distribution. But it is mostly advisory; it does not pair diagnosis with durable rollback or strong repair flows. Public already has deeper repair machinery—auto-repair for FTS drift, orphan-edge cleanup, restore barriers, checkpoint restore index rebuilds—but the surface is more technical and less “single-screen triage.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:46:- **Why it matters for us**: The net-new idea is not Modus’s recovery engine; it is the usability of a compact operator diagnostic surface. Public could benefit from a friendlier “doctor summary” layered over existing `memory_health`, checkpoints, and mutation ledgers.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:47:- **Recommendation**: **NEW FEATURE**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:48:- **Impact**: **medium**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:9:Research Modus Memory's FSRS spaced repetition, BM25 search ranking, and cross-referencing patterns to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`'s memory search and retrieval, especially around memory decay and reinforcement, query expansion, knowledge linking, and plain-markdown memory storage patterns. Determine which ideas should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:51:14. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every meaningful finding must cite exact file paths, explain what Modus does, why it matters here, whether to `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, what Public subsystem it affects, and what migration, compatibility, or validation risk comes with it.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:96:`internal/vault/facts.go` reinforces memories automatically when `memory_search` returns them, increasing stability, slightly lowering difficulty, updating `last_accessed`, and incrementing `access_count`. A strong finding would explain how that recall loop differs from Spec Kit Memory's current FSRS-aware decay layer, identify where automatic reinforcement could improve real-world memory freshness in `Code_Environment/Public`, and classify the idea as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:140:- clear classification of each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:150:- recommendation: `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:176:- each finding is tagged `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:191:### Finding N: [Title]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:193:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:194:- **Why it matters for us**: concrete relevance
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:195:- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:196:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:1965:   180		// OOD detection — reject garbage results
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:3663:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/internal/mcp/vault.go:737:	srv.AddTool("atlas_reject_pr", "Reject an evolution PR. Weakens linked beliefs. Operator only.", map[string]interface{}{
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5225:    31	// safePath resolves relPath within the vault and rejects traversal attempts.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6560:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/orchestrator-error-cascade.vitest.ts:174:    await expect(executePipeline(config)).rejects.toThrow('embedding service unavailable');
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7916:### Finding 1: Modus is a single-binary, vault-first MCP server with a deliberately narrow runtime shape
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7918:- **What it does**: Startup resolves a vault path, creates `memory/facts`, `brain`, and `atlas`, builds one in-memory index, wraps it in `vault.Vault`, then exposes a minimal stdio JSON-RPC MCP server with 11 retained memory tools.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7919:- **Why it matters for us**: This is much simpler than our current retrieval runtime and is a useful reference for a lightweight local-first deployment mode, but it is not comparable to our full retrieval stack feature-for-feature.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7920:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7921:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7923:### Finding 2: The main search architecture is “scan markdown -> build in-memory services -> serve from RAM”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7925:- **What it does**: `Build()` scans all `.md` files, materializes `document` structs, builds a BM25 inverted index, a 256-entry exact-plus-Jaccard query cache, a cross-reference index, and an in-memory fact store; `Search()` then runs cache -> BM25 -> OOD filter -> snippet extraction -> normalized ranking -> cache write.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7926:- **Why it matters for us**: The architecture is operationally cheap and easy to reason about, but it trades away persistent index structures and richer multi-stage fusion for startup-time rebuild simplicity.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7927:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7928:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7930:### Finding 3: Modus splits “all documents” from “memory facts” and gives facts their own recall-and-decay loop
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7932:- **What it does**: Persisted markdown fact files are decayed and reinforced through `vault/facts.go`, while `index/facts.go` keeps a separate in-memory searchable fact slice with recency and confidence boosts; `memory_search` auto-reinforces each returned fact asynchronously, unlike our Stage 2 FSRS testing effect which only writes back when `trackAccess=true`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7933:- **Why it matters for us**: Modus has a cleaner “returned result == recall event” model for fact memories, while our current system is more conservative and avoids write-on-read side effects by default.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7934:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7935:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7937:### Finding 4: Cross-referencing is intentionally shallow adjacency, not a real graph engine
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7939:- **What it does**: Modus builds three adjacency maps (`bySubject`, `byTag`, `byEntity`), scores connected docs with fixed weights (subject `3.0`, entity `2.0`, tag `1.0`), and appends connected-doc hints to search responses. The “second pass” only checks title and subject for entity mentions even though the comments imply broader body linkage.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7940:- **Why it matters for us**: This is weaker than our causal/code-graph style signal layers, but it is much easier to expose to users as understandable “connected docs” rather than invisible score boosts.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7941:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7942:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7944:### Finding 5: The markdown layer is very portable, but intentionally loose and only lightly normalized
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7946:- **What it does**: Documents are plain markdown with YAML frontmatter; parse failures fall back to raw body, `ScanDir()` silently skips unreadable/unparseable files and `discard/`, wiki links are extracted from body text, and writes are just frontmatter-plus-body file rewrites after path traversal checks.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7947:- **Why it matters for us**: This is excellent for human editability and git portability, but it also means weaker schema enforcement, silent parse skips, and nondeterministic frontmatter ordering from Go map iteration.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7948:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7949:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7951:### Finding 6: The Librarian is not just documentation rhetoric; it is a real local model dependency in the search architecture
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7953:- **What it does**: When available, the Librarian is contacted over HTTP at `127.0.0.1:8090` and is used for query expansion, result ranking, summarization, fact extraction, intent classification, and briefing production.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7954:- **Why it matters for us**: Modus’s architecture is not purely “BM25 only” in practice once the Librarian is up, so any adoption decision has to treat LLM-mediated lexical expansion as an optional subsystem with availability and correctness risks.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7955:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7956:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7958:### Finding 7: Public already has the broader retrieval architecture; Modus is a simplification reference, not a replacement candidate
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7960:- **What it does**: Public already runs a 4-stage hybrid pipeline with vector + BM25/FTS5 + graph channels, session and causal boosts, reranking, fallback-state reporting, and opt-in FSRS write-back. Modus is simpler, but the README also overstates some properties: `go.mod` is not zero-dependency, and the search path can invoke a local model when Librarian is available.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7961:- **Why it matters for us**: The real comparison is selective borrowing, not replacement. The highest-value overlaps are adjacency hints and a cleaner recall-event policy; the rest would mostly duplicate or downgrade existing Public capabilities.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7962:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7963:- **Impact**: high

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:8048:- **What it does**: When available, the Librarian is contacted over HTTP at `127.0.0.1:8090` and is used for query expansion, result ranking, summarization, fact extraction, intent classification, and briefing production.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:8049:- **Why it matters for us**: Modus’s architecture is not purely “BM25 only” in practice once the Librarian is up, so any adoption decision has to treat LLM-mediated lexical expansion as an optional subsystem with availability and correctness risks.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:8055:- **What it does**: Public already runs a 4-stage hybrid pipeline with vector + BM25/FTS5 + graph channels, session and causal boosts, reranking, fallback-state reporting, and opt-in FSRS write-back.  succeeded in 0ms:

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10:- **What it does**: Our stack mints server-managed session IDs, rejects untrusted caller IDs, binds sessions to tenant/user/agent scope, persists `session_state`, keeps ephemeral retrieval-state for seen results/open questions/anchors, and injects working-memory prompt context during resume. Mex’s code has config, drift, scanner, sync, and scaffold types, but no comparable runtime session identity, scope validation, or cross-turn retrieval state.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:15:### Finding 5: `generate-context.js` is a real session-capture pipeline; Mex’s GROW model is authoring discipline, not structured capture
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:17:- **What it does**: Our save path accepts structured JSON/stdin, treats explicit CLI spec-folder targets as authoritative, and materializes durable artifacts containing summary, outcomes, blockers, next action, canonical-doc awareness, session ID, git provenance, and context typing. Determine which Mex patterns should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:86:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit validation via `validate.sh` with severity-oriented checks, indexed memory files with frontmatter, and a `MEMORY.md`-style documentation/memory model. Research should stay centered on that gap rather than re-covering general memory persistence already addressed by phase `001`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:110:15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every finding must cite exact file paths, describe what Mex actually does, why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, whether it should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, what subsystem it would affect, and what migration or validation risk comes with it. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:152:`src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts` does not rely on human judgment alone; it flags scaffold files by both elapsed days and commits since last change.
