/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6412:### Finding 1: Mex has a cleaner single hygiene surface, but our hygiene logic is richer and fragmented
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6414:- **What it does**: Mex runs all scaffold hygiene through one deterministic pass: collect claims, run eight checker families, then compute one score from severity costs. Our system spreads hygiene across multiple entry points: `memory_health` for DB/FTS/vector/alias integrity, `memory_causal_stats` for graph coverage/orphans, `post-save-review` for saved-metadata drift, and `validate-memory-quality` for rendered-memory quality.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6415:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex is weaker semantically, but much easier to audit operationally. Today we have better hygiene rules, but no single “memory hygiene report” that tells an operator where the system is drifting overall.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6416:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6417:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6419:### Finding 2: Mex does not have a real trigger system; our trigger stack is an actual scoped retrieval subsystem
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6421:- **What it does**: In Mex, `triggers:` only appears in the pattern template and the router contract; there is no runtime loader, no trigger matcher, no trigger health report, and no scope-aware recall path. Our stack loads persisted `trigger_phrases`, normalizes them, drops non-specific phrases, builds a candidate index, exposes degraded-state stats, rejects untrusted session IDs, fail-closes on scope filtering, and can layer cognitive decay and signal boosts on top.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6422:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex contributes almost nothing to trigger hygiene beyond authoring intent. Replacing our trigger path with Mex-style markdown triggers would be a major retrieval regression.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6423:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6424:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6426:### Finding 3: Our trigger hygiene is already stricter than Mex’s scaffold hygiene, but it is mostly hidden in save-time review code
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6428:- **What it does**: We already lint trigger hygiene aggressively after save: path-fragment detection, missing authored phrases, frontmatter-versus-metadata drift, unsanitized junk, duplicate phrases, empty trigger sets on dense saves, and error-dominated trigger leakage. Mex’s drift engine has no comparable checker for trigger quality or trigger/render parity.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6429:- **Why it matters for us**: The hygiene gap is not “we need Mex-style trigger rules”; it is “we should expose our existing trigger rules in a recurring operator-facing audit instead of only during save flows.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6430:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6431:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6433:### Finding 4: Our causal-link hygiene is a true graph lifecycle; Mex frontmatter edges are only dead-link validation
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6435:- **What it does**: Mex checks whether YAML `edges.target` files exist. Our system parses `causalLinks` from saved memory, inserts typed relations, caps auto-edge fanout and strength, auto-invalidates contradictory relations, tracks coverage/orphans, and wires orphan-edge cleanup into `memory_health`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6436:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex is useful as a reminder that cheap dead-reference checks matter, but it has no equivalent to our lineage graph, contradiction handling, or graph maintenance. The reusable idea is lightweight broken-reference auditing, not the causal model itself.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6437:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6438:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6440:### Finding 5: Mex surfaces staleness much better than we do, even though our graph already has latent stale-edge primitives
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6442:- **What it does**: Mex turns staleness into first-class hygiene: every scaffold file can error at 90 days or 200 commits, and warn at 30 days or 50 commits. Our stack already tracks stale causal edges via `last_accessed` and threshold constants, but `memory_health` does not surface stale memories, stale triggers, or stale causal edges as operator-visible hygiene signals.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6443:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest hygiene blind spot in our current stack. We are strong on correctness and repair, but weak on “this memory is still valid, yet probably old and should be reviewed.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6444:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6445:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6495:### Finding 1: Mex has a cleaner single hygiene surface, but our hygiene logic is richer and fragmented
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6497:- **What it does**: Mex runs all scaffold hygiene through one deterministic pass: collect claims, run eight checker families, then compute one score from severity costs. Our system spreads hygiene across multiple entry points: `memory_health` for DB/FTS/vector/alias integrity, `memory_causal_stats` for graph coverage/orphans, `post-save-review` for saved-metadata drift, and `validate-memory-quality` for rendered-memory quality.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6498:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex is weaker semantically, but much easier to audit operationally. Today we have better hygiene rules, but no single “memory hygiene report” that tells an operator where the system is drifting overall.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6499:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6500:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6502:### Finding 2: Mex does not have a real trigger system; our trigger stack is an actual scoped retrieval subsystem
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6504:- **What it does**: In Mex, `triggers:` only appears in the pattern template and the router contract; there is no runtime loader, no trigger matcher, no trigger health report, and no scope-aware recall path. Our stack loads persisted `trigger_phrases`, normalizes them, drops non-specific phrases, builds a candidate index, exposes degraded-state stats, rejects untrusted session IDs, fail-closes on scope filtering, and can layer cognitive decay and signal boosts on top.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6505:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex contributes almost nothing to trigger hygiene beyond authoring intent. Replacing our trigger path with Mex-style markdown triggers would be a major retrieval regression.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6506:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6507:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6509:### Finding 3: Our trigger hygiene is already stricter than Mex’s scaffold hygiene, but it is mostly hidden in save-time review code
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6511:- **What it does**: We already lint trigger hygiene aggressively after save: path-fragment detection, missing authored phrases, frontmatter-versus-metadata drift, unsanitized junk, duplicate phrases, empty trigger sets on dense saves, and error-dominated trigger leakage. Mex’s drift engine has no comparable checker for trigger quality or trigger/render parity.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6512:- **Why it matters for us**: The hygiene gap is not “we need Mex-style trigger rules”; it is “we should expose our existing trigger rules in a recurring operator-facing audit instead of only during save flows.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6513:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6514:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6516:### Finding 4: Our causal-link hygiene is a true graph lifecycle; Mex frontmatter edges are only dead-link validation
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6518:- **What it does**: Mex checks whether YAML `edges.target` files exist. Our system parses `causalLinks` from saved memory, inserts typed relations, caps auto-edge fanout and strength, auto-invalidates contradictory relations, tracks coverage/orphans, and wires orphan-edge cleanup into `memory_health`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6519:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex is useful as a reminder that cheap dead-reference checks matter, but it has no equivalent to our lineage graph, contradiction handling, or graph maintenance. The reusable idea is lightweight broken-reference auditing, not the causal model itself.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6520:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6521:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6523:### Finding 5: Mex surfaces staleness much better than we do, even though our graph already has latent stale-edge primitives
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6525:- **What it does**: Mex turns staleness into first-class hygiene: every scaffold file can error at 90 days or 200 commits, and warn at 30 days or 50 commits. Our stack already tracks stale causal edges via `last_accessed` and threshold constants, but `memory_health` does not surface stale memories, stale triggers, or stale causal edges as operator-visible hygiene signals.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6526:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest hygiene blind spot in our current stack. We are strong on correctness and repair, but weak on “this memory is still valid, yet probably old and should be reviewed.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6527:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:6528:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:31:Research Mex's markdown scaffold and drift detection patterns to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around staleness detection, codebase-knowledge synchronization, and structured project knowledge. Determine which Mex patterns should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:50:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit validation via `validate.sh` with severity-oriented checks, indexed memory files with frontmatter, and a `MEMORY.md`-style documentation/memory model. What it does **not** currently have is an automated drift checker that compares documentation or memory scaffolds against the actual filesystem, commands, dependencies, git staleness, or cross-file contradictions. Research should stay centered on that gap rather than re-covering general memory persistence already addressed by phase `001`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:74:15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every finding must cite exact file paths, describe what Mex actually does, why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, whether it should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, what subsystem it would affect, and what migration or validation risk comes with it. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:116:`src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts` does not rely on human judgment alone; it flags scaffold files by both elapsed days and commits since last change. A strong finding would explain why combining temporal and repository-activity signals is stronger than date-only freshness, show how that could improve Public's documentation hygiene, and classify the idea as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:161:- findings that classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:171:- recommendation: `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:211:### Finding N: [Title]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:213:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:214:- **Why it matters for us**: concrete relevance
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:215:- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:216:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:3286:    reason: 'Sparse trigger phrases are diagnostic, but not enough to reject an otherwise valid rendered memory.',
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:4963:    89	    reason: 'Sparse trigger phrases are diagnostic, but not enough to reject an otherwise valid rendered memory.',
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5288:### Finding 1: Mex preserves issue locality from drift detection into repair
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5290:- **What it does**: drift starts as typed `Claim` objects, becomes typed `DriftIssue` objects carrying `file`, `line`, and optional triggering `claim`, then `runSync()` groups those issues into `SyncTarget[]` by file before any AI step. The repair unit is not “the scaffold” or “the repo”; it is a concrete broken scaffold file with enumerated issue codes.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5291:- **Why it matters for us**: this is the real difference between Mex and our current surfaces. Spec Kit has retrieval and health tools, but no file-scoped documentation repair pipeline. A Spec Kit equivalent would let us repair drifted docs/spec guidance surgically instead of falling back to broad manual resync or fresh repo-wide analysis.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5292:- **Recommendation**: `NEW FEATURE`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5293:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5295:### Finding 2: The sync brief is bounded on purpose and only adds evidence needed to repair the flagged file
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5297:- **What it does**: `buildFileSection()` embeds only four things: the flagged file path, issue list, current file contents, and narrowly relevant repair context. For `MISSING_PATH`, it lists actual nearby directory contents and a shallow extension-based fuzzy search. For path-linked issues, it adds a recent `HEAD~5..HEAD` diff for the referenced paths. The prompt then explicitly says “Only change what’s necessary.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5298:- **Why it matters for us**: this is the mechanism that makes targeted sync cheap and stable. Our post-save review and memory-quality validation detect problems after writes, but they do not assemble a bounded repair packet with just-enough filesystem and git evidence. That missing middle layer is why our remediation is still more operator-driven than system-driven.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5299:- **Recommendation**: `adopt now`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5300:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5302:### Finding 3: Mex’s repair loop is severity-gated and iterative, which limits churn
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5304:- **What it does**: `runSync()` ignores warning-only files unless `--warnings` is passed, batches only error-bearing files into targets, runs one repair cycle, reruns drift detection, reports score delta, and asks whether to continue. The score itself is intentionally simple: `100 - 10/error - 3/warning - 1/info`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5305:- **Why it matters for us**: if we add a sync surface, this gating behavior is more reusable than the score itself. The important pattern is “repair only hard-fail surfaces first, verify immediately, then optionally widen scope.” That fits Spec Kit’s strict-validation workflow much better than a blanket rewrite mode.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5306:- **Recommendation**: `adopt now`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5307:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5309:### Finding 4: Claim extraction is the prerequisite for targeted sync, but the heuristic layer is shallow by design
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5311:- **What it does**: Mex extracts path claims from inline code that looks path-like, command claims from inline code and fenced blocks, and dependency/version claims from bold text inside dependency-like headings. It explicitly filters routes, HTTP methods, code-like snippets, quoted assignments, and template placeholders.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5312:- **Why it matters for us**: a Spec Kit sync loop would need the same “claim envelope” concept, but probably not Mex’s exact markdown heuristics. Our corpus has stronger structure than generic scaffold markdown: frontmatter, anchors, spec-doc sections, memory metadata blocks, checklist semantics, and command surfaces. The transferable idea is typed claim extraction, not Mex’s literal regex set.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5313:- **Recommendation**: `prototype later`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5314:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5316:### Finding 5: Spec Kit has adjacent diagnostics, but not a repair control plane
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5318:- **What it does**: `memory_context` routes retrieval; `session_bootstrap` merges resume plus health hints; `post-save-review` compares rendered memory markdown against original collected data after save; `validate-memory-quality` blocks or downgrades bad memory writes/indexing; `memory_health` reports diagnostics and guarded auto-repair metadata. These are real safeguards, but they are retrieval-time, save-time, or system-health-time safeguards.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5319:- **Why it matters for us**: this clarifies the exact gap. We are stronger than Mex on durable-memory validation and retrieval orchestration, but weaker on turning documentation/spec drift into a bounded remediation loop. Mex’s second pattern is not a better memory store; it is a missing repair layer between “validator found drift” and “human manually fixes docs.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5320:- **Recommendation**: `NEW FEATURE`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5321:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5323:### Finding 6: Mex’s bounded sync is useful, but its evidence model has clear limits we should not copy blindly
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5325:- **What it does**: Mex still sends all current targets into one Claude session, uses a shallow extension search for missing-path hints, and limits diff context to `HEAD~5..HEAD`. That keeps prompts small, but it can miss older renames, deeper moves, or semantically related repo changes outside the claimed paths.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5326:- **Why it matters for us**: if we build this in Spec Kit, the core loop is worth copying, but the evidence retrieval should probably use our stronger search surfaces: code graph for structural doc claims, CocoIndex for semantic recovery, and spec-local metadata rather than extension-only filesystem scans.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5327:- **Recommendation**: `prototype later`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5328:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5373:### Finding 1: Mex preserves issue locality from drift detection into repair
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5375:- **What it does**: drift starts as typed `Claim` objects, becomes typed `DriftIssue` objects carrying `file`, `line`, and optional triggering `claim`, then `runSync()` groups those issues into `SyncTarget[]` by file before any AI step. The repair unit is not “the scaffold” or “the repo”; it is a concrete broken scaffold file with enumerated issue codes.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5376:- **Why it matters for us**: this is the real difference between Mex and our current surfaces. Spec Kit has retrieval and health tools, but no file-scoped documentation repair pipeline. A Spec Kit equivalent would let us repair drifted docs/spec guidance surgically instead of falling back to broad manual resync or fresh repo-wide analysis.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5377:- **Recommendation**: `NEW FEATURE`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5378:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5380:### Finding 2: The sync brief is bounded on purpose and only adds evidence needed to repair the flagged file
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5382:- **What it does**: `buildFileSection()` embeds only four things: the flagged file path, issue list, current file contents, and narrowly relevant repair context. For `MISSING_PATH`, it lists actual nearby directory contents and a shallow extension-based fuzzy search. For path-linked issues, it adds a recent `HEAD~5..HEAD` diff for the referenced paths. The prompt then explicitly says “Only change what’s necessary.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5383:- **Why it matters for us**: this is the mechanism that makes targeted sync cheap and stable. Our post-save review and memory-quality validation detect problems after writes, but they do not assemble a bounded repair packet with just-enough filesystem and git evidence. That missing middle layer is why our remediation is still more operator-driven than system-driven.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5384:- **Recommendation**: `adopt now`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5385:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5387:### Finding 3: Mex’s repair loop is severity-gated and iterative, which limits churn
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5389:- **What it does**: `runSync()` ignores warning-only files unless `--warnings` is passed, batches only error-bearing files into targets, runs one repair cycle, reruns drift detection, reports score delta, and asks whether to continue. The score itself is intentionally simple: `100 - 10/error - 3/warning - 1/info`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5390:- **Why it matters for us**: if we add a sync surface, this gating behavior is more reusable than the score itself. The important pattern is “repair only hard-fail surfaces first, verify immediately, then optionally widen scope.” That fits Spec Kit’s strict-validation workflow much better than a blanket rewrite mode.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5391:- **Recommendation**: `adopt now`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5392:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5394:### Finding 4: Claim extraction is the prerequisite for targeted sync, but the heuristic layer is shallow by design
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5396:- **What it does**: Mex extracts path claims from inline code that looks path-like, command claims from inline code and fenced blocks, and dependency/version claims from bold text inside dependency-like headings. It explicitly filters routes, HTTP methods, code-like snippets, quoted assignments, and template placeholders.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5397:- **Why it matters for us**: a Spec Kit sync loop would need the same “claim envelope” concept, but probably not Mex’s exact markdown heuristics. Our corpus has stronger structure than generic scaffold markdown: frontmatter, anchors, spec-doc sections, memory metadata blocks, checklist semantics, and command surfaces. The transferable idea is typed claim extraction, not Mex’s literal regex set.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5398:- **Recommendation**: `prototype later`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5399:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5401:### Finding 5: Spec Kit has adjacent diagnostics, but not a repair control plane
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5403:- **What it does**: `memory_context` routes retrieval; `session_bootstrap` merges resume plus health hints; `post-save-review` compares rendered memory markdown against original collected data after save; `validate-memory-quality` blocks or downgrades bad memory writes/indexing; `memory_health` reports diagnostics and guarded auto-repair metadata. These are real safeguards, but they are retrieval-time, save-time, or system-health-time safeguards.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5404:- **Why it matters for us**: this clarifies the exact gap. We are stronger than Mex on durable-memory validation and retrieval orchestration, but weaker on turning documentation/spec drift into a bounded remediation loop. Mex’s second pattern is not a better memory store; it is a missing repair layer between “validator found drift” and “human manually fixes docs.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5405:- **Recommendation**: `NEW FEATURE`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5406:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5408:### Finding 6: Mex’s bounded sync is useful, but its evidence model has clear limits we should not copy blindly
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5410:- **What it does**: Mex still sends all current targets into one Claude session, uses a shallow extension search for missing-path hints, and limits diff context to `HEAD~5..HEAD`. That keeps prompts small, but it can miss older renames, deeper moves, or semantically related repo changes outside the claimed paths.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5411:- **Why it matters for us**: if we build this in Spec Kit, the core loop is worth copying, but the evidence retrieval should probably use our stronger search surfaces: code graph for structural doc claims, CocoIndex for semantic recovery, and spec-local metadata rather than extension-only filesystem scans.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5412:- **Recommendation**: `prototype later`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:5413:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:9:Research Mex's markdown scaffold and drift detection patterns to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around staleness detection, codebase-knowledge synchronization, and structured project knowledge. Determine which Mex patterns should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:28:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit validation via `validate.sh` with severity-oriented checks, indexed memory files with frontmatter, and a `MEMORY.md`-style documentation/memory model. What it does **not** currently have is an automated drift checker that compares documentation or memory scaffolds against the actual filesystem, commands, dependencies, git staleness, or cross-file contradictions. Research should stay centered on that gap rather than re-covering general memory persistence already addressed by phase `001`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:52:15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every finding must cite exact file paths, describe what Mex actually does, why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, whether it should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, what subsystem it would affect, and what migration or validation risk comes with it. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:94:`src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts` does not rely on human judgment alone; it flags scaffold files by both elapsed days and commits since last change. A strong finding would explain why combining temporal and repository-activity signals is stronger than date-only freshness, show how that could improve Public's documentation hygiene, and classify the idea as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:139:- findings that classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:149:- recommendation: `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`

If not provided and mode=auto, intent is auto-detected from input.' }, specFolder: { type: 'string', description: 'Limit context to specific spec folder' }, tenantId: { type: 'string', description: 'Tenant boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, userId: { type: 'string', description: 'User boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, agentId: { type: 'string', description: 'Agent boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, sharedSpaceId: { type: 'string', description: 'Shared-space boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, limit: { type: 'number', minimum: 1, maximum: 100, description: 'Maximum results (mode-specific defaults apply)' }, sessionId: { type: 'string', description: 'Optional server-issued session identifier for working-memory continuity. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5705:    98	| Command | What it does |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5911:### Finding 1: Mex uses a router-first markdown scaffold as its control plane
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5913:- **What it does**: Mex keeps the always-loaded file tiny, then routes the agent through `ROUTER.md` into `context/*.md` and `patterns/*.md`. The `GROW` step feeds completed work back into the scaffold by updating current state or adding new patterns.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5914:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a different layer than Spec Kit Memory. Our system is strong at retrieval and session continuity, but Mex adds a very explicit, human-readable navigation contract for project knowledge.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5915:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5916:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5918:### Finding 2: The CLI is a thin orchestrator over distinct setup, drift, scan, sync, pattern, and hook subsystems
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5920:- **What it does**: `cli.ts` wires `mex setup`, `check`, `init`, `sync`, `pattern add`, `watch`, and `commands` to focused modules. `findConfig()` enforces project-root execution, locates `.git`, and resolves either `.mex/` or root-layout scaffolds.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5921:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex’s architecture is easy to slice. We would not need to import the whole product to reuse the drift-checking or targeted-sync parts as a Public-side docs-drift command.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5922:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5923:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5925:### Finding 3: Mex front-loads static scanning so AI writes from a structured brief instead of raw repo exploration

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5928:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has strong retrieval at runtime, but not an equivalent “static brief first” path for initializing or resyncing markdown knowledge surfaces. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5930:- **Impact**: medium

This is fully static and zero-token.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5935:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the biggest capability gap versus `Code_Environment/Public`. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5937:- **Impact**: high

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5941:- **What it does**: Sync groups issues by file, injects the current file content, issue list, nearby filesystem reality, and recent git diffs into a repair brief, then loops until the score improves. `watch` can surface drift after commits.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5942:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a practical bridge between static detection and AI repair. Public could borrow the brief-building pattern without inheriting the Claude-specific interactive loop. That would fit our existing validator, memory, and code-graph stack better than a full Mex-style workflow.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5943:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5944:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5981:### Finding 1: Mex uses a router-first markdown scaffold as its control plane
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5983:- **What it does**: Mex keeps the always-loaded file tiny, then routes the agent through `ROUTER.md` into `context/*.md` and `patterns/*.md`. The `GROW` step feeds completed work back into the scaffold by updating current state or adding new patterns.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5984:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a different layer than Spec Kit Memory. Our system is strong at retrieval and session continuity, but Mex adds a very explicit, human-readable navigation contract for project knowledge.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5985:- **Recommendation**: prototype later

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:3:- **What it does**: Mex starts at `100` and subtracts `10/3/1` for error/warning/info, regardless of whether the issue is a hard trust break or mild hygiene noise.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:4:- **Why it matters for us**: Spec Kit already distinguishes blocked, degraded, exact, estimated, unknown, and rejected states. A single number would blur meaningful contract differences and could make a partially unsafe surface look “mostly fine.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:5:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:6:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:18:- Questions answered: the only clear `adopt now` item is static reference integrity built on typed claim extraction. Freshness is the next best candidate, but only with explicit degraded/unknown semantics. Repair briefing is worth building as a new Spec Kit feature. Command/dependency/script parity needs a separate inventory abstraction first. Auto-growth and a single drift score should stay out.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:29:5. Keep rejected: automatic pattern/context growth from task completion, and any single-number health score as the primary truth surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:40:Research Mex's markdown scaffold and drift detection patterns to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around staleness detection, codebase-knowledge synchronization, and structured project knowledge. Determine which Mex patterns should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:59:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit validation via `validate.sh` with severity-oriented checks, indexed memory files with frontmatter, and a `MEMORY.md`-style documentation/memory model. What it does **not** currently have is an automated drift checker that compares documentation or memory scaffolds against the actual filesystem, commands, dependencies, git staleness, or cross-file contradictions. Research should stay centered on that gap rather than re-covering general memory persistence already addressed by phase `001`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:83:15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every finding must cite exact file paths, describe what Mex actually does, why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, whether it should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, what subsystem it would affect, and what migration or validation risk comes with it. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:125:`src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts` does not rely on human judgment alone; it flags scaffold files by both elapsed days and commits since last change. A strong finding would explain why combining temporal and repository-activity signals is stronger than date-only freshness, show how that could improve Public's documentation hygiene, and classify the idea as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:6984:FINAL SYNTHESIS: Classify every major Mex mechanism as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, `NEW FEATURE`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:6989:### Finding 1: Static markdown reference integrity is the only clear adopt-now slice
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:6991:- **What it does**: Mex extracts typed markdown claims from inline code, code blocks, and dependency headings, then runs zero-token checks for missing paths, dead frontmatter edges, and `patterns/INDEX.md` drift. It already distinguishes real missing references from pattern/template placeholders by downgrading some path misses to warnings.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:6992:- **Why it matters for us**: This cleanly maps to our real gap. Spec Kit already has semantic retrieval, trigger recall, causal links, and health tooling, but it does not statically verify that markdown references still point to real files or that pattern indexes stay in sync.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:6993:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:6994:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:6996:### Finding 2: Dual freshness signals are useful, but only as supplemental trust metadata
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:6998:- **What it does**: Mex emits separate stale-file issues from both elapsed days (`30`/`90`) and commits since last change (`50`/`200`). If git history cannot answer, `git.ts` returns `null`, so the checker silently emits nothing.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:6999:- **Why it matters for us**: The days-plus-commits idea is strong, but silent `null` is too weak for Spec Kit. Our current surfaces already distinguish `exact`, `estimated`, `defaulted`, `unknown`, and degraded matching; freshness should plug into that trust model instead of pretending “no signal” means “healthy.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7000:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7001:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7003:### Finding 3: Mex’s repair brief flow is worth building as a provider-neutral Spec Kit feature
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7005:- **What it does**: `mex sync` groups issues by file, builds one combined brief, includes the current markdown, issue list, nearby filesystem listings for missing paths, and `HEAD~5..HEAD` diffs for referenced paths, then runs an interactive repair loop and re-checks drift after each cycle.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7006:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the best “AI-assisted” idea in Mex. It converts static failures into bounded, auditable repair work instead of re-running broad doc rewrites. That fits Spec Kit’s packet-local remediation style very well.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7007:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7008:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7010:### Finding 4: Command, dependency, cross-file, and script-coverage audits are good ideas, but Mex’s implementation is too Node-centric for direct adoption
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7012:- **What it does**: Mex validates npm/bun/yarn/pnpm scripts and Make targets, checks package dependencies and versions against `package.json`, flags version contradictions across docs, and warns if package scripts never appear in scaffold text. Non-Node manifests are mostly stubbed.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7013:- **Why it matters for us**: We do need broader wrapper/config/doc parity checks, but our truth surfaces span shell commands, MCP tools, Python, TS, generated docs, and spec packets. Mex’s current inventory model is too narrow to trust in our repo.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7014:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7015:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7017:### Finding 5: Scanner-first setup briefs are useful for bootstrapping, but not for durable truth maintenance
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7019:- **What it does**: `mex init` builds a structured brief from manifest, entry points, folder tree, tooling, and README, then tells the AI to populate scaffold files from that brief rather than from raw browsing.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7020:- **Why it matters for us**: This is good for first-pass scaffold seeding or packet bootstrap because it reduces exploratory noise. It is not good enough to become an authority layer for Spec Kit memory or documentation parity, especially with shallow non-Node support and no richer trust contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7021:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7022:- **Impact**: low
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7024:### Finding 6: Mex’s GROW loop and `pattern add` workflow should not become part of Spec Kit’s default documentation lifecycle
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7026:- **What it does**: The contract requires post-task scaffold growth, and `pattern add` immediately creates a task-pattern template plus a placeholder `[description]` row in `patterns/INDEX.md`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7027:- **Why it matters for us**: In Spec Kit, partially formed docs become durable retrieval material quickly. Mex’s loop optimizes for compounding coverage, but it does so before truth is secured. That is the wrong default for a trust-sensitive memory system.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7028:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7029:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7031:### Finding 7: Mex’s single drift score should not be promoted into Spec Kit’s primary health surface
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7033:- **What it does**: Mex starts at `100` and subtracts fixed costs per issue. That makes score the headline surface even though the underlying findings have very different trust implications.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7034:- **Why it matters for us**: Spec Kit already has richer truth states: block-on-write rules, `REJECTED` post-save outcomes, and certainty/provenance fields. A single score would compress those distinctions and make unsafe or unknown states look deceptively acceptable.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7035:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7036:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7074:- Questions answered: adopt only static reference integrity now; prototype freshness only with explicit trust semantics; build repair briefs as a new feature; defer inventory-backed parity until a cross-surface model exists; reject auto-growth defaults and reject score-first health
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7077:- What worked: tracing Mex in execution order, then comparing each mechanism to current Spec Kit trust and health contracts, made the keep/defer/reject boundaries much clearer than the README alone.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7094:FINAL SYNTHESIS: Classify every major Mex mechanism as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, `NEW FEATURE`, or `reject`.
