/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9250:### Finding 1: Mex does not implement live ranked retrieval; it implements deterministic scaffold navigation
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9252:- **What it does**: Mex tells the agent to read `AGENTS.md`, then `ROUTER.md`, then load fixed `context/*.md` or `patterns/INDEX.md` by task type. The CLI exposes `check`, `sync`, `init`, `pattern`, `watch`, and `commands`, but no queryable retrieval/search command over scaffold knowledge.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9253:- **Why it matters for us**: this is not equivalent to `memory_search`, `memory_context`, CocoIndex, or code-graph. Mex retrieval is a navigation protocol over markdown files, not a scored recall system. Replacing our retrieval stack with this would be a capability downgrade.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9254:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9255:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9257:### Finding 2: Mex’s strongest bootstrap retrieval idea is the bounded scanner brief, but it is shallow and heuristic
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9259:- **What it does**: `mex init` builds a compact brief from manifest metadata, guessed entry points, top-level folder categories, tooling detection, and a truncated README, then explicitly tells the AI not to explore the filesystem and to reason from the brief.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9260:- **Why it matters for us**: this is useful as a low-token pre-brief for scaffold/doc generation, but it is not semantic retrieval. It misses deep symbol relationships, intent-based ranking, and any session-aware recall. The non-`package.json` manifest parsers are also stubs.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9261:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9262:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9264:### Finding 3: Mex’s repair-time retrieval is issue-seeded, file-scoped, and more surgical than its general navigation model
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9266:- **What it does**: after drift detection, Mex groups issues by file, embeds the current file contents, lists nearby filesystem reality for missing paths, and attaches a recent `HEAD~5..HEAD` diff for claimed paths. The AI is then asked to fix only those files and only those issues, followed by an immediate re-check loop.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9267:- **Why it matters for us**: this is the most adoptable retrieval pattern in Mex. It is not broad search, but it is very good targeted evidence retrieval for doc repair. It would fit a Spec Kit drift/doc-hygiene runner well.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9268:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9269:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9271:### Finding 4: Mex’s `triggers` are scaffold metadata, not an executable recall mechanism
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9273:- **What it does**: Mex scaffolds `triggers:` into markdown templates, but I found no runtime trigger-matching path in `external/src/` beyond template/prompt emission. By contrast, our `memory_match_triggers` validates scope, supports trusted sessions, applies decay, activates working memory, spreads co-activation, and returns tiered content.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9274:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex’s trigger idea is authoring guidance, not retrieval. We should not simplify our trigger recall down to static frontmatter keywords.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9275:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9276:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9278:### Finding 5: Spec Kit Memory is a real hybrid retrieval engine; Mex is not in the same class
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9280:- **What it does**: our `memory_search` runs a true retrieval pipeline: intent detection, channel routing by query complexity, hybrid candidate generation, vector/FTS/BM25/graph/degree fusion, community fallback, session boost, causal boost, co-activation spreading, artifact routing, feedback signals, reranking, and progressive disclosure.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9281:- **Why it matters for us**: this is the core comparison result. Mex retrieves author-maintained markdown context. We retrieve from indexed memory plus graph signals with runtime scoring and fallback behavior. Mex can inspire add-ons, but it should not be treated as an alternative retrieval engine for our current use case.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9282:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9283:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9285:### Finding 6: Our semantic-to-structural bridge is the sharpest retrieval advantage over Mex
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9287:- **What it does**: our stack classifies queries as structural, semantic, or hybrid; routes structural work to code graph; advertises CocoIndex for semantic code search; and can resolve CocoIndex file-line seeds into graph anchors, then expand them into neighborhood/outline/impact context with next actions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9288:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex has no equivalent bridge between “find related code semantically” and “expand the structural neighborhood around it.” This is the clearest place where our retrieval model is more powerful than markdown scaffold routing.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9289:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9290:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9330:### Finding 1: Mex does not implement live ranked retrieval; it implements deterministic scaffold navigation
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9332:- **What it does**: Mex tells the agent to read `AGENTS.md`, then `ROUTER.md`, then load fixed `context/*.md` or `patterns/INDEX.md` by task type. The CLI exposes `check`, `sync`, `init`, `pattern`, `watch`, and `commands`, but no queryable retrieval/search command over scaffold knowledge.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9333:- **Why it matters for us**: this is not equivalent to `memory_search`, `memory_context`, CocoIndex, or code-graph. Mex retrieval is a navigation protocol over markdown files, not a scored recall system. Replacing our retrieval stack with this would be a capability downgrade.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9334:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9335:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9337:### Finding 2: Mex’s strongest bootstrap retrieval idea is the bounded scanner brief, but it is shallow and heuristic
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9339:- **What it does**: `mex init` builds a compact brief from manifest metadata, guessed entry points, top-level folder categories, tooling detection, and a truncated README, then explicitly tells the AI not to explore the filesystem and to reason from the brief.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9340:- **Why it matters for us**: this is useful as a low-token pre-brief for scaffold/doc generation, but it is not semantic retrieval. It misses deep symbol relationships, intent-based ranking, and any session-aware recall. The non-`package.json` manifest parsers are also stubs.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9341:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9342:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9344:### Finding 3: Mex’s repair-time retrieval is issue-seeded, file-scoped, and more surgical than its general navigation model
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9346:- **What it does**: after drift detection, Mex groups issues by file, embeds the current file contents, lists nearby filesystem reality for missing paths, and attaches a recent `HEAD~5..HEAD` diff for claimed paths. The AI is then asked to fix only those files and only those issues, followed by an immediate re-check loop.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9347:- **Why it matters for us**: this is the most adoptable retrieval pattern in Mex. It is not broad search, but it is very good targeted evidence retrieval for doc repair. It would fit a Spec Kit drift/doc-hygiene runner well.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9348:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9349:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9351:### Finding 4: Mex’s `triggers` are scaffold metadata, not an executable recall mechanism
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9353:- **What it does**: Mex scaffolds `triggers:` into markdown templates, but I found no runtime trigger-matching path in `external/src/` beyond template/prompt emission. By contrast, our `memory_match_triggers` validates scope, supports trusted sessions, applies decay, activates working memory, spreads co-activation, and returns tiered content.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9354:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex’s trigger idea is authoring guidance, not retrieval. We should not simplify our trigger recall down to static frontmatter keywords.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9355:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9356:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9358:### Finding 5: Spec Kit Memory is a real hybrid retrieval engine; Mex is not in the same class
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9360:- **What it does**: our `memory_search` runs a true retrieval pipeline: intent detection, channel routing by query complexity, hybrid candidate generation, vector/FTS/BM25/graph/degree fusion, community fallback, session boost, causal boost, co-activation spreading, artifact routing, feedback signals, reranking, and progressive disclosure.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9361:- **Why it matters for us**: this is the core comparison result. Mex retrieves author-maintained markdown context. We retrieve from indexed memory plus graph signals with runtime scoring and fallback behavior. Mex can inspire add-ons, but it should not be treated as an alternative retrieval engine for our current use case.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9362:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9363:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9365:### Finding 6: Our semantic-to-structural bridge is the sharpest retrieval advantage over Mex
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9367:- **What it does**: our stack classifies queries as structural, semantic, or hybrid; routes structural work to code graph; advertises CocoIndex for semantic code search; and can resolve CocoIndex file-line seeds into graph anchors, then expand them into neighborhood/outline/impact context with next actions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9368:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex has no equivalent bridge between “find related code semantically” and “expand the structural neighborhood around it.” This is the clearest place where our retrieval model is more powerful than markdown scaffold routing.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9369:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9370:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:1:### Finding 5: Auto-Growth Is Powerful, But In Spec Kit It Could Turn Partial Knowledge Into Durable Policy
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:3:- **What it does**: Mex’s contract says every task should update current state and grow patterns. Setup tries to constrain population through a pre-built scanner brief, and `pattern add` creates a template pattern plus a placeholder `[description]` index row immediately.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:4:- **Why it matters for us**: In Spec Kit, docs and packet artifacts are treated as high-authority evidence surfaces. The risk of automatic growth is that incomplete scans, stub manifest parsing, or rushed task summaries become durable instructions that later validators and future sessions treat as truth. Once that happens, repair loops start preserving or propagating the contamination instead of correcting it.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:8:### Finding 6: Mex’s Single Drift Score Would Be Misleading If We Treated It As A Primary Health Signal
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:10:- **What it does**: Mex pools all issues into one score starting at `100`, subtracting `10` per error, `3` per warning, and `1` per info. Spec Kit instead exposes explicit blocking states, degraded/healthy modes, and certainty labels.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:11:- **Why it matters for us**: The risk is overcompression. A repo can still look “healthy” numerically while one critical surface is untrustworthy, or look “bad” because of a cluster of low-value warnings. That is fine for a lightweight CLI summary, but it is the wrong abstraction for a system that already models `unknown`, `estimated`, `exact`, `REJECTED`, and `degraded`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:12:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:13:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:46:Research Mex's markdown scaffold and drift detection patterns to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around staleness detection, codebase-knowledge synchronization, and structured project knowledge. Determine which Mex patterns should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:65:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit validation via `validate.sh` with severity-oriented checks, indexed memory files with frontmatter, and a `MEMORY.md`-style documentation/memory model. What it does **not** currently have is an automated drift checker that compares documentation or memory scaffolds against the actual filesystem, commands, dependencies, git staleness, or cross-file contradictions. Research should stay centered on that gap rather than re-covering general memory persistence already addressed by phase `001`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:89:15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every finding must cite exact file paths, describe what Mex actually does, why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, whether it should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, what subsystem it would affect, and what migration or validation risk comes with it. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:131:`src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts` does not rely on human judgment alone; it flags scaffold files by both elapsed days and commits since last change. A strong finding would explain why combining temporal and repository-activity signals is stronger than date-only freshness, show how that could improve Public's documentation hygiene, and classify the idea as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:176:- findings that classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:186:- recommendation: `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`

When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5695:### Finding 4: Command And Dependency Parity Checks Need A Broader Inventory Model Than Mex Has
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5697:- **What it does**: Mex validates `npm run`, `yarn`, `pnpm`, `bun run`, and `make` claims against `package.json` or `Makefile`; checks dependency claims only against `package.json` deps/devDeps; and flags undocumented scripts by substring-searching scaffold text. Its non-Node manifest handling is scanner-only stub logic, not real parity validation.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5698:- **Why it matters for us**: This addresses a real Public pain point because command-surface drift keeps recurring across docs and wrappers. But integration difficulty is **high**: Spec Kit spans TypeScript, Python, shell, MCP commands, wrapper commands, and spec docs, so we would need a shared command/dependency inventory layer and better text matching than Mex’s substring heuristics to avoid noisy false positives.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5699:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5700:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5702:### Finding 5: Drift-To-Repair Briefing Is Promising, But It Should Become A Spec Kit Repair Surface, Not A Mex-Style CLI Loop
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5704:- **What it does**: Mex groups drift issues by file, embeds current file content, nearby filesystem listings, and `HEAD~5..HEAD` diffs into a repair prompt, then reruns drift checks after an interactive Claude session. The briefing logic is good, but failure/degraded-state handling is shallow compared with Spec Kit’s explicit MCP error envelopes, certainty fields, and post-save review gates.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5705:- **Why it matters for us**: The transferable part is the prompt builder, not the interactive loop. Integration difficulty is **medium-high** because Spec Kit should emit packet-local, auditable repair briefs and route them through existing spec workflows, health surfaces, and certainty contracts rather than shelling out to a provider-specific CLI and treating `status === null` as success.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5706:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5707:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5744:Take the two `adopt now` candidates and design the smallest Spec Kit-native shape for each: `memory_health(reportMode="doc_drift")` for static reference integrity, and a per-file freshness supplement for `memory_health` or `session_bootstrap`. After that, test Modus Memory and Mnemosyne specifically on whether they outperform Mex on explicit degraded-state reporting and scale-aware evidence retrieval.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5755:Research Mex's markdown scaffold and drift detection patterns to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around staleness detection, codebase-knowledge synchronization, and structured project knowledge. Determine which Mex patterns should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5774:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit validation via `validate.sh` with severity-oriented checks, indexed memory files with frontmatter, and a `MEMORY.md`-style documentation/memory model. What it does **not** currently have is an automated drift checker that compares documentation or memory scaffolds against the actual filesystem, commands, dependencies, git staleness, or cross-file contradictions. Research should stay centered on that gap rather than re-covering general memory persistence already addressed by phase `001`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:5798:15. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6058:`src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts` does not rely on human judgment alone; it flags scaffold files by both elapsed days and commits since last change. A strong finding would explain why combining temporal and repository-activity signals is stronger than date-only freshness, show how that could improve Public's documentation hygiene, and classify the idea as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6098:### Finding 1: Typed claim extraction plus packet-local reference integrity is the best immediate import [Rank 1: high x high]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6100:- **What it does**: Mex turns markdown into typed `path`/`command`/`dependency`/`version` claims, then applies deterministic checks for missing paths, dead frontmatter edges, and `patterns/INDEX.md` parity. The path checker already handles scaffold-root vs project-root resolution and downgrades placeholders/pattern examples instead of hard-failing them.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6101:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the cleanest match to the gap in Public: we already have rich memory/search/health surfaces, but we still lack a cheap docs-to-filesystem integrity layer for spec docs, memory docs, and packet-local registries.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6102:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6103:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6105:### Finding 2: Dual-signal freshness is worth adding only if it fails explicitly, not silently [Rank 2: high x medium]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6107:- **What it does**: Mex flags files by both elapsed days and commits since last change, but its git helpers return `null` on failure and the checker simply emits nothing in that case.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6108:- **Why it matters for us**: The signal is strong; the failure contract is weak. Spec Kit already models `exact`, `estimated`, `unknown`, `defaulted`, `REJECTED`, and degraded matching, so a freshness feature should plug into that certainty system instead of quietly disappearing when git evidence is missing.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6109:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6110:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6112:### Finding 3: Drift-to-repair briefing should become a Spec Kit repair surface, not a Mex-style CLI loop [Rank 3: medium x medium]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6114:- **What it does**: Mex groups issues by file and builds repair briefs with the current file, issue list, nearby filesystem inventory, and `HEAD~5..HEAD` diffs. The surrounding sync loop is provider-bound to `claude` and treats exit `0` or `null` as success before re-checking.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6115:- **Why it matters for us**: The brief builder is reusable; the trust model is not. We should preserve the file-scoped, evidence-rich prompt construction and route it through packet-local, auditable Spec Kit workflows with existing review/health gates.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6116:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6117:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6119:### Finding 4: Command, dependency, and coverage parity are valuable, but Mex is too Node-centric to copy directly [Rank 4: medium x low]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6121:- **What it does**: Mex validates `package.json` scripts, Makefile targets, `package.json` dependencies, and scaffold mentions of npm scripts. Its Python/Go/Cargo handling in the scanner is stub-level metadata only, not real parity validation.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6122:- **Why it matters for us**: Public absolutely has command-surface drift, but a direct port would under-model our TypeScript/Python/shell/MCP/spec-doc mix and generate noisy false positives from substring matching.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6123:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6124:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6126:### Finding 5: Auto-growth and pattern seeding are too permissive for Spec Kit’s evidence model [Rank 5: medium x high, negative]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6128:- **What it does**: Mex’s contract says every task should update current state and create new patterns when needed; `pattern add` immediately appends a placeholder `[description]` entry to the index.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6129:- **Why it matters for us**: In Spec Kit, partial or guessed documentation becomes durable policy very quickly. Mex’s growth loop is excellent for momentum, but it is too easy for stub knowledge, shallow scans, or rushed summaries to become “official” and then survive future repair loops.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6130:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6131:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6133:### Finding 6: Mex’s single drift score should not become a primary health signal in Spec Kit [Rank 6: medium x high, negative]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6135:- **What it does**: Mex starts at `100` and subtracts `10/3/1` for error/warning/info, regardless of whether the issue is a hard trust break or mild hygiene noise.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6136:- **Why it matters for us**: Spec Kit already distinguishes blocked, degraded, exact, estimated, unknown, and rejected states. A single number would blur meaningful contract differences and could make a partially unsafe surface look “mostly fine.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6137:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6138:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6150:- Questions answered: the only clear `adopt now` item is static reference integrity built on typed claim extraction. Freshness is the next best candidate, but only with explicit degraded/unknown semantics. Repair briefing is worth building as a new Spec Kit feature. Command/dependency/script parity needs a separate inventory abstraction first. Auto-growth and a single drift score should stay out.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6161:5. Keep rejected: automatic pattern/context growth from task completion, and any single-number health score as the primary truth surface.

The path checker already handles scaffold-root vs project-root resolution and downgrades placeholders/pattern examples instead of hard-failing them.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6183:- **What it does**: Mex flags files by both elapsed days and commits since last change, but its git helpers return `null` on failure and the checker simply emits nothing in that case.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6185:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6186:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6188:### Finding 3: Drift-to-repair briefing should become a Spec Kit repair surface, not a Mex-style CLI loop [Rank 3: medium x medium]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6190:- **What it does**: Mex groups issues by file and builds repair briefs with the current file, issue list, nearby filesystem inventory, and `HEAD~5..HEAD` diffs. The surrounding sync loop is provider-bound to `claude` and treats exit `0` or `null` as success before re-checking.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6191:- **Why it matters for us**: The brief builder is reusable; the trust model is not. We should preserve the file-scoped, evidence-rich prompt construction and route it through packet-local, auditable Spec Kit workflows with existing review/health gates.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6192:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6193:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6195:### Finding 4: Command, dependency, and coverage parity are valuable, but Mex is too Node-centric to copy directly [Rank 4: medium x low]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6197:- **What it does**: Mex validates `package.json` scripts, Makefile targets, `package.json` dependencies, and scaffold mentions of npm scripts. Its Python/Go/Cargo handling in the scanner is stub-level metadata only, not real parity validation.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6198:- **Why it matters for us**: Public absolutely has command-surface drift, but a direct port would under-model our TypeScript/Python/shell/MCP/spec-doc mix and generate noisy false positives from substring matching.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6199:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6200:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6202:### Finding 5: Auto-growth and pattern seeding are too permissive for Spec Kit’s evidence model [Rank 5: medium x high, negative]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6204:- **What it does**: Mex’s contract says every task should update current state and create new patterns when needed; `pattern add` immediately appends a placeholder `[description]` entry to the index.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6205:- **Why it matters for us**: In Spec Kit, partial or guessed documentation becomes durable policy very quickly. Mex’s growth loop is excellent for momentum, but it is too easy for stub knowledge, shallow scans, or rushed summaries to become “official” and then survive future repair loops.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6206:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6207:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6209:### Finding 6: Mex’s single drift score should not become a primary health signal in Spec Kit [Rank 6: medium x high, negative]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6211:- **What it does**: Mex starts at `100` and subtracts `10/3/1` for error/warning/info, regardless of whether the issue is a hard trust break or mild hygiene noise.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6212:- **Why it matters for us**: Spec Kit already distinguishes blocked, degraded, exact, estimated, unknown, and rejected states. A single number would blur meaningful contract differences and could make a partially unsafe surface look “mostly fine.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6213:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6214:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6226:- Questions answered: the only clear `adopt now` item is static reference integrity built on typed claim extraction. Freshness is the next best candidate, but only with explicit degraded/unknown semantics. Repair briefing is worth building as a new Spec Kit feature. Command/dependency/script parity needs a separate inventory abstraction first. Auto-growth and a single drift score should stay out.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:6237:5. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:77:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:78:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:80:### Finding 4: Scope and mutation authority must stay governed even when ergonomics improve
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:82:- **What it does**: Engram’s lifecycle and topic boundaries, Mnemosyne’s project/global split, MemPalace’s separate fact plane, and Modus’s failure modes all reinforce the same rule: convenience surfaces are acceptable only when they compile down to a trusted scope and audited mutation model.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:83:- **Why it matters**: Public is already closer to the right default here than any external system in this set. The safe direction is ergonomic aliases over existing authority, not cheaper write paths or weaker identity rules.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:84:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:85:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:87:### Finding 5: Mex is uniquely valuable because it checks documentation truth against the filesystem, not because it stores memory better
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:89:- **What it does**: Mex’s standout mechanism is typed markdown claim extraction plus zero-token path, edge, index, and freshness checking. None of the other four phases contributed a comparably strong docs-to-code integrity layer.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:90:- **Why it matters**: This is the one area where Mex adds genuinely new capability to Public rather than overlapping with retrieval, session recovery, or local-memory ergonomics we already have.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:91:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:92:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:94:### Finding 6: Mex’s repair-brief loop is also unique, but its auto-growth and score-centric habits are the wrong transplant
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:96:- **What it does**: `mex sync` turns failed drift checks into bounded repair briefs with local file context and recent diffs, while `pattern add`, scaffold GROW defaults, and the single drift score push partially trusted content and compressed health reporting too early.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:97:- **Why it matters**: The repair loop fits Public’s packet-local remediation style very well. The auto-growth and score-first pieces cut directly against Public’s trust-sensitive memory model.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:98:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:99:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:116:Research Mex's markdown scaffold and drift detection patterns to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around staleness detection, codebase-knowledge synchronization, and structured project knowledge. Determine which Mex patterns should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:135:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit validation via `validate.sh` with severity-oriented checks, indexed memory files with frontmatter, and a `MEMORY.md`-style documentation/memory model. What it does **not** currently have is an automated drift checker that compares documentation or memory scaffolds against the actual filesystem, commands, dependencies, git staleness, or cross-file contradictions. Research should stay centered on that gap rather than re-covering general memory persistence already addressed by phase `001`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:159:15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every finding must cite exact file paths, describe what Mex actually does, why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, whether it should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, what subsystem it would affect, and what migration or validation risk comes with it. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:201:`src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts` does not rely on human judgment alone; it flags scaffold files by both elapsed days and commits since last change. A strong finding would explain why combining temporal and repository-activity signals is stronger than date-only freshness, show how that could improve Public's documentation hygiene, and classify the idea as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:246:- findings that classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`

Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) Create a rich sandbox memory file and a thin sandbox memory file 2) `memory_stats()` and capture baseline totals 3) `memory_save({filePath:"<thin-sandbox-file>", dryRun:true})` and verify preflight details, `qualityLoop`, `sufficiency`, and `rejectionCode:"INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT"` 4) `memory_stats()` and `memory_search({query:"<thin title>"})` to verify no new indexed record from dry-run 5) `memory_save({filePath:"<thin-sandbox-file>", force:true})` and verify it still rejects before indexing 6) `memory_save({filePath:"<rich-sandbox-file>", dryRun:true})` and verify `would_pass:true` with no side effects 7) `memory_save({filePath:"<rich-sandbox-file>", dryRun:false})` and verify the memory becomes searchable | Dry-run returns preflight plus quality-loop and sufficiency payloads; thin memories report `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT` without indexing/database mutation; `force:true` does not bypass insufficiency; rich non-dry-run save indexes the same file | Dry-run responses for thin and rich files + before/after stats + search outputs + non-dry-run save output | PASS if dry-run surfaces sufficiency explicitly with no index mutation, forced thin save still rejects, and rich non-dry-run save makes the record searchable | Inspect `handlers/memory-save.ts` dryRun and insufficiency branches, `shared/parsing/memory-sufficiency.ts`, `handlers/quality-loop.ts`, and `lib/validation/preflight.ts` |
