Capture the evidence needed to prove Dry-run surfaces template-contract violations; non-dry-run rejects malformed files before index side effects; valid rendered output remains validator-clean. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) Create a sandbox memory missing mandatory anchors or required frontmatter keys 2) `memory_save({ filePath:"<sandbox-file>", dryRun:true })` and verify contract-violation details 3) `memory_save({ filePath:"<sandbox-file>", force:true })` and verify rejection before indexing 4) Run `generate-context.js` with a valid rich JSON payload and verify the rendered output remains validator-clean | Dry-run surfaces template-contract violations; non-dry-run rejects malformed files before index side effects; valid rendered output remains validator-clean | Dry-run/save rejection output + successful render validation evidence | PASS if malformed files are rejected before write/index and valid rendered output remains validator-clean | Inspect `shared/parsing/memory-template-contract.ts`, `scripts/core/workflow.ts`, and `mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts` |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:5099:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/06--analysis/07-learning-history-memorygetlearninghistory.md:24:Handler safety around the backing table is tighter now. Schema initialization is tracked per database instance with `WeakSet<Database>` instead of a process-global boolean, so swapping to a fresh DB connection still creates the `session_learning` table and index when needed. Score validation also rejects `NaN` via `Number.isFinite(...)`, closing the old loophole where `typeof NaN === 'number'` let malformed learning scores pass validation.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:5130:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/11--scoring-and-calibration/198-temporal-structural-coherence-scoring.md:19:- Prompt: `Verify temporal-structural coherence scoring in the quality loop using one well-formed control memory and flawed variants. Submit variants that are too short or missing headings, include a future-dated completion claim, and include self-referential or unresolved causal links. Confirm the coherence breakdown applies the expected structural checks and bounded penalties, and verify that low-coherence content is rejected or downgraded while the quality loop retry cycle stays immediate and bounded. Capture the score breakdown and return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:5131:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/11--scoring-and-calibration/198-temporal-structural-coherence-scoring.md:20:- Expected signals: structural checks evaluate non-empty content, minimum-length thresholds, and Markdown heading presence; future-dated completion claims reduce coherence; self-referential or unresolved causal links reduce coherence; sufficiently low coherence can trigger quality-loop rejection; verify-fix-verify retries remain immediate with no backoff and stay within the configured retry limit
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:5132:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/11--scoring-and-calibration/198-temporal-structural-coherence-scoring.md:21:- Pass/fail: PASS: flawed variants receive the expected coherence penalties and rejection or downgrade behavior while retries stay immediate and bounded; FAIL: structural defects do not affect coherence, temporal or causal-link issues are ignored, or retry behavior contradicts the documented bounded immediate cycle
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:5133:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/11--scoring-and-calibration/198-temporal-structural-coherence-scoring.md:29:| 198 | Temporal-structural coherence scoring | Confirm structural penalties, bounded coherence deductions, and immediate retry behavior in the quality loop | `Verify temporal-structural coherence scoring in the quality loop using one well-formed control memory and flawed variants. Submit variants that are too short or missing headings, include a future-dated completion claim, and include self-referential or unresolved causal links. Confirm the coherence breakdown applies the expected structural checks and bounded penalties, and verify that low-coherence content is rejected or downgraded while the quality loop retry cycle stays immediate and bounded. Capture the score breakdown and return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) Prepare a well-formed control memory plus flawed variants covering short or heading-less content, future-dated completion claims, and broken causal-link metadata 2) Run the quality loop or ingest path for each case 3) Inspect the coherence score breakdown and quality-loop decision for control vs flawed variants 4) Confirm penalties apply to temporal and causal-link issues without inventing broader chronology logic 5) For an auto-fixable case, capture the retry cycle and confirm retries occur immediately with no backoff and stop within the configured limit | Structural checks evaluate content presence, minimum length, and headings; future-dated completion claims reduce coherence; self-referential or unresolved causal links reduce coherence; low-coherence variants are rejected or downgraded; retry attempts are immediate and bounded | Quality-loop transcript, coherence score breakdown, control-vs-variant results, and retry timing evidence | PASS: coherence penalties appear for the flawed variants, low-quality cases are blocked or downgraded, and retry timing stays immediate and bounded; FAIL: penalties are missing, weak content passes without explanation, or retries show unexpected backoff or unbounded looping | Verify control content satisfies structural checks -> Inspect coherence penalty branches for future-dated claims and causal links -> Confirm rejection threshold or downgrade path -> Check retry loop configuration and attempt count -> Ensure broader chronology analysis was not assumed in the verdict |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:5245:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/12-session-capturing-pipeline-quality.md:84:   - `validate-memory-quality.ts` V11 rule rejects memories with error-dominated descriptions, titles, or trigger phrases
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:5357:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/32-shared-script-libraries-and-utilities.md:29:The rest of `lib/` provides policy and compatibility helpers. `validate-memory-quality.ts` is the pure post-render quality gate with explicit validation-rule metadata, write-vs-index dispositions, contamination handling, YAML parsing, and source-aware blocking behavior. `memory-frontmatter.ts` strips markdown noise from titles, rejects legacy generic descriptions and trigger phrases, derives higher-quality descriptions, and regenerates trigger phrases from summary text plus spec-folder tokens. `phase-classifier.ts` groups exchanges into topic clusters and conversation phases such as Research, Planning, Implementation, Debugging, and Verification. The shell helpers keep the Bash workflows thin: `shell-common.sh` provides JSON escaping and repo-root detection, while `template-utils.sh` maps documentation levels to template directories and copies files with level-specific fallback behavior. `embeddings.ts` is intentionally only a re-export shim to the canonical shared embeddings module.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:5502:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/13--memory-quality-and-indexing/17-outsourced-agent-memory-capture.md:3:description: "Outsourced agent memory capture keeps external CLI save payloads aligned with the current `generate-context.js` contract. It enforces hard-fail validation for explicit JSON data files, persists `nextSteps` fields into memory observations, and now documents the post-010 save gates that can still reject a valid handback after normalization."
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:5503:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/13--memory-quality-and-indexing/17-outsourced-agent-memory-capture.md:10:Outsourced agent handback keeps external CLI save payloads aligned with the current `generate-context.js` contract. It enforces hard-fail validation for explicit JSON data files, persists `nextSteps` fields into memory observations, and now documents the post-010 save gates that can still reject a valid handback after normalization.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:5571:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/04--maintenance/01-workspace-scanning-and-indexing-memoryindexscan.md:24:In incremental mode (the default), the scanner categorizes every discovered file into one of four buckets: to-index (new files), to-update (changed mtime or content hash), to-skip (unchanged mtime and matching content hash) and to-delete (files that disappeared from disk). The content-hash secondary check catches timestamp-preserving rewrites that would otherwise look unchanged from mtime alone. Batch processing with configurable `BATCH_SIZE` handles large workspaces, but oversized requests are clamped to the hard runtime maximum with a warning instead of exploding fan-out. Scan throttling now uses an atomic lease instead of a check-then-set cooldown gate: `acquireIndexScanLease()` reads `last_index_scan` and `scan_started_at` in one transaction, expires stale leases left behind by crashed scans, reserves a fresh run by writing `scan_started_at`, rejects overlapping fresh scans with `reason: 'lease_active'`, and still enforces `INDEX_SCAN_COOLDOWN` after completed runs with `reason: 'cooldown'`. The handler returns an E429 response with the computed wait time whenever either guard rejects the request.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:5664:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:935:Pass `onlyComplete: true` to restrict results to tasks where both preflight and postflight were recorded. This gives you clean data for trend analysis without incomplete records skewing the averages. Records are ordered by `updated_at` descending so the most recent learning cycles appear first. The handler now tracks schema initialization per database instance rather than per process, so fresh DB connections still create `session_learning` correctly, and score validation rejects `NaN` so malformed learning inputs cannot silently persist.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:5835:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/22--context-preservation-and-code-graph/265-gemini-hooks.md:21:- **Prompt**: `Validate 265 Gemini hooks. Simulate a Gemini SessionStart event with source=startup. Confirm: (1) session-prime.ts reads JSON from stdin with session_id, (2) output is JSON with hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext containing session context, (3) compact-inject.ts performs one-shot injection on BeforeAgent, (4) shared.ts parseGeminiStdin correctly parses Gemini input format, (5) stale cache (>30 min) is rejected.`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:5836:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/22--context-preservation-and-code-graph/265-gemini-hooks.md:28:  - PASS: Session-prime delivers context on startup, compact-inject is one-shot, stdin parsing works, stale cache rejected

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:10944:- **What it does**: Mex uses dual thresholds on days and commits, but silently collapses git failures to `null`. Spec Kit already has a better trust precedent in `session_resume`: freshness is surfaced as an explicit contract, not hidden behind a score. The right transplant is a supplemental signal, not a ranker.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:10945:- **Why it matters**: We already persist `headRef`, `commitRef`, `repositoryState`, and reviewer guardrails for missing provenance. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:1:### Finding 3: Mex’s repair-time retrieval is issue-seeded, file-scoped, and more surgical than its general navigation model
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:3:- **What it does**: after drift detection, Mex groups issues by file, embeds the current file contents, lists nearby filesystem reality for missing paths, and attaches a recent `HEAD~5..HEAD` diff for claimed paths. The AI is then asked to fix only those files and only those issues, followed by an immediate re-check loop.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:4:- **Why it matters for us**: this is the most adoptable retrieval pattern in Mex. It is not broad search, but it is very good targeted evidence retrieval for doc repair. It would fit a Spec Kit drift/doc-hygiene runner well.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:5:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:6:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8:### Finding 4: Mex’s `triggers` are scaffold metadata, not an executable recall mechanism
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:10:- **What it does**: Mex scaffolds `triggers:` into markdown templates, but I found no runtime trigger-matching path in `external/src/` beyond template/prompt emission. By contrast, our `memory_match_triggers` validates scope, supports trusted sessions, applies decay, activates working memory, spreads co-activation, and returns tiered content.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:11:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex’s trigger idea is authoring guidance, not retrieval. We should not simplify our trigger recall down to static frontmatter keywords.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:12:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:13:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:15:### Finding 5: Spec Kit Memory is a real hybrid retrieval engine; Mex is not in the same class
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:17:- **What it does**: our `memory_search` runs a true retrieval pipeline: intent detection, channel routing by query complexity, hybrid candidate generation, vector/FTS/BM25/graph/degree fusion, community fallback, session boost, causal boost, co-activation spreading, artifact routing, feedback signals, reranking, and progressive disclosure.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:18:- **Why it matters for us**: this is the core comparison result. Mex retrieves author-maintained markdown context. We retrieve from indexed memory plus graph signals with runtime scoring and fallback behavior. Mex can inspire add-ons, but it should not be treated as an alternative retrieval engine for our current use case.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:19:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:20:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:22:### Finding 6: Our semantic-to-structural bridge is the sharpest retrieval advantage over Mex
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:24:- **What it does**: our stack classifies queries as structural, semantic, or hybrid; routes structural work to code graph; advertises CocoIndex for semantic code search; and can resolve CocoIndex file-line seeds into graph anchors, then expand them into neighborhood/outline/impact context with next actions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:25:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex has no equivalent bridge between “find related code semantically” and “expand the structural neighborhood around it.” This is the clearest place where our retrieval model is more powerful than markdown scaffold routing.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:26:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:27:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:65:Research Mex's markdown scaffold and drift detection patterns to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around staleness detection, codebase-knowledge synchronization, and structured project knowledge. Determine which Mex patterns should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:84:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit validation via `validate.sh` with severity-oriented checks, indexed memory files with frontmatter, and a `MEMORY.md`-style documentation/memory model. What it does **not** currently have is an automated drift checker that compares documentation or memory scaffolds against the actual filesystem, commands, dependencies, git staleness, or cross-file contradictions. Research should stay centered on that gap rather than re-covering general memory persistence already addressed by phase `001`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:108:15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every finding must cite exact file paths, describe what Mex actually does, why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, whether it should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, what subsystem it would affect, and what migration or validation risk comes with it. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:150:`src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts` does not rely on human judgment alone; it flags scaffold files by both elapsed days and commits since last change. A strong finding would explain why combining temporal and repository-activity signals is stronger than date-only freshness, show how that could improve Public's documentation hygiene, and classify the idea as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:195:- findings that classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:205:- recommendation: `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9256:   798	- symptom: memory save quality gate rejects closeout payload; cause: weak primary evidence + noisy trigger phrases; fix: include explicit files/observations/toolCalls/decisions and clean trigger phrases before rerunning quality validation [Task 1]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9278:### Finding 1: Mex’s “session bootstrap” is a static reading ritual, not an executable recovery surface
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9280:- **What it does**: Mex defines bootstrap as “read `AGENTS.md`, then `ROUTER.md`, then the routed context files,” with `Current Project State` in markdown acting as the grounding mechanism. Our `session_bootstrap()` is executable: it calls `session_resume`, calls `session_health`, builds a structural readiness contract, emits hints/next actions, and attaches trust metadata to the payload.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9281:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex has a useful human-readable anchor, but it cannot answer “is resume context stale?”, “is the code graph missing?”, or “what recovery action should I take next?” at runtime. Our bootstrap layer is materially stronger for non-hook recovery and operational safety.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9282:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9283:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9285:### Finding 2: Mex’s `init`/`setup` flow creates a cold-start brief, but it is not continuity state
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9287:- **What it does**: Mex scans once, serializes a compact brief, and either launches Claude with that prompt or prints it for manual paste. Verification is “start a fresh session and ask the agent what it knows.” Our resume path instead reconstitutes live state each time through `memory_context(mode:"resume")`, then optionally appends a cached continuity summary only if transcript identity, freshness window, and scope checks all pass.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9288:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex’s scanner is a good bootstrap compression tool, but it is setup-time scaffolding, not session continuity. It does not persist or validate ongoing conversational state the way our resume stack does.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9289:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9290:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9292:### Finding 3: Mex has a strong repair lifecycle loop even though it lacks a true session subsystem
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9294:- **What it does**: Mex runs a tight lifecycle: drift check, filter/group issues, build one combined repair brief, run one Claude session, re-check, compute score delta, and ask whether to continue. The state is not durable, but the operational loop is disciplined and verification-first.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9295:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the most reusable lifecycle idea in Mex. It fits our doc/memory drift work well: keep `session_bootstrap`, `memory_context`, and `generate-context.js` as the continuity backbone, but wrap drift remediation in a Mex-style “fix only the broken files, then re-verify immediately” loop.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9296:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9297:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9299:### Finding 4: Spec Kit Memory has a trusted, scoped session model; Mex effectively has none
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9301:- **What it does**: Our stack mints server-managed session IDs, rejects untrusted caller IDs, binds sessions to tenant/user/agent scope, persists `session_state`, keeps ephemeral retrieval-state for seen results/open questions/anchors, and injects working-memory prompt context during resume. Mex’s code has config, drift, scanner, sync, and scaffold types, but no comparable runtime session identity, scope validation, or cross-turn retrieval state.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9302:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the biggest lifecycle gap. Mex can preserve project knowledge in markdown, but it cannot preserve trusted per-session retrieval state or safely resume scoped working context across turns.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9303:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9304:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9306:### Finding 5: `generate-context.js` is a real session-capture pipeline; Mex’s GROW model is authoring discipline, not structured capture
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9308:- **What it does**: Our save path accepts structured JSON/stdin, treats explicit CLI spec-folder targets as authoritative, and materializes durable artifacts containing summary, outcomes, blockers, next action, canonical-doc awareness, session ID, git provenance, and context typing. Mex’s equivalent is “after task, update `ROUTER.md`, update context files, create/update patterns, and bump `last_updated`.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9309:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex’s GROW step is valuable for maintaining a human-readable scaffold, but it is not a substitute for structured session capture. Replacing `generate-context.js` with Mex-style manual scaffold mutation would be a lifecycle regression.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9310:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9311:- **Impact**: high

Our `session_bootstrap()` is executable: it calls `session_resume`, calls `session_health`, builds a structural readiness contract, emits hints/next actions, and attaches trust metadata to the payload.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9366:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9368:### Finding 2: Mex’s `init`/`setup` flow creates a cold-start brief, but it is not continuity state
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9370:- **What it does**: Mex scans once, serializes a compact brief, and either launches Claude with that prompt or prints it for manual paste. Verification is “start a fresh session and ask the agent what it knows.” Our resume path instead reconstitutes live state each time through `memory_context(mode:"resume")`, then optionally appends a cached continuity summary only if transcript identity, freshness window, and scope checks all pass.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9371:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex’s scanner is a good bootstrap compression tool, but it is setup-time scaffolding, not session continuity. It does not persist or validate ongoing conversational state the way our resume stack does.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9372:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9373:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9375:### Finding 3: Mex has a strong repair lifecycle loop even though it lacks a true session subsystem
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9377:- **What it does**: Mex runs a tight lifecycle: drift check, filter/group issues, build one combined repair brief, run one Claude session, re-check, compute score delta, and ask whether to continue. The state is not durable, but the operational loop is disciplined and verification-first.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9378:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the most reusable lifecycle idea in Mex. It fits our doc/memory drift work well: keep `session_bootstrap`, `memory_context`, and `generate-context.js` as the continuity backbone, but wrap drift remediation in a Mex-style “fix only the broken files, then re-verify immediately” loop.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9379:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9380:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9382:### Finding 4: Spec Kit Memory has a trusted, scoped session model; Mex effectively has none
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9384:- **What it does**: Our stack mints server-managed session IDs, rejects untrusted caller IDs, binds sessions to tenant/user/agent scope, persists `session_state`, keeps ephemeral retrieval-state for seen results/open questions/anchors, and injects working-memory prompt context during resume. Mex’s code has config, drift, scanner, sync, and scaffold types, but no comparable runtime session identity, scope validation, or cross-turn retrieval state.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9385:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the biggest lifecycle gap. Mex can preserve project knowledge in markdown, but it cannot preserve trusted per-session retrieval state or safely resume scoped working context across turns.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9386:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9387:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9389:### Finding 5: `generate-context.js` is a real session-capture pipeline; Mex’s GROW model is authoring discipline, not structured capture
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9391:- **What it does**: Our save path accepts structured JSON/stdin, treats explicit CLI spec-folder targets as authoritative, and materializes durable artifacts containing summary, outcomes, blockers, next action, canonical-doc awareness, session ID, git provenance, and context typing. Mex’s equivalent is “after task, update `ROUTER.md`, update context files, create/update patterns, and bump `last_updated`.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9392:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex’s GROW step is valuable for maintaining a human-readable scaffold, but it is not a substitute for structured session capture. Replacing `generate-context.js` with Mex-style manual scaffold mutation would be a lifecycle regression.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9393:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9394:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:11:- Questions answered: the only clear `adopt now` item is static reference integrity built on typed claim extraction. Freshness is the next best candidate, but only with explicit degraded/unknown semantics. Repair briefing is worth building as a new Spec Kit feature. Command/dependency/script parity needs a separate inventory abstraction first. Auto-growth and a single drift score should stay out.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:22:5. Keep rejected: automatic pattern/context growth from task completion, and any single-number health score as the primary truth surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:25:### Finding N: [Title]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:27:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:28:- **Why it matters for us**: concrete relevance
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:29:- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:30:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:46:### Finding 1: Static markdown reference integrity is the only clear adopt-now slice
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:48:- **What it does**: Mex extracts typed markdown claims from inline code, code blocks, and dependency headings, then runs zero-token checks for missing paths, dead frontmatter edges, and `patterns/INDEX.md` drift. It already distinguishes real missing references from pattern/template placeholders by downgrading some path misses to warnings.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:49:- **Why it matters for us**: This cleanly maps to our real gap. Spec Kit already has semantic retrieval, trigger recall, causal links, and health tooling, but it does not statically verify that markdown references still point to real files or that pattern indexes stay in sync.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:50:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:51:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:62:Research Mex's markdown scaffold and drift detection patterns to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around staleness detection, codebase-knowledge synchronization, and structured project knowledge. Determine which Mex patterns should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:81:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit validation via `validate.sh` with severity-oriented checks, indexed memory files with frontmatter, and a `MEMORY.md`-style documentation/memory model. What it does **not** currently have is an automated drift checker that compares documentation or memory scaffolds against the actual filesystem, commands, dependencies, git staleness, or cross-file contradictions. Research should stay centered on that gap rather than re-covering general memory persistence already addressed by phase `001`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:105:15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every finding must cite exact file paths, describe what Mex actually does, why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, whether it should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, what subsystem it would affect, and what migration or validation risk comes with it. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:147:`src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts` does not rely on human judgment alone; it flags scaffold files by both elapsed days and commits since last change. A strong finding would explain why combining temporal and repository-activity signals is stronger than date-only freshness, show how that could improve Public's documentation hygiene, and classify the idea as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
