/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:135989:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:135990:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:136045:Research Mex's markdown scaffold and drift detection patterns to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around staleness detection, codebase-knowledge synchronization, and structured project knowledge. Determine which Mex patterns should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:136064:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit validation via `validate.sh` with severity-oriented checks, indexed memory files with frontmatter, and a `MEMORY.md`-style documentation/memory model. What it does **not** currently have is an automated drift checker that compares documentation or memory scaffolds against the actual filesystem, commands, dependencies, git staleness, or cross-file contradictions. Research should stay centered on that gap rather than re-covering general memory persistence already addressed by phase `001`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:136088:15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every finding must cite exact file paths, describe what Mex actually does, why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, whether it should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, what subsystem it would affect, and what migration or validation risk comes with it. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:136130:`src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts` does not rely on human judgment alone; it flags scaffold files by both elapsed days and commits since last change. A strong finding would explain why combining temporal and repository-activity signals is stronger than date-only freshness, show how that could improve Public's documentation hygiene, and classify the idea as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:136175:- findings that classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:136185:- recommendation: `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:136225:### Finding N: [Title]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:136227:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:136228:- **Why it matters for us**: concrete relevance
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:136229:- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:136230:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:139300:    reason: 'Sparse trigger phrases are diagnostic, but not enough to reject an otherwise valid rendered memory.',
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:140977:    89	    reason: 'Sparse trigger phrases are diagnostic, but not enough to reject an otherwise valid rendered memory.',
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141302:### Finding 1: Mex preserves issue locality from drift detection into repair
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141304:- **What it does**: drift starts as typed `Claim` objects, becomes typed `DriftIssue` objects carrying `file`, `line`, and optional triggering `claim`, then `runSync()` groups those issues into `SyncTarget[]` by file before any AI step. The repair unit is not “the scaffold” or “the repo”; it is a concrete broken scaffold file with enumerated issue codes.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141305:- **Why it matters for us**: this is the real difference between Mex and our current surfaces. Spec Kit has retrieval and health tools, but no file-scoped documentation repair pipeline. A Spec Kit equivalent would let us repair drifted docs/spec guidance surgically instead of falling back to broad manual resync or fresh repo-wide analysis.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141306:- **Recommendation**: `NEW FEATURE`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141307:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141309:### Finding 2: The sync brief is bounded on purpose and only adds evidence needed to repair the flagged file
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141311:- **What it does**: `buildFileSection()` embeds only four things: the flagged file path, issue list, current file contents, and narrowly relevant repair context. For `MISSING_PATH`, it lists actual nearby directory contents and a shallow extension-based fuzzy search. For path-linked issues, it adds a recent `HEAD~5..HEAD` diff for the referenced paths. The prompt then explicitly says “Only change what’s necessary.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141312:- **Why it matters for us**: this is the mechanism that makes targeted sync cheap and stable. Our post-save review and memory-quality validation detect problems after writes, but they do not assemble a bounded repair packet with just-enough filesystem and git evidence. That missing middle layer is why our remediation is still more operator-driven than system-driven.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141313:- **Recommendation**: `adopt now`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141314:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141316:### Finding 3: Mex’s repair loop is severity-gated and iterative, which limits churn
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141318:- **What it does**: `runSync()` ignores warning-only files unless `--warnings` is passed, batches only error-bearing files into targets, runs one repair cycle, reruns drift detection, reports score delta, and asks whether to continue. The score itself is intentionally simple: `100 - 10/error - 3/warning - 1/info`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141319:- **Why it matters for us**: if we add a sync surface, this gating behavior is more reusable than the score itself. The important pattern is “repair only hard-fail surfaces first, verify immediately, then optionally widen scope.” That fits Spec Kit’s strict-validation workflow much better than a blanket rewrite mode.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141320:- **Recommendation**: `adopt now`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141321:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141323:### Finding 4: Claim extraction is the prerequisite for targeted sync, but the heuristic layer is shallow by design
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141325:- **What it does**: Mex extracts path claims from inline code that looks path-like, command claims from inline code and fenced blocks, and dependency/version claims from bold text inside dependency-like headings. It explicitly filters routes, HTTP methods, code-like snippets, quoted assignments, and template placeholders.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141326:- **Why it matters for us**: a Spec Kit sync loop would need the same “claim envelope” concept, but probably not Mex’s exact markdown heuristics. Our corpus has stronger structure than generic scaffold markdown: frontmatter, anchors, spec-doc sections, memory metadata blocks, checklist semantics, and command surfaces. The transferable idea is typed claim extraction, not Mex’s literal regex set.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141327:- **Recommendation**: `prototype later`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141328:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141330:### Finding 5: Spec Kit has adjacent diagnostics, but not a repair control plane
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141332:- **What it does**: `memory_context` routes retrieval; `session_bootstrap` merges resume plus health hints; `post-save-review` compares rendered memory markdown against original collected data after save; `validate-memory-quality` blocks or downgrades bad memory writes/indexing; `memory_health` reports diagnostics and guarded auto-repair metadata. These are real safeguards, but they are retrieval-time, save-time, or system-health-time safeguards.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141333:- **Why it matters for us**: this clarifies the exact gap. We are stronger than Mex on durable-memory validation and retrieval orchestration, but weaker on turning documentation/spec drift into a bounded remediation loop. Mex’s second pattern is not a better memory store; it is a missing repair layer between “validator found drift” and “human manually fixes docs.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141334:- **Recommendation**: `NEW FEATURE`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141335:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141337:### Finding 6: Mex’s bounded sync is useful, but its evidence model has clear limits we should not copy blindly
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141339:- **What it does**: Mex still sends all current targets into one Claude session, uses a shallow extension search for missing-path hints, and limits diff context to `HEAD~5..HEAD`. That keeps prompts small, but it can miss older renames, deeper moves, or semantically related repo changes outside the claimed paths.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141340:- **Why it matters for us**: if we build this in Spec Kit, the core loop is worth copying, but the evidence retrieval should probably use our stronger search surfaces: code graph for structural doc claims, CocoIndex for semantic recovery, and spec-local metadata rather than extension-only filesystem scans.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141341:- **Recommendation**: `prototype later`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141342:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141387:### Finding 1: Mex preserves issue locality from drift detection into repair
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141389:- **What it does**: drift starts as typed `Claim` objects, becomes typed `DriftIssue` objects carrying `file`, `line`, and optional triggering `claim`, then `runSync()` groups those issues into `SyncTarget[]` by file before any AI step. The repair unit is not “the scaffold” or “the repo”; it is a concrete broken scaffold file with enumerated issue codes.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141390:- **Why it matters for us**: this is the real difference between Mex and our current surfaces. Spec Kit has retrieval and health tools, but no file-scoped documentation repair pipeline. A Spec Kit equivalent would let us repair drifted docs/spec guidance surgically instead of falling back to broad manual resync or fresh repo-wide analysis.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141391:- **Recommendation**: `NEW FEATURE`

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141396:- **What it does**: `buildFileSection()` embeds only four things: the flagged file path, issue list, current file contents, and narrowly relevant repair context. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141474:- **What it does**: `memory_context` routes retrieval; `session_bootstrap` merges resume plus health hints; `post-save-review` compares rendered memory markdown against original collected data after save; `validate-memory-quality` blocks or downgrades bad memory writes/indexing; `memory_health` reports diagnostics and guarded auto-repair metadata. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141475:- **Why it matters for us**: this clarifies the exact gap. We are stronger than Mex on durable-memory validation and retrieval orchestration, but weaker on turning documentation/spec drift into a bounded remediation loop. Mex’s second pattern is not a better memory store; it is a missing repair layer between “validator found drift” and “human manually fixes docs.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141476:- **Recommendation**: `NEW FEATURE`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141477:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141479:### Finding 6: Mex’s bounded sync is useful, but its evidence model has clear limits we should not copy blindly
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141481:- **What it does**: Mex still sends all current targets into one Claude session, uses a shallow extension search for missing-path hints, and limits diff context to `HEAD~5..HEAD`. That keeps prompts small, but it can miss older renames, deeper moves, or semantically related repo changes outside the claimed paths.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141482:- **Why it matters for us**: if we build this in Spec Kit, the core loop is worth copying, but the evidence retrieval should probably use our stronger search surfaces: code graph for structural doc claims, CocoIndex for semantic recovery, and spec-local metadata rather than extension-only filesystem scans.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141483:- **Recommendation**: `prototype later`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141484:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141529:Research Mex's markdown scaffold and drift detection patterns to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around staleness detection, codebase-knowledge synchronization, and structured project knowledge. Determine which Mex patterns should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141548:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit validation via `validate.sh` with severity-oriented checks, indexed memory files with frontmatter, and a `MEMORY.md`-style documentation/memory model. What it does **not** currently have is an automated drift checker that compares documentation or memory scaffolds against the actual filesystem, commands, dependencies, git staleness, or cross-file contradictions. Research should stay centered on that gap rather than re-covering general memory persistence already addressed by phase `001`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141572:15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every finding must cite exact file paths, describe what Mex actually does, why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, whether it should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, what subsystem it would affect, and what migration or validation risk comes with it. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141614:`src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts` does not rely on human judgment alone; it flags scaffold files by both elapsed days and commits since last change. A strong finding would explain why combining temporal and repository-activity signals is stronger than date-only freshness, show how that could improve Public's documentation hygiene, and classify the idea as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141659:- findings that classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141669:- recommendation: `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141709:### Finding N: [Title]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:141711:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:147997:   401	      else reject(new Error(`Claude exited with code ${code}`));
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:148001:   405	      reject(new Error(`Failed to launch Claude: ${err.message}`));
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:148792:    ).rejects.toThrow("already exists");
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:148805:    ).rejects.toThrow("Invalid pattern name");
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:148809:    ).rejects.toThrow("Invalid pattern name");
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:149637:    reason: 'Sparse trigger phrases are diagnostic, but not enough to reject an otherwise valid rendered memory.',
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150379:    89	    reason: 'Sparse trigger phrases are diagnostic, but not enough to reject an otherwise valid rendered memory.',
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150601:### Finding 1: Mex fails open when git- or manifest-backed evidence disappears, so checks quietly lose authority instead of reporting degraded state
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150603:- **What it does**: git freshness and diff helpers return `null` or `""` on failure, command/dependency loaders return `null` on parse failure, and setup falls back from scanner-brief mode to raw filesystem exploration with only a warning. Mex keeps running, but several checks simply stop contributing evidence.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150604:- **Why it matters for us**: this is the opposite of how Spec Kit Memory already treats operational uncertainty: our health/bootstrap surfaces emit explicit error payloads, request IDs, and stale/missing hints instead of silently narrowing authority ([memory-crud-health.ts:222](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:222), [session-bootstrap.ts:163](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts:163)). If we add a repair plane, the lesson is “degrade visibly,” not “skip checks quietly.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150605:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150606:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150608:### Finding 2: Frontmatter corruption is tolerated by omission, which prevents crashes but also suppresses edge validation entirely
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150610:- **What it does**: invalid YAML frontmatter is parsed as `null`; `parseFrontmatter()` also returns `null` on read failure; `checkEdges()` treats missing frontmatter as “no edges,” so corrupted metadata removes one whole checker surface rather than generating a corruption issue.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150611:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex is resilient against malformed markdown, but it conflates “no frontmatter” with “broken frontmatter.” Spec Kit’s current memory pipeline is stronger here because malformed frontmatter is a named hard-blocking quality rule rather than a silent skip ([validate-memory-quality.ts:45](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/validate-memory-quality.ts:45)).
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150612:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150613:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150615:### Finding 3: The repair loop is robust after AI execution, but its process-status semantics are ambiguous under timeout or interrupted sessions
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150617:- **What it does**: `runClaudeInteractive()` treats `status === 0` and `status === null` as success, so a timed-out or otherwise nonstandard child result can be accepted as okay; the safety net is that Mex always reruns drift detection afterward and shows the score delta before asking to continue. In this snapshot, tests cover checkers/parsing/scanner/config/patterns, but there are no dedicated `sync`, `watch`, or interactive `setup` suites under `external/test/`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150618:- **Why it matters for us**: the transferable part is the immediate re-verification step, not the exit handling. If Spec Kit ever adds bounded AI repair, we should keep “verify after every cycle,” but model result states as `success / failed / indeterminate` rather than treating null exit as acceptable.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150619:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150620:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150622:### Finding 4: Mex’s advertised multi-language breadth is real in scanner setup, but not in drift validation or repair evidence
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150624:- **What it does**: the scanner can detect `package.json`, `pyproject.toml`, `go.mod`, and `Cargo.toml`, but only `package.json` gets full dependency/script parsing; non-Node manifests are stubbed for name/version only, and runtime drift checks still center on npm scripts, Make targets, and package.json dependencies.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150625:- **Why it matters for us**: this is a migration trap. Mex looks repo-agnostic at the scaffold level, but the enforcement/control plane is mostly Node-centric. For Spec Kit, any doc-drift layer should either be explicitly JS-first or split into pluggable validators per ecosystem; otherwise we’ll inherit silent under-validation across mixed-language packets.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150626:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150627:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150629:### Finding 5: Scaling is controlled with hard caps and shallow searches, which stabilizes prompts but creates predictable blind spots in larger repos
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150631:- **What it does**: recent diff context is limited to `HEAD~5..HEAD`, fuzzy extension search stops at depth 5, recursive folder counts stop at depth 3, test entry points are capped at 10, and README context is truncated at 3000 characters.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150632:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex is clearly optimized for “small enough prompt, fast enough loop,” not exhaustive truth. That tradeoff is reasonable, but it means scaling behavior is lossy by design. If we add a Spec Kit repair plane, our stronger retrieval stack should decide when to stay bounded and when to escalate to CocoIndex/code-graph rather than relying on fixed caps alone.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150633:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150634:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150636:### Finding 6: Setup and migration rely on heuristics that preserve user edits, but they can also preserve stale scaffolds and misclassify repo maturity
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150638:- **What it does**: config loading blocks obviously incomplete `.mex/` scaffolds, but setup decides `fresh/existing/partial` from placeholder text plus a shallow source-file count; populated scaffold files are skipped if they no longer look templated; existing tool-config destinations are warned-and-skipped rather than reconciled.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150639:- **Why it matters for us**: this is safe for avoiding overwrite damage, but weak for migration correctness. It protects user edits better than it proves scaffold consistency. Spec Kit is already more explicit about follow-up health/repair loops and packet validation, so the lesson is to keep preservation guards while pairing them with a first-class “migration drift” report instead of heuristic skipping alone.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150640:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150641:- **Impact**: medium

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150712:- **What it does**: git freshness and diff helpers return `null` or `""` on failure, command/dependency loaders return `null` on parse failure, and setup falls back from scanner-brief mode to raw filesystem exploration with only a warning. Mex keeps running, but several checks simply stop contributing evidence.

If we add a repair plane, the lesson is “degrade visibly,” not “skip checks quietly.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150714:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150715:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150717:### Finding 2: Frontmatter corruption is tolerated by omission, which prevents crashes but also suppresses edge validation entirely
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150719:- **What it does**: invalid YAML frontmatter is parsed as `null`; `parseFrontmatter()` also returns `null` on read failure; `checkEdges()` treats missing frontmatter as “no edges,” so corrupted metadata removes one whole checker surface rather than generating a corruption issue.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150720:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex is resilient against malformed markdown, but it conflates “no frontmatter” with “broken frontmatter.” Spec Kit’s current memory pipeline is stronger here because malformed frontmatter is a named hard-blocking quality rule rather than a silent skip ([validate-memory-quality.ts:45](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/validate-memory-quality.ts:45)).
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150721:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150722:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150724:### Finding 3: The repair loop is robust after AI execution, but its process-status semantics are ambiguous under timeout or interrupted sessions
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150726:- **What it does**: `runClaudeInteractive()` treats `status === 0` and `status === null` as success, so a timed-out or otherwise nonstandard child result can be accepted as okay; the safety net is that Mex always reruns drift detection afterward and shows the score delta before asking to continue. In this snapshot, tests cover checkers/parsing/scanner/config/patterns, but there are no dedicated `sync`, `watch`, or interactive `setup` suites under `external/test/`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150727:- **Why it matters for us**: the transferable part is the immediate re-verification step, not the exit handling. If Spec Kit ever adds bounded AI repair, we should keep “verify after every cycle,” but model result states as `success / failed / indeterminate` rather than treating null exit as acceptable.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150728:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150729:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150731:### Finding 4: Mex’s advertised multi-language breadth is real in scanner setup, but not in drift validation or repair evidence
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150733:- **What it does**: the scanner can detect `package.json`, `pyproject.toml`, `go.mod`, and `Cargo.toml`, but only `package.json` gets full dependency/script parsing; non-Node manifests are stubbed for name/version only, and runtime drift checks still center on npm scripts, Make targets, and package.json dependencies.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150734:- **Why it matters for us**: this is a migration trap. Mex looks repo-agnostic at the scaffold level, but the enforcement/control plane is mostly Node-centric. For Spec Kit, any doc-drift layer should either be explicitly JS-first or split into pluggable validators per ecosystem; otherwise we’ll inherit silent under-validation across mixed-language packets.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150735:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150736:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150738:### Finding 5: Scaling is controlled with hard caps and shallow searches, which stabilizes prompts but creates predictable blind spots in larger repos
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150740:- **What it does**: recent diff context is limited to `HEAD~5..HEAD`, fuzzy extension search stops at depth 5, recursive folder counts stop at depth 3, test entry points are capped at 10, and README context is truncated at 3000 characters.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150741:- **Why it matters for us**: Mex is clearly optimized for “small enough prompt, fast enough loop,” not exhaustive truth. That tradeoff is reasonable, but it means scaling behavior is lossy by design. If we add a Spec Kit repair plane, our stronger retrieval stack should decide when to stay bounded and when to escalate to CocoIndex/code-graph rather than relying on fixed caps alone.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150742:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150743:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150745:### Finding 6: Setup and migration rely on heuristics that preserve user edits, but they can also preserve stale scaffolds and misclassify repo maturity
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150747:- **What it does**: config loading blocks obviously incomplete `.mex/` scaffolds, but setup decides `fresh/existing/partial` from placeholder text plus a shallow source-file count; populated scaffold files are skipped if they no longer look templated; existing tool-config destinations are warned-and-skipped rather than reconciled.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150748:- **Why it matters for us**: this is safe for avoiding overwrite damage, but weak for migration correctness. It protects user edits better than it proves scaffold consistency. Spec Kit is already more explicit about follow-up health/repair loops and packet validation, so the lesson is to keep preservation guards while pairing them with a first-class “migration drift” report instead of heuristic skipping alone.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150749:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150750:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150875:Research Mex's markdown scaffold and drift detection patterns to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around staleness detection, codebase-knowledge synchronization, and structured project knowledge. Determine which Mex patterns should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150894:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit validation via `validate.sh` with severity-oriented checks, indexed memory files with frontmatter, and a `MEMORY.md`-style documentation/memory model. What it does **not** currently have is an automated drift checker that compares documentation or memory scaffolds against the actual filesystem, commands, dependencies, git staleness, or cross-file contradictions. Research should stay centered on that gap rather than re-covering general memory persistence already addressed by phase `001`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150918:15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every finding must cite exact file paths, describe what Mex actually does, why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, whether it should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, what subsystem it would affect, and what migration or validation risk comes with it. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:150960:`src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts` does not rely on human judgment alone; it flags scaffold files by both elapsed days and commits since last change. A strong finding would explain why combining temporal and repository-activity signals is stronger than date-only freshness, show how that could improve Public's documentation hygiene, and classify the idea as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.

Capture the evidence needed to prove scan summary and updated index state, that spec documents remain indexed in warn-only quality mode, and that scan leases reserve scan_started_at atomically, reject overlapping fresh scans, expire stale crashed-scan leases, and only move to last_index_scan on completion. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:157416:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/04--maintenance/014-workspace-scanning-and-indexing-memory-index-scan.md:20:- Expected signals: Scan summary, updated index state, spec-doc warn-only indexing behavior, atomic lease reservation, rejection wait time, stale-lease expiry, and completion handoff to `last_index_scan`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:157425:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/22--context-preservation-and-code-graph/265-gemini-hooks.md:21:- **Prompt**: `Validate 265 Gemini hooks. Simulate a Gemini SessionStart event with source=startup. Confirm: (1) session-prime.ts reads JSON from stdin with session_id, (2) output is JSON with hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext containing session context, (3) compact-inject.ts performs one-shot injection on BeforeAgent, (4) shared.ts parseGeminiStdin correctly parses Gemini input format, (5) stale cache (>30 min) is rejected.`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:157426:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/22--context-preservation-and-code-graph/265-gemini-hooks.md:28:  - PASS: Session-prime delivers context on startup, compact-inject is one-shot, stdin parsing works, stale cache rejected
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:157502:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/04--maintenance/01-workspace-scanning-and-indexing-memoryindexscan.md:24:In incremental mode (the default), the scanner categorizes every discovered file into one of four buckets: to-index (new files), to-update (changed mtime or content hash), to-skip (unchanged mtime and matching content hash) and to-delete (files that disappeared from disk). The content-hash secondary check catches timestamp-preserving rewrites that would otherwise look unchanged from mtime alone. Batch processing with configurable `BATCH_SIZE` handles large workspaces, but oversized requests are clamped to the hard runtime maximum with a warning instead of exploding fan-out. Scan throttling now uses an atomic lease instead of a check-then-set cooldown gate: `acquireIndexScanLease()` reads `last_index_scan` and `scan_started_at` in one transaction, expires stale leases left behind by crashed scans, reserves a fresh run by writing `scan_started_at`, rejects overlapping fresh scans with `reason: 'lease_active'`, and still enforces `INDEX_SCAN_COOLDOWN` after completed runs with `reason: 'cooldown'`. The handler returns an E429 response with the computed wait time whenever either guard rejects the request.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:157836:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/session-cached-consumer.vitest.ts.test.ts:217:        expected: { status: 'rejected', category: 'freshness', reason: 'stale_summary' },
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158053:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/31-evaluation-benchmark-and-import-policy-tooling.md:33:`check-architecture-boundaries.ts` is the architecture-policy enforcer for two documented gaps. GAP A walks all source files under `shared/` and rejects imports that reach into `mcp_server/` or `scripts/`, whether by relative path, absolute path, or `@spec-kit/*` package prefix. GAP B scans top-level `mcp_server/scripts/*.ts` wrappers and fails anything that is too large, lacks `child_process` usage, or does not delegate to `scripts/dist/`, which keeps wrapper files thin and prevents business logic from drifting into the compatibility layer.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158855:### Finding 1: Static Reference Integrity Is The Cleanest Near-Term Fit
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158857:- **What it does**: Mex extracts typed markdown claims from inline code, code blocks, and bold dependency/version text, then validates referenced paths and frontmatter `edges` against disk. `checkPaths()` resolves both project-root and scaffold-root paths, strips `.mex/`, and recursively searches bare filenames; `checkEdges()` validates `edges[].target`; `parseFrontmatter()` silently returns `null` on bad YAML.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158858:- **Why it matters for us**: This maps directly onto Spec Kit’s markdown-heavy surfaces and is the strongest `adopt now` candidate. Integration difficulty is **medium** because we already have stricter malformed-frontmatter handling via V13 in `validate-memory-quality.ts`, so the main work is building a shared claim-extraction pass for spec docs, memory docs, and catalog docs, then hanging it off `memory_health` or `validate.sh`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158859:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158860:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158862:### Finding 2: Registry And Index Parity Would Help, But Only If Scoped Narrowly
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158864:- **What it does**: Mex cross-checks `patterns/INDEX.md` against actual pattern files, ignoring HTML comments, flagging both missing entries and orphan references. It also auto-appends placeholder index rows when creating new patterns. Spec Kit already does a related but different job in `memory_health`, where it reports divergent alias groups and divergent content hashes for `specs/` vs `.opencode/specs/`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158865:- **Why it matters for us**: The idea is valuable, but Mex assumes one small registry. Spec Kit has multiple authoritative inventories, so a wholesale port would sprawl. Integration difficulty is **medium-high** unless we pilot it on a single surface first, such as packet-local pattern registries or one doc index, rather than trying to reconcile every checklist, catalog, and alias map at once.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158866:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158867:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158869:### Finding 3: Git-Based Freshness Signals Would Integrate Cleanly With Existing Health UX
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158871:- **What it does**: Mex emits staleness issues from two independent signals: days since last change and commits since last change, with warn/error thresholds at `30/90` days and `50/200` commits. Git helpers fail closed to `null`, so missing git evidence suppresses the issue rather than throwing. Spec Kit already has a first-class `fresh/stale/missing` vocabulary and certainty payloads in `session_bootstrap`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158872:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a low-friction enhancement because we already expose stale/degraded state. Integration difficulty is **low-medium**: add per-file freshness computation to `memory_health` or a doc-health mode, then calibrate exclusions for append-only memory files, generated artifacts, and intentionally low-churn docs so the signal stays meaningful.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158873:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158874:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158876:### Finding 4: Command And Dependency Parity Checks Need A Broader Inventory Model Than Mex Has
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158878:- **What it does**: Mex validates `npm run`, `yarn`, `pnpm`, `bun run`, and `make` claims against `package.json` or `Makefile`; checks dependency claims only against `package.json` deps/devDeps; and flags undocumented scripts by substring-searching scaffold text. Its non-Node manifest handling is scanner-only stub logic, not real parity validation.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158879:- **Why it matters for us**: This addresses a real Public pain point because command-surface drift keeps recurring across docs and wrappers. But integration difficulty is **high**: Spec Kit spans TypeScript, Python, shell, MCP commands, wrapper commands, and spec docs, so we would need a shared command/dependency inventory layer and better text matching than Mex’s substring heuristics to avoid noisy false positives.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158880:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158881:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158883:### Finding 5: Drift-To-Repair Briefing Is Promising, But It Should Become A Spec Kit Repair Surface, Not A Mex-Style CLI Loop
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158885:- **What it does**: Mex groups drift issues by file, embeds current file content, nearby filesystem listings, and `HEAD~5..HEAD` diffs into a repair prompt, then reruns drift checks after an interactive Claude session. The briefing logic is good, but failure/degraded-state handling is shallow compared with Spec Kit’s explicit MCP error envelopes, certainty fields, and post-save review gates.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158886:- **Why it matters for us**: The transferable part is the prompt builder, not the interactive loop. Integration difficulty is **medium-high** because Spec Kit should emit packet-local, auditable repair briefs and route them through existing spec workflows, health surfaces, and certainty contracts rather than shelling out to a provider-specific CLI and treating `status === null` as success.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158887:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158888:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158925:Take the two `adopt now` candidates and design the smallest Spec Kit-native shape for each: `memory_health(reportMode="doc_drift")` for static reference integrity, and a per-file freshness supplement for `memory_health` or `session_bootstrap`. After that, test Modus Memory and Mnemosyne specifically on whether they outperform Mex on explicit degraded-state reporting and scale-aware evidence retrieval.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158938:### Finding 1: Static Reference Integrity Is The Cleanest Near-Term Fit
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158940:- **What it does**: Mex extracts typed markdown claims from inline code, code blocks, and bold dependency/version text, then validates referenced paths and frontmatter `edges` against disk. `checkPaths()` resolves both project-root and scaffold-root paths, strips `.mex/`, and recursively searches bare filenames; `checkEdges()` validates `edges[].target`; `parseFrontmatter()` silently returns `null` on bad YAML.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:158941:- **Why it matters for us**: This maps directly onto Spec Kit’s markdown-heavy surfaces and is the strongest `adopt now` candidate. Integration difficulty is **medium** because we already have stricter malformed-frontmatter handling via V13 in `validate-memory-quality.ts`, so the main work is building a shared claim-extraction pass for spec docs, memory docs, and catalog docs, then hanging it off `memory_health` or `validate.sh`.
