/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:79960:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:79990:### Finding 3: Add an inline `memory_remember` intake path instead of requiring file-first saves
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:79992:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed versus current Public surface: Mnemosyne stores a concise memory directly from a string argument. Public’s write path is file-centric: `memory_save` accepts an absolute `filePath`, then runs parsing, quality gates, governance, dedup, and embedding.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:79993:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a genuine missing feature, not just wrapper thinness. We should add an audited inline intake tool that accepts short text plus minimal metadata, materializes canonical markdown internally, and then runs the existing save pipeline. That would preserve Public’s safety while removing the current authoring friction.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:79994:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:79995:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:79997:### Finding 4: Add write-time pinning for always-on memories
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:79999:- **What it does**: Both: Mnemosyne lets an agent set `core=true` at creation time and treats that as always-relevant context. Public guarantees constitutional memories are always surfaced, but creation-time pinning is not a first-class tool parameter; it is encoded via file metadata or later `memory_update`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:80000:- **Why it matters for us**: Public has the semantics, but not the authoring shortcut. A new `pin: "always"` or `priority: "core"` write-time option would reduce friction for durable user preferences and architecture rules while still mapping into existing constitutional or critical tiers.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:80001:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:80002:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:80004:### Finding 5: Build an explicit offline warmup and doctor flow for memory readiness
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:80006:- **What it does**: Both: Mnemosyne documents a binary prerequisite and one-time local model download, and its wrapper returns a clear missing-binary install message. Public already has `memory_health` and startup readiness logs, but it still relies on lazy first-use initialization and manual follow-up when embeddings are not ready.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:80007:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is operationally richer, but it lacks a proactive “make memory ready now” surface. A `memory_warmup` or `memory_doctor` feature that preloads providers, validates embedding profile, primes indices, and returns deterministic ready/not-ready output would remove a lot of first-query uncertainty.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:80008:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:80009:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:80046:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:80093:15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every meaningful finding must cite exact file paths, say whether the evidence is wrapper-confirmed or README-level, explain why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, classify the recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, and identify the affected subsystem. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:80138:- What it does: The README states that Mnemosyne combines SQLite FTS5/BM25 and sqlite-vec cosine similarity with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, while the wrapper simply forwards quoted search queries to `mnemosyne search`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:80139:- Why it matters: Public should not copy README marketing language into implementation plans without deciding whether the value lies in ranking math, wrapper ergonomics, or agent-facing tool guidance.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:80140:- Recommendation: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:80150:- What it does: Mnemosyne appends a compact memory-usage instruction block during `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public's existing compaction plugin injects structural transport context rather than memory-tool reminders.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:80151:- Why it matters: Public may already preserve structural state across compaction, but it does not yet remind the agent of concrete memory tools in the same direct way.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:84439:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/06--analysis/07-learning-history-memorygetlearninghistory.md:24:Handler safety around the backing table is tighter now. Schema initialization is tracked per database instance with `WeakSet<Database>` instead of a process-global boolean, so swapping to a fresh DB connection still creates the `session_learning` table and index when needed. Score validation also rejects `NaN` via `Number.isFinite(...)`, closing the old loophole where `typeof NaN === 'number'` let malformed learning scores pass validation.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:84486:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/04--maintenance/014-workspace-scanning-and-indexing-memory-index-scan.md:19:- Prompt: `Run the incremental scan flow and the targeted atomic lease checks for memory_index_scan. Capture the evidence needed to prove scan summary and updated index state, that spec documents remain indexed in warn-only quality mode, and that scan leases reserve scan_started_at atomically, reject overlapping fresh scans, expire stale crashed-scan leases, and only move to last_index_scan on completion. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:84487:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/04--maintenance/014-workspace-scanning-and-indexing-memory-index-scan.md:31:| EX-014 | Workspace scanning and indexing (memory_index_scan) | Overlapping fresh scans are rejected with an active-lease wait time | `Run the targeted active-lease rejection validation. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | `cd .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server` -> `npm test -- --run tests/db-state.vitest.ts -t "acquires scan lease once and rejects a concurrent fresh lease"` | Second lease attempt is rejected with `reason: 'lease_active'` and a positive wait time | Targeted test transcript showing the second attempt failed for `lease_active` | PASS if the targeted test passes and the rejection path still reports `reason: 'lease_active'` with `waitSeconds > 0` | Inspect `core/db-state.ts` lease-activity branch and `handlers/memory-index.ts` E429 details if concurrent scans start slipping through |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:84544:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/13--memory-quality-and-indexing/042-spec-folder-description-discovery-pi-b3.md:21:- Pass/fail: PASS: description.json created, stale detection works, per-folder preferred, mixed aggregation correct, no crash on corrupt description.json, invalid metadata repaired on regeneration, missing files fall back without implicit writes, traversal attempts rejected, frontmatter stripping works for CRLF-heavy files, folder routing active, and regenerated files are valid JSON with no leftover temp files; FAIL: Any of the scenario checks fails
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:84545:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/13--memory-quality-and-indexing/042-spec-folder-description-discovery-pi-b3.md:29:| 042 | Spec folder description discovery (PI-B3) | Confirm per-folder + aggregated routing | `Validate PI-B3 folder description discovery. Capture the evidence needed to prove description.json exists after create.sh; stale detection triggers on spec.md edit; per-folder files preferred over spec.md fallback; mixed-mode aggregation works; invalid JSON or schema-invalid description.json files are ignored, spec.md fallback is used, and existing files are repaired in place; missing description.json falls back cleanly without implicit backfill; out-of-base or prefix-bypass paths are rejected by realpath containment checks; YAML frontmatter is stripped before description extraction, including CRLF-heavy frontmatter cases; memory_context uses folder routing; regeneration leaves valid JSON on disk with no leftover temp files. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) Create spec folder via create.sh → verify description.json exists 2) Edit spec.md → verify isPerFolderDescriptionStale detects change 3) Run generateFolderDescriptions → verify per-folder files preferred over spec.md 4) Mixed mode: some folders with/without description.json → verify aggregation 5) Corrupt description.json with invalid JSON and schema-invalid field types → run generateFolderDescriptions() and verify spec.md fallback plus repaired description.json 6) Verify missing description.json falls back to spec.md without forcing a write 7) Attempt generation against an out-of-base or prefix-bypass path → verify rejection and no file written 8) Use spec.md with large YAML frontmatter and CRLF-heavy line endings → verify extracted description comes from post-frontmatter content 9) Run memory_context query → verify short-circuit folder routing | description.json exists after create.sh; stale detection triggers on spec.md edit; per-folder files preferred over spec.md fallback; mixed-mode aggregation works; invalid JSON or schema-invalid description.json files are ignored, spec.md fallback is used, and existing files are repaired in place; missing description.json falls back cleanly without implicit backfill; out-of-base or prefix-bypass paths are rejected by realpath containment checks; YAML frontmatter is stripped before description extraction, including CRLF-heavy frontmatter cases; memory_context uses folder routing; regeneration leaves valid JSON on disk with no leftover temp files | description.json content + stale detection output + folder routing trace in memory_context + recovery evidence for corrupt/schema-invalid description.json files + missing-file fallback evidence + rejection evidence for traversal attempts + proof of valid regenerated JSON with no leftover temp files | PASS: description.json created, stale detection works, per-folder preferred, mixed aggregation correct, no crash on corrupt description.json, invalid metadata repaired on regeneration, missing files fall back without implicit writes, traversal attempts rejected, frontmatter stripping works for CRLF-heavy files, folder routing active, and regenerated files are valid JSON with no leftover temp files; FAIL: Any of the scenario checks fails | Verify create.sh generates description.json → Check stale detection mtime comparison → Inspect generateFolderDescriptions preference logic and repair path → Confirm missing-file fallback does not backfill unexpectedly → Verify realpath containment rejects traversal/prefix-bypass paths → Confirm frontmatter stripping happens before description extraction → Verify memory_context folder routing |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:84596:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/32-shared-script-libraries-and-utilities.md:29:The rest of `lib/` provides policy and compatibility helpers. `validate-memory-quality.ts` is the pure post-render quality gate with explicit validation-rule metadata, write-vs-index dispositions, contamination handling, YAML parsing, and source-aware blocking behavior. `memory-frontmatter.ts` strips markdown noise from titles, rejects legacy generic descriptions and trigger phrases, derives higher-quality descriptions, and regenerates trigger phrases from summary text plus spec-folder tokens. `phase-classifier.ts` groups exchanges into topic clusters and conversation phases such as Research, Planning, Implementation, Debugging, and Verification. The shell helpers keep the Bash workflows thin: `shell-common.sh` provides JSON escaping and repo-root detection, while `template-utils.sh` maps documentation levels to template directories and copies files with level-specific fallback behavior. `embeddings.ts` is intentionally only a re-export shim to the canonical shared embeddings module.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:84648:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/04--maintenance/01-workspace-scanning-and-indexing-memoryindexscan.md:24:In incremental mode (the default), the scanner categorizes every discovered file into one of four buckets: to-index (new files), to-update (changed mtime or content hash), to-skip (unchanged mtime and matching content hash) and to-delete (files that disappeared from disk). The content-hash secondary check catches timestamp-preserving rewrites that would otherwise look unchanged from mtime alone. Batch processing with configurable `BATCH_SIZE` handles large workspaces, but oversized requests are clamped to the hard runtime maximum with a warning instead of exploding fan-out. Scan throttling now uses an atomic lease instead of a check-then-set cooldown gate: `acquireIndexScanLease()` reads `last_index_scan` and `scan_started_at` in one transaction, expires stale leases left behind by crashed scans, reserves a fresh run by writing `scan_started_at`, rejects overlapping fresh scans with `reason: 'lease_active'`, and still enforces `INDEX_SCAN_COOLDOWN` after completed runs with `reason: 'cooldown'`. The handler returns an E429 response with the computed wait time whenever either guard rejects the request.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:84670:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog_in_simple_terms.md:692:The quality gate normally rejects memories that are too short, but some types of content are naturally brief. A decision record might be only a few sentences but still be extremely valuable. This feature recognizes that short documents with strong structural signals (like a clear title and proper labels) are worth keeping, so they are not falsely rejected by the length check.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:84759:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:3883:The shipped session-capture pipeline enforces crypto session IDs, atomic batch writes with rollback, contamination filtering, quality abort thresholds, alignment blocking, and configurable pipeline constants. Structured `--stdin` / `--json` input is the only save path; direct positional saves exit non-zero with migration guidance. A semantic sufficiency gate rejects aligned but under-evidenced saves with `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT`. Rendered memory files preserve ANCHOR comments through post-render cleanup, render session-specific trigger phrases, and accept both camelCase and snake_case save contracts. The shared rendered-memory template contract validates structural output before write/index so routine saves stay structurally clean. Decision confidence, truncated outcome handling, and `git_changed_file_count` follow stable priority chains that respect explicit input values.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:84802:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/11--scoring-and-calibration/198-temporal-structural-coherence-scoring.md:29:| 198 | Temporal-structural coherence scoring | Confirm structural penalties, bounded coherence deductions, and immediate retry behavior in the quality loop | `Verify temporal-structural coherence scoring in the quality loop using one well-formed control memory and flawed variants. Submit variants that are too short or missing headings, include a future-dated completion claim, and include self-referential or unresolved causal links. Confirm the coherence breakdown applies the expected structural checks and bounded penalties, and verify that low-coherence content is rejected or downgraded while the quality loop retry cycle stays immediate and bounded. Capture the score breakdown and return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) Prepare a well-formed control memory plus flawed variants covering short or heading-less content, future-dated completion claims, and broken causal-link metadata 2) Run the quality loop or ingest path for each case 3) Inspect the coherence score breakdown and quality-loop decision for control vs flawed variants 4) Confirm penalties apply to temporal and causal-link issues without inventing broader chronology logic 5) For an auto-fixable case, capture the retry cycle and confirm retries occur immediately with no backoff and stop within the configured limit | Structural checks evaluate content presence, minimum length, and headings; future-dated completion claims reduce coherence; self-referential or unresolved causal links reduce coherence; low-coherence variants are rejected or downgraded; retry attempts are immediate and bounded | Quality-loop transcript, coherence score breakdown, control-vs-variant results, and retry timing evidence | PASS: coherence penalties appear for the flawed variants, low-quality cases are blocked or downgraded, and retry timing stays immediate and bounded; FAIL: penalties are missing, weak content passes without explanation, or retries show unexpected backoff or unbounded looping | Verify control content satisfies structural checks -> Inspect coherence penalty branches for future-dated claims and causal links -> Confirm rejection threshold or downgrade path -> Check retry loop configuration and attempt count -> Ensure broader chronology analysis was not assumed in the verdict |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:84827:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/18--ux-hooks/107-checkpoint-confirmname-and-schema-enforcement.md:29:| 107 | Checkpoint confirmName and schema enforcement | Confirm delete safety is required across handler and validation layers | `Validate checkpoint delete confirmName enforcement across handler and schema layers. Capture the evidence needed to prove Validation and handler suites pass with missing-confirmName rejection plus successful delete confirmation reporting, and context-server Group 13b structural tests pass. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) `npx vitest run tests/handler-checkpoints.vitest.ts tests/tool-input-schema.vitest.ts tests/mcp-input-validation.vitest.ts` 2) `npx vitest run tests/context-server.vitest.ts` (Group 13b: T103–T106 structural source-code pattern verification) 3) inspect rejection assertions for missing `confirmName` 4) inspect success assertions for `safetyConfirmationUsed=true` | Validation and handler suites pass with missing-`confirmName` rejection plus successful delete confirmation reporting. `context-server.vitest.ts` Group 13b structural tests (T103–T106) verify source-code patterns for checkpoint confirmName enforcement | Test transcript + assertion snippets | PASS if the three suites plus `context-server.vitest.ts` Group 13b pass and prove required `confirmName` enforcement end to end | Inspect checkpoint handler, schemas, tool typing alignment, and context-server structural test expectations |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:84870:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/22--context-preservation-and-code-graph/254-code-graph-scan-query.md:47:| 254e | Code graph storage and query | Symlink boundary validation | `Validate 254e symlink boundary enforcement` | `cd .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server && npx vitest run tests/unit-path-security.vitest.ts tests/code-graph-scan.vitest.ts` | `code_graph_scan` canonicalizes `rootDir`, rejects broken or escaping symlinks, and does not index files outside the project root | Vitest output showing symlink escape rejection and scan boundary enforcement | PASS if symlinked external files are NOT indexed and escaping paths are rejected | Check `handlers/code-graph/scan.ts` realpath-based boundary check and `tests/unit-path-security.vitest.ts` for escape scenarios |

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86695:### Finding 1: Refactor compaction guidance into the existing shared transport pipeline instead of adding a second Mnemosyne-style memory hook
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86697:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed on Mnemosyne: compaction always injects a static memory-tool usage card. Public already has compaction-specific memory surfacing and transport injection, but its OpenCode compaction block is generic continuity payload text rather than explicit memory-tool instructions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86698:- **Why it matters for us**: The gap is not missing compaction infrastructure; it is split messaging. The right change is to enrich the existing shared compaction payload with concise memory actions and routing cues, not bolt on a parallel plugin-local instruction block.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86699:- **Recommendation**: adopt now

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86702:### Finding 2: Reframe “memory warmup/doctor” as a refactor over existing readiness primitives, not a net-new subsystem
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86704:- **What it does**: Both: Mnemosyne exposes prerequisite friction plainly. Public already has `waitForEmbeddingModel`, degraded `memory_health`, startup-scan gating, and a CLI `--eager-warmup` path, but those behaviors are scattered and partially hidden behind lazy-init semantics.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86705:- **Why it matters for us**: Earlier “new feature” thinking is too big. Public should consolidate these existing pieces into a single deterministic readiness surface that runs health, warmup, and optional scan in one place.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86706:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86707:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86709:### Finding 3: If we add scoped memory ergonomics, implement them as aliases over governed scope metadata, not as separate project/global stores
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86711:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed on Mnemosyne: “project” is a directory-basename collection and “global” is a separate collection. Public already centers one governed store with normalized tenant/user/agent/shared-space scope, while `retrievalLevel: "global"` means community fallback, not personal-memory scope.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86712:- **Why it matters for us**: Copying Mnemosyne’s two-store topology would fight Public’s governance model and risks namespace collisions. The correct direction is a thin UX facade like `scope: "project|personal|shared"` that compiles into current scope metadata.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86713:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86714:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86716:### Finding 4: Expose write-time pinning through the current save/update surface instead of overloading tiers or inventing a new “core” path
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86718:- **What it does**: Both: Mnemosyne surfaces `core=true` at write time. Public already has latent pinned-memory semantics in storage and ranking (`is_pinned` bypasses decay), but `memory_save` and `memory_update` do not expose pinning in the tool contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86719:- **Why it matters for us**: This is less a new feature than a buried one. Public can keep constitutional as a content-governance tier while exposing `pin` as an operational retrieval flag for always-on memories.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86720:- **Recommendation**: adopt now

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86723:### Finding 5: Do not move toward AGENTS.md-dependent memory discipline; keep memory awareness runtime-owned
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86725:- **What it does**: README-documented on Mnemosyne: proactive memory behavior is recommended via manual `AGENTS.md` instructions. Public already injects tool routing, session recovery, and auto-prime guidance from the runtime itself.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86726:- **Why it matters for us**: Public should not regress toward doc-dependent behavior. If we want Mnemosyne’s proactive recall pattern, the right place is runtime-owned prime/compaction payloads so it works consistently across sessions and runtimes.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86727:- **Recommendation**: reject

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86730:### Finding 6: Reject any architectural pivot from MCP-first memory to a thin OpenCode binary wrapper; if desired, add a facade on top of the current server
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86732:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed on Mnemosyne: the repo is a thin OpenCode plugin around a single binary with five tools. Public is already an MCP memory platform with retrieval, health, bootstrap, governance, and analysis surfaces.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86733:- **Why it matters for us**: Mnemosyne’s transferable value is UX thinness, not backend architecture. Replacing Spec Kit Memory with a binary-wrapper shape would collapse tool richness and governance. A better direction is an optional OpenCode facade that calls the existing MCP server.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86790:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86791:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86793:### Finding 3: If we add scoped memory ergonomics, implement them as aliases over governed scope metadata, not as separate project/global stores
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86795:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed on Mnemosyne: “project” is a directory-basename collection and “global” is a separate collection. Public already centers one governed store with normalized tenant/user/agent/shared-space scope, while `retrievalLevel: "global"` means community fallback, not personal-memory scope.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86796:- **Why it matters for us**: Copying Mnemosyne’s two-store topology would fight Public’s governance model and risks namespace collisions. The correct direction is a thin UX facade like `scope: "project|personal|shared"` that compiles into current scope metadata.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86797:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86798:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86800:### Finding 4: Expose write-time pinning through the current save/update surface instead of overloading tiers or inventing a new “core” path
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86802:- **What it does**: Both: Mnemosyne surfaces `core=true` at write time. Public already has latent pinned-memory semantics in storage and ranking (`is_pinned` bypasses decay), but `memory_save` and `memory_update` do not expose pinning in the tool contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86803:- **Why it matters for us**: This is less a new feature than a buried one. Public can keep constitutional as a content-governance tier while exposing `pin` as an operational retrieval flag for always-on memories.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86804:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86805:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86807:### Finding 5: Do not move toward AGENTS.md-dependent memory discipline; keep memory awareness runtime-owned
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86809:- **What it does**: README-documented on Mnemosyne: proactive memory behavior is recommended via manual `AGENTS.md` instructions. Public already injects tool routing, session recovery, and auto-prime guidance from the runtime itself.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86810:- **Why it matters for us**: Public should not regress toward doc-dependent behavior. If we want Mnemosyne’s proactive recall pattern, the right place is runtime-owned prime/compaction payloads so it works consistently across sessions and runtimes.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86811:- **Recommendation**: reject

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86814:### Finding 6: Reject any architectural pivot from MCP-first memory to a thin OpenCode binary wrapper; if desired, add a facade on top of the current server
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86816:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed on Mnemosyne: the repo is a thin OpenCode plugin around a single binary with five tools. Public is already an MCP memory platform with retrieval, health, bootstrap, governance, and analysis surfaces.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:86817:- **Why it matters for us**: Mnemosyne’s transferable value is UX thinness, not backend architecture. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:91164:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/memory-search-ux-hooks.vitest.ts:180:  it('rejects continuation cursors when the resuming scope does not match the original query scope', async () => {
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:91186:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/session-manager-extended.vitest.ts:296:    it('rejects scope mismatches for bound sessions', () => {
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:91215:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/memory-parser.vitest.ts:287:    it('T500-09c: overlapping anchors are rejected', () => {
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:92258:44:  inputSchema: { type: 'object', additionalProperties: false, properties: { input: { type: 'string', minLength: 1, description: 'The query, prompt, or context description (required)' }, mode: { type: 'string', enum: ['auto', 'quick', 'deep', 'focused', 'resume'], default: 'auto', description: 'Context retrieval mode: auto (detect intent), quick (fast triggers), deep (comprehensive search), focused (intent-optimized), resume (session recovery)' }, intent: { type: 'string', enum: ['add_feature', 'fix_bug', 'refactor', 'security_audit', 'understand', 'find_spec', 'find_decision'], description: 'Explicit task intent. If not provided and mode=auto, intent is auto-detected from input.' }, specFolder: { type: 'string', description: 'Limit context to specific spec folder' }, tenantId: { type: 'string', description: 'Tenant boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, userId: { type: 'string', description: 'User boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, agentId: { type: 'string', description: 'Agent boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, sharedSpaceId: { type: 'string', description: 'Shared-space boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, limit: { type: 'number', minimum: 1, maximum: 100, description: 'Maximum results (mode-specific defaults apply)' }, sessionId: { type: 'string', description: 'Optional server-issued session identifier for working-memory continuity. When provided, it must match an existing server-managed session or the call is rejected. Omit it to let the server generate a new session for this request.' }, enableDedup: { type: 'boolean', default: true, description: 'Enable session deduplication' }, includeContent: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Include full file content in results' }, includeTrace: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Include provenance-rich trace data (scores, source, trace) in results when underlying memory_search is called' }, tokenUsage: { type: 'number', minimum: 0.0, maximum: 1.0, description: "Optional caller token usage ratio (0.0-1.0)" }, anchors: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'Filter content to specific anchors (e.g., ["state", "next-steps"] for resume mode)' }, profile: { type: 'string', enum: ['quick', 'research', 'resume', 'debug'], description: 'Optional response profile formatter. Returns a reduced or mode-aware response shape when profile formatting is enabled.' } }, required: ['input'] },
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93001:   171	 * When enabled without scope constraints in the query, all results are rejected
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93441:### Finding 1: Shift compaction from passive state carryover to runtime-owned behavioral continuity
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93443:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne hard-codes a memory-usage instruction block into `experimental.session.compacting`, telling the agent when to recall, store, delete, use `core`, and use global memory. Public already has transport-level compaction notes and prime packages, and Claude/Gemini already inject explicit recovery instructions, but OpenCode transport is still marked `retrievalPolicyOwner: 'runtime'` and only ships continuity payloads, not explicit memory-action guidance.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93444:- **Why it matters for us**: The paradigm shift is that compaction should preserve not just facts, but the agent’s operating model for memory. Public already has the infrastructure; the gap is OpenCode-specific behavioral guidance, not a missing compaction system.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93445:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93446:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93448:### Finding 2: Shift retrieval strategy from hidden internals to explicit agent-callable API surface
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93450:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne exposes strategy through tool names: project recall vs global recall. Public’s handler already supports `retrievalLevel: 'local' | 'global' | 'auto'`, community fallback, and logs `fusionMethod: 'rrf'`, while the auto-prime guidance tells agents to use `retrievalLevel`. But the published `memory_search` schema still describes vector search and does not expose `retrievalLevel`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93451:- **Why it matters for us**: This suggests a paradigm shift from “smart backend, thin docs” to “strategy is part of the public contract.” If the runtime tells agents to use a retrieval posture, that posture needs to exist in the surfaced tool contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93452:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93453:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93455:### Finding 3: Split memory policy into orthogonal axes instead of overloading tiers
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93457:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s `core` is a write-time operational flag. Public already has a separate operational dimension in ranking: `is_pinned = 1` bypasses decay and preserves raw importance weight. But Public’s save/update contracts expose tier and weight, not pinning.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93458:- **Why it matters for us**: The architecture should separate three concerns: governance/content tier (`constitutional`, `critical`), operational continuity (`pin`), and scope/visibility (`tenant`, `shared`, project alias). Mnemosyne’s simpler model makes that separation easier to see.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93459:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93460:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93462:### Finding 4: Move from one universal memory surface to runtime-specific facades over a governed core
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93464:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne is a wrapper-thin OpenCode plugin around one binary and five tools. Public is already a memory platform with retrieval, governance, health, bootstrap, and routing. The OpenCode transport layer already implies a separation between core payload production and runtime-specific delivery.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93465:- **Why it matters for us**: The paradigm shift is not “replace MCP with a thin wrapper,” but “treat runtime UX as a facade layer.” Public can keep the rich governed core and add a smaller OpenCode-optimized surface where that ergonomics matters.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93466:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93467:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93469:### Finding 5: Treat memory retrieval as one retrieval plane in a federated stack, not the universal semantic engine
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93471:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s hybrid BM25+vector story is still a memory-store story. Public already has a federated model: `memory_search` for memories, CocoIndex for semantic code intent, and code graph for structural questions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93472:- **Why it matters for us**: The architecture signal here is to keep planes distinct and route by task intent, not merge every embedding-backed system into one “hybrid retrieval” feature. Mnemosyne reinforces the value of a clear memory plane, but Public’s broader federation is the stronger design.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93473:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93474:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93476:### Finding 6: Make offline backend readiness a first-class memory concern, not a hidden first-use side effect
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93478:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s README documents a one-time model download of about 500 MB, and the wrapper handles missing binaries with a string fallback. Its project auto-init is best-effort and does not inspect the `init` exit status before logging success. Public, by contrast, already models readiness and recovery through `session_bootstrap`, structural status, and explicit next actions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93479:- **Why it matters for us**: The paradigm shift is to treat offline model/backend warmup as architecture, not ops trivia. If Public ever adds heavier local inference backends, readiness, degraded mode, and repair guidance should stay first-class surfaces.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93480:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93481:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93521:### Finding 1: Shift compaction from passive state carryover to runtime-owned behavioral continuity

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93596:- **Why it matters for us**: This suggests a paradigm shift from “smart backend, thin docs” to “strategy is part of the public contract.” If the runtime tells agents to use a retrieval posture, that posture needs to exist in the surfaced tool contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93597:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93598:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93600:### Finding 3: Split memory policy into orthogonal axes instead of overloading tiers
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93602:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s `core` is a write-time operational flag. Public already has a separate operational dimension in ranking: `is_pinned = 1` bypasses decay and preserves raw importance weight. But Public’s save/update contracts expose tier and weight, not pinning.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93603:- **Why it matters for us**: The architecture should separate three concerns: governance/content tier (`constitutional`, `critical`), operational continuity (`pin`), and scope/visibility (`tenant`, `shared`, project alias). Mnemosyne’s simpler model makes that separation easier to see.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93604:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93605:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93607:### Finding 4: Move from one universal memory surface to runtime-specific facades over a governed core
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93609:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne is a wrapper-thin OpenCode plugin around one binary and five tools. Public is already a memory platform with retrieval, governance, health, bootstrap, and routing. The OpenCode transport layer already implies a separation between core payload production and runtime-specific delivery.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93610:- **Why it matters for us**: The paradigm shift is not “replace MCP with a thin wrapper,” but “treat runtime UX as a facade layer.” Public can keep the rich governed core and add a smaller OpenCode-optimized surface where that ergonomics matters.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93611:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93612:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93614:### Finding 5: Treat memory retrieval as one retrieval plane in a federated stack, not the universal semantic engine
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93616:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s hybrid BM25+vector story is still a memory-store story. Public already has a federated model: `memory_search` for memories, CocoIndex for semantic code intent, and code graph for structural questions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93617:- **Why it matters for us**: The architecture signal here is to keep planes distinct and route by task intent, not merge every embedding-backed system into one “hybrid retrieval” feature. Mnemosyne reinforces the value of a clear memory plane, but Public’s broader federation is the stronger design.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93618:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93619:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93621:### Finding 6: Make offline backend readiness a first-class memory concern, not a hidden first-use side effect
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93623:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s README documents a one-time model download of about 500 MB, and the wrapper handles missing binaries with a string fallback. Its project auto-init is best-effort and does not inspect the `init` exit status before logging success. Public, by contrast, already models readiness and recovery through `session_bootstrap`, structural status, and explicit next actions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93624:- **Why it matters for us**: The paradigm shift is to treat offline model/backend warmup as architecture, not ops trivia. If Public ever adds heavier local inference backends, readiness, degraded mode, and repair guidance should stay first-class surfaces.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93625:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93626:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93666:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93713:15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every meaningful finding must cite exact file paths, say whether the evidence is wrapper-confirmed or README-level, explain why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, classify the recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, and identify the affected subsystem. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93758:- What it does: The README states that Mnemosyne combines SQLite FTS5/BM25 and sqlite-vec cosine similarity with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, while the wrapper simply forwards quoted search queries to `mnemosyne search`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93759:- Why it matters: Public should not copy README marketing language into implementation plans without deciding whether the value lies in ranking math, wrapper ergonomics, or agent-facing tool guidance.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93760:- Recommendation: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93770:- What it does: Mnemosyne appends a compact memory-usage instruction block during `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public's existing compaction plugin injects structural transport context rather than memory-tool reminders.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93771:- Why it matters: Public may already preserve structural state across compaction, but it does not yet remind the agent of concrete memory tools in the same direct way.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93772:- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on desired runtime surface
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:93816:- each finding labeled `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
