/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98425:### Finding 1: The strongest pattern is compaction-time preservation of memory behavior, not memory content
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98428:- **What it does**: the wrapper’s `experimental.session.compacting` hook appends a plain-language instruction block listing the five memory tools and exactly when to search, store, delete, use `core`, and use `global`. No retrieval happens here; it restores agent posture after compression.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98429:- **Why it matters for us**: this is more transferable than Mnemosyne’s ranking story. It changes agent behavior at the moment context is lost, which is the highest-leverage seam in OpenCode.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98430:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98431:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98433:### Finding 2: Mnemosyne turns memory use into a lifecycle contract by repeating the same policy at startup and compaction
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98436:- **What it does**: the README recommends putting the same recall/store/delete/global/core rules in `AGENTS.md`, while the compaction hook re-injects nearly the same operating model after context resets.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98437:- **Why it matters for us**: the insight is not “write better docs.” It is “surface retrieval posture as runtime contract.” Public already has rich recovery tools, but OpenCode currently does not restate that posture as directly during compaction.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98438:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98439:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98441:### Finding 3: The hook compresses scope policy into a few agent-facing verbs, which makes it usable under pressure
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98444:- **What it does**: project scope is derived from the working directory basename, `global` is a separate write/read path, and `core` is expressed as a simple tag. The compaction text teaches those choices in human terms rather than schema terms.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98445:- **Why it matters for us**: Public’s governed core is richer, but the runtime facade can still expose simpler decision verbs such as `retrievalLevel`, `pin`, and “resume vs search” without flattening the backend model.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98446:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98447:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98449:### Finding 4: Public already preserves continuity across compaction, but its OpenCode surface intentionally avoids owning retrieval policy
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98452:- **What it does**: Public builds an OpenCode transport plan from shared payloads, labels `retrievalPolicyOwner: 'runtime'`, and injects continuity notes during compaction. Claude and Gemini also recover cached compact briefs plus a `memory_context(...resume...)` instruction.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98453:- **Why it matters for us**: the gap is narrow and specific. We do not lack compaction support. We lack Mnemosyne-style, OpenCode-facing memory-behavior guidance on top of the continuity payload.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98454:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98455:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98457:### Finding 5: We can borrow the strongest pattern without borrowing Mnemosyne’s backend architecture
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98460:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s wrapper is just a Bun-spawned CLI bridge plus hook text; the hybrid BM25/vector/RRF implementation is only README-documented here. Public already has hybrid retrieval, `retrievalLevel` local/global/auto, community fallback, and internal `is_pinned` ranking behavior, but those operational controls are not surfaced as clearly in the OpenCode-facing memory contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98461:- **Why it matters for us**: the right move is not backend replacement. It is adding a thin OpenCode memory-policy facade over the governed core we already have.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98462:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98463:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98508:### Finding 1: The strongest pattern is compaction-time preservation of memory behavior, not memory content
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98511:- **What it does**: the wrapper’s `experimental.session.compacting` hook appends a plain-language instruction block listing the five memory tools and exactly when to search, store, delete, use `core`, and use `global`. No retrieval happens here; it restores agent posture after compression.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98512:- **Why it matters for us**: this is more transferable than Mnemosyne’s ranking story. It changes agent behavior at the moment context is lost, which is the highest-leverage seam in OpenCode.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98513:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98514:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98516:### Finding 2: Mnemosyne turns memory use into a lifecycle contract by repeating the same policy at startup and compaction
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98519:- **What it does**: the README recommends putting the same recall/store/delete/global/core rules in `AGENTS.md`, while the compaction hook re-injects nearly the same operating model after context resets.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98520:- **Why it matters for us**: the insight is not “write better docs.” It is “surface retrieval posture as runtime contract.” Public already has rich recovery tools, but OpenCode currently does not restate that posture as directly during compaction.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98521:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98522:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98524:### Finding 3: The hook compresses scope policy into a few agent-facing verbs, which makes it usable under pressure
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98527:- **What it does**: project scope is derived from the working directory basename, `global` is a separate write/read path, and `core` is expressed as a simple tag. The compaction text teaches those choices in human terms rather than schema terms.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98528:- **Why it matters for us**: Public’s governed core is richer, but the runtime facade can still expose simpler decision verbs such as `retrievalLevel`, `pin`, and “resume vs search” without flattening the backend model.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98529:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98530:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98532:### Finding 4: Public already preserves continuity across compaction, but its OpenCode surface intentionally avoids owning retrieval policy
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98535:- **What it does**: Public builds an OpenCode transport plan from shared payloads, labels `retrievalPolicyOwner: 'runtime'`, and injects continuity notes during compaction. Claude and Gemini also recover cached compact briefs plus a `memory_context(...resume...)` instruction.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98536:- **Why it matters for us**: the gap is narrow and specific. We do not lack compaction support. We lack Mnemosyne-style, OpenCode-facing memory-behavior guidance on top of the continuity payload.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98537:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98538:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98540:### Finding 5: We can borrow the strongest pattern without borrowing Mnemosyne’s backend architecture
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98543:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s wrapper is just a Bun-spawned CLI bridge plus hook text; the hybrid BM25/vector/RRF implementation is only README-documented here. Public already has hybrid retrieval, `retrievalLevel` local/global/auto, community fallback, and internal `is_pinned` ranking behavior, but those operational controls are not surfaced as clearly in the OpenCode-facing memory contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98544:- **Why it matters for us**: the right move is not backend replacement. It is adding a thin OpenCode memory-policy facade over the governed core we already have.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98545:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98546:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98577:### Finding 5: Treat memory retrieval as one retrieval plane in a federated stack, not the universal semantic engine
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98579:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s hybrid BM25+vector story is still a memory-store story. Public already has a federated model: `memory_search` for memories, CocoIndex for semantic code intent, and code graph for structural questions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98580:- **Why it matters for us**: The architecture signal here is to keep planes distinct and route by task intent, not merge every embedding-backed system into one “hybrid retrieval” feature. Mnemosyne reinforces the value of a clear memory plane, but Public’s broader federation is the stronger design.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98581:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98582:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98584:### Finding 6: Make offline backend readiness a first-class memory concern, not a hidden first-use side effect
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98586:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s README documents a one-time model download of about 500 MB, and the wrapper handles missing binaries with a string fallback. Its project auto-init is best-effort and does not inspect the `init` exit status before logging success. Public, by contrast, already models readiness and recovery through `session_bootstrap`, structural status, and explicit next actions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98587:- **Why it matters for us**: The paradigm shift is to treat offline model/backend warmup as architecture, not ops trivia. If Public ever adds heavier local inference backends, readiness, degraded mode, and repair guidance should stay first-class surfaces.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98588:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98589:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98623:### Finding N: [Title]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98625:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98626:- **Why it matters for us**: concrete relevance
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98627:- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98628:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98710:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98757:15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every meaningful finding must cite exact file paths, say whether the evidence is wrapper-confirmed or README-level, explain why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, classify the recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, and identify the affected subsystem. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98802:- What it does: The README states that Mnemosyne combines SQLite FTS5/BM25 and sqlite-vec cosine similarity with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, while the wrapper simply forwards quoted search queries to `mnemosyne search`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98803:- Why it matters: Public should not copy README marketing language into implementation plans without deciding whether the value lies in ranking math, wrapper ergonomics, or agent-facing tool guidance.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98804:- Recommendation: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98814:- What it does: Mnemosyne appends a compact memory-usage instruction block during `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public's existing compaction plugin injects structural transport context rather than memory-tool reminders.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98815:- Why it matters: Public may already preserve structural state across compaction, but it does not yet remind the agent of concrete memory tools in the same direct way.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98816:- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on desired runtime surface
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98860:- each finding labeled `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98915:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98916:- **Why it matters for us**: concrete relevance

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98918:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:99384:   180	- symptom: valid manual trigger phrase with numeric prefix disappears and causes `PSR-2`; cause: broad `^\\d{1,3}\\s` rejection rule; fix: apply numeric-prefix rejection only when phrase remains short (`<=3` words) [Task 1]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:99433:     3	- **Why it matters for us**: This suggests a paradigm shift from “smart backend, thin docs” to “strategy is part of the public contract.” If the runtime tells agents to use a retrieval posture, that posture needs to exist in the surfaced tool contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:99434:     4	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:99435:     5	- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:99439:     9	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s `core` is a write-time operational flag. Public already has a separate operational dimension in ranking: `is_pinned = 1` bypasses decay and preserves raw importance weight. But Public’s save/update contracts expose tier and weight, not pinning.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:99440:    10	- **Why it matters for us**: The architecture should separate three concerns: governance/content tier (`constitutional`, `critical`), operational continuity (`pin`), and scope/visibility (`tenant`, `shared`, project alias). Mnemosyne’s simpler model makes that separation easier to see.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:99441:    11	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:99442:    12	- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:99446:    16	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne is a wrapper-thin OpenCode plugin around one binary and five tools. Public is already a memory platform with retrieval, governance, health, bootstrap, and routing. The OpenCode transport layer already implies a separation between core payload production and runtime-specific delivery.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:99447:    17	- **Why it matters for us**: The paradigm shift is not “replace MCP with a thin wrapper,” but “treat runtime UX as a facade layer.” Public can keep the rich governed core and add a smaller OpenCode-optimized surface where that ergonomics matters.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:99448:    18	- **Recommendation**: prototype later

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:99596:   166	- Why it matters: Public should not copy README marketing language into implementation plans without deciding whether the value lies in ranking math, wrapper ergonomics, or agent-facing tool guidance.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:99597:   167	- Recommendation: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:99607:   177	- What it does: Mnemosyne appends a compact memory-usage instruction block during `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public's existing compaction plugin injects structural transport context rather than memory-tool reminders.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:99608:   178	- Why it matters: Public may already preserve structural state across compaction, but it does not yet remind the agent of concrete memory tools in the same direct way.

If not provided and mode=auto, intent is auto-detected from input.' }, specFolder: { type: 'string', description: 'Limit context to specific spec folder' }, tenantId: { type: 'string', description: 'Tenant boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, userId: { type: 'string', description: 'User boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, agentId: { type: 'string', description: 'Agent boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, sharedSpaceId: { type: 'string', description: 'Shared-space boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, limit: { type: 'number', minimum: 1, maximum: 100, description: 'Maximum results (mode-specific defaults apply)' }, sessionId: { type: 'string', description: 'Optional server-issued session identifier for working-memory continuity. If not provided and mode=auto, intent is auto-detected from input.' }, specFolder: { type: 'string', description: 'Limit context to specific spec folder' }, tenantId: { type: 'string', description: 'Tenant boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, userId: { type: 'string', description: 'User boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, agentId: { type: 'string', description: 'Agent boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, sharedSpaceId: { type: 'string', description: 'Shared-space boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, limit: { type: 'number', minimum: 1, maximum: 100, description: 'Maximum results (mode-specific defaults apply)' }, sessionId: { type: 'string', description: 'Optional server-issued session identifier for working-memory continuity. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102968:### Finding 1: Mnemosyne’s hybrid story stays inside one memory plane, while Public already codifies a federated retrieval stack
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102970:- **What it does**: README-documented Mnemosyne promises BM25 plus vector plus RRF, but wrapper-confirmed behavior exposes only memory tools over `mnemosyne search/add/delete`. Public, by contrast, explicitly routes memory retrieval to `memory_context` or `memory_search`, semantic code retrieval to `mcp__cocoindex_code__search`, and structural code retrieval to `code_graph_query`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102971:- **Why it matters for us**: The right lesson is not “merge everything semantic into one hybrid engine.” It is “keep memory retrieval as one plane in a larger task-routed system.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102972:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102973:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102975:### Finding 2: Public’s memory plane already goes deeper than Mnemosyne’s project/global split through `retrievalLevel` and community fallback
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102977:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed Mnemosyne has two collection scopes: project and global. Public’s memory handler already supports `retrievalLevel: local/global/auto`, and when enabled it can fall back from entity search into community summaries and inject member memories back into the result set.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102978:- **Why it matters for us**: Mnemosyne does not add a stronger retrieval model than Public already has inside the memory plane. Its value is ergonomic simplicity, not retrieval depth.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102979:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102980:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102982:### Finding 3: Public operationalizes retrieval federation at runtime; Mnemosyne mostly documents it as expected agent behavior
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102984:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s README recommends AGENTS instructions and its compaction hook reminds the agent that memory tools exist. Public goes further: bootstrap, auto-prime, and session-prime all surface concrete recovery calls and tool-routing guidance before the user has to remember the architecture.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102985:- **Why it matters for us**: Public’s federation is not just conceptual; it is already encoded into runtime guidance. That is a stronger pattern than Mnemosyne’s README-first discipline.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102986:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102987:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102989:### Finding 4: Mnemosyne’s project/global model is a thin UX alias over collection naming, not a replacement for Public’s governed scope model
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102991:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed Mnemosyne derives the project collection from `path.basename(targetDir)`, renames `global` to `default`, auto-inits the project collection at load, and lazily inits the global collection on first global write. Public’s exposed scope surfaces are instead `specFolder`, `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, and `sharedSpaceId`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102992:- **Why it matters for us**: Mnemosyne’s UX is attractive, but its basename-derived project key is much weaker than Public’s explicit governed scope model. If we copy anything, it should be the convenience aliasing, not the authority model.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102993:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102994:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102996:### Finding 5: Public has a contract-visibility gap: `retrievalLevel` exists in runtime behavior but not in the shared `memory_search` schema
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102998:- **What it does**: Public’s handler and runtime hints clearly support local or global memory retrieval plus automatic community fallback, but the shared `memory_search` schema block does not expose a `retrievalLevel` input at all. Mnemosyne avoids this kind of split because its project/global distinction is visible directly in separate tools.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102999:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest actionable gap from the second pattern: Public’s memory-plane federation is stronger than Mnemosyne’s, but its contract surface does not fully reveal that power.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103000:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103001:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103042:### Finding 1: Mnemosyne’s hybrid story stays inside one memory plane, while Public already codifies a federated retrieval stack

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103054:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103056:### Finding 3: Public operationalizes retrieval federation at runtime; Mnemosyne mostly documents it as expected agent behavior
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103058:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s README recommends AGENTS instructions and its compaction hook reminds the agent that memory tools exist. Public goes further: bootstrap, auto-prime, and session-prime all surface concrete recovery calls and tool-routing guidance before the user has to remember the architecture.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103059:- **Why it matters for us**: Public’s federation is not just conceptual; it is already encoded into runtime guidance. That is a stronger pattern than Mnemosyne’s README-first discipline.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103060:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103061:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103063:### Finding 4: Mnemosyne’s project/global model is a thin UX alias over collection naming, not a replacement for Public’s governed scope model
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103065:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed Mnemosyne derives the project collection from `path.basename(targetDir)`, renames `global` to `default`, auto-inits the project collection at load, and lazily inits the global collection on first global write. Public’s exposed scope surfaces are instead `specFolder`, `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, and `sharedSpaceId`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103066:- **Why it matters for us**: Mnemosyne’s UX is attractive, but its basename-derived project key is much weaker than Public’s explicit governed scope model. If we copy anything, it should be the convenience aliasing, not the authority model.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103067:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103068:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103070:### Finding 5: Public has a contract-visibility gap: `retrievalLevel` exists in runtime behavior but not in the shared `memory_search` schema
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103072:- **What it does**: Public’s handler and runtime hints clearly support local or global memory retrieval plus automatic community fallback, but the shared `memory_search` schema block does not expose a `retrievalLevel` input at all. Mnemosyne avoids this kind of split because its project/global distinction is visible directly in separate tools.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103073:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest actionable gap from the second pattern: Public’s memory-plane federation is stronger than Mnemosyne’s, but its contract surface does not fully reveal that power.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103074:- **Recommendation**: adopt now

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103128:### Finding 2: Public’s memory plane already goes deeper than Mnemosyne’s project/global split through `retrievalLevel` and community fallback
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103130:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed Mnemosyne has two collection scopes: project and global. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103287:- What it does: The README states that Mnemosyne combines SQLite FTS5/BM25 and sqlite-vec cosine similarity with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, while the wrapper simply forwards quoted search queries to `mnemosyne search`.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114669:### Finding 1: Init and first-use readiness fail late and ambiguously
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114671:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne auto-runs `mnemosyne init` for project/global collections but never checks the subprocess exit code in those init paths; the README also says first use downloads about 500 MB of models. If init or model download fails, the wrapper does not surface a readiness state up front. Public instead validates provider config, runs integrity checks, refuses embedding-dimension mismatches, and emits explicit degraded or fatal startup guidance.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114672:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the biggest operational gap. Missing model, permissions failure, corrupt collection, and init failure can all look like “tool broke later,” not “system is not ready.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114673:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114674:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114676:### Finding 2: The wrapper collapses most failures into raw stderr or one install hint
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114678:- **What it does**: `mnemosyne(...args)` returns a friendly message only for missing binary cases; everything else becomes raw stderr or a thrown error. The search/store/delete tools add almost no structure on top. Public exposes degraded-state metadata like `lexicalPath` and `fallbackState`, plus `memory_health` with confirmation-gated repair actions and clearer recovery paths.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114679:- **Why it matters for us**: We should not copy the “shell out and trust stderr” pattern into any governed memory surface. It hides failure classes that matter operationally.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114680:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114681:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114683:### Finding 3: Basename-derived project keys create rename, collision, and migration risk
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114685:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne derives project scope from `path.basename(targetDir)`, rewrites the basename `global` to `default`, and auto-inits that derived collection. That is ergonomic, but it also means repo renames, duplicate directory names, or worktree layout changes can silently remap memory authority. Public’s scope model is explicit and even guards against empty-database path drift during rebind.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114686:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a migration hazard, not just a naming quirk. A repo rename can look like “lost memory,” while same-basename repos can merge memory unintentionally.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114687:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114688:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114690:### Finding 4: Delete hygiene depends on parsing plain-text recall output
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114692:- **What it does**: Recall uses `--format plain`, and delete only accepts a numeric document ID that the agent is expected to pick out from bracketed text output. The wrapper does not attach scope, title, or structured confirmation to the delete flow.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114693:- **Why it matters for us**: This is workable for a light plugin, but brittle for automation. Stale IDs, misparsed output, or unclear scope boundaries raise deletion risk.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114694:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114695:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114697:### Finding 5: The compaction hook can advertise broken memory tools
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114699:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne always injects its memory-usage instructions during `experimental.session.compacting`; it does not check whether the binary is missing, init failed, or the backend is unhealthy. Public’s compaction transport only injects when a transport plan is available, tracks `runtimeReady`, preserves `lastRuntimeError`, and exposes a status tool.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114700:- **Why it matters for us**: The useful transferable idea is not “always remind the agent about memory.” It is “remind the agent only when the memory surface is actually healthy.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114701:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114702:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114704:### Finding 6: The wrapper adds per-call process overhead without its own scaling or repair controls
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114706:- **What it does**: Every Mnemosyne tool call launches a fresh CLI process. The wrapper does not add timeouts, batching, health caches, or repair orchestration of its own. Public keeps a persistent server process, mutexes DB reinitialization, bounds startup scans, forces WAL mode, and rebuilds BM25 from the database on startup.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114707:- **Why it matters for us**: A binary wrapper can be a good convenience facade, but it is a weak place to center scaling, migration, or corruption recovery.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114708:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114709:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114747:### Finding 1: Init and first-use readiness fail late and ambiguously
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114749:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne auto-runs `mnemosyne init` for project/global collections but never checks the subprocess exit code in those init paths; the README also says first use downloads about 500 MB of models. If init or model download fails, the wrapper does not surface a readiness state up front. Public instead validates provider config, runs integrity checks, refuses embedding-dimension mismatches, and emits explicit degraded or fatal startup guidance.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114750:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the biggest operational gap. Missing model, permissions failure, corrupt collection, and init failure can all look like “tool broke later,” not “system is not ready.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114751:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114752:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114754:### Finding 2: The wrapper collapses most failures into raw stderr or one install hint
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114756:- **What it does**: `mnemosyne(...args)` returns a friendly message only for missing binary cases; everything else becomes raw stderr or a thrown error. The search/store/delete tools add almost no structure on top. Public exposes degraded-state metadata like `lexicalPath` and `fallbackState`, plus `memory_health` with confirmation-gated repair actions and clearer recovery paths.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114757:- **Why it matters for us**: We should not copy the “shell out and trust stderr” pattern into any governed memory surface. It hides failure classes that matter operationally.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114758:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114759:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114761:### Finding 3: Basename-derived project keys create rename, collision, and migration risk
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114763:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne derives project scope from `path.basename(targetDir)`, rewrites the basename `global` to `default`, and auto-inits that derived collection. That is ergonomic, but it also means repo renames, duplicate directory names, or worktree layout changes can silently remap memory authority. Public’s scope model is explicit and even guards against empty-database path drift during rebind.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114764:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a migration hazard, not just a naming quirk. A repo rename can look like “lost memory,” while same-basename repos can merge memory unintentionally.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114765:- **Recommendation**: reject

The wrapper does not attach scope, title, or structured confirmation to the delete flow.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114856:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114892:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114939:15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every meaningful finding must cite exact file paths, say whether the evidence is wrapper-confirmed or README-level, explain why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, classify the recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, and identify the affected subsystem. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114984:- What it does: The README states that Mnemosyne combines SQLite FTS5/BM25 and sqlite-vec cosine similarity with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, while the wrapper simply forwards quoted search queries to `mnemosyne search`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114985:- Why it matters: Public should not copy README marketing language into implementation plans without deciding whether the value lies in ranking math, wrapper ergonomics, or agent-facing tool guidance.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114986:- Recommendation: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114996:- What it does: Mnemosyne appends a compact memory-usage instruction block during `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public's existing compaction plugin injects structural transport context rather than memory-tool reminders.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114997:- Why it matters: Public may already preserve structural state across compaction, but it does not yet remind the agent of concrete memory tools in the same direct way.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121703:### Finding 1: Health-gated memory compaction guidance is the easiest high-value transfer
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121705:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne appends a fixed memory-usage block during `experimental.session.compacting`; Public already has a transport-gated compaction pipeline with cache, `runtimeReady`, `lastRuntimeError`, and a status tool.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121706:- **Why it matters for us**: Integration effort is low because the hook plumbing already exists in Public. The work is mostly adding a memory-oriented compaction block to the transport plan, not changing retrieval internals.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121707:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121708:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121710:### Finding 2: Mnemosyne’s AGENTS/startup memory discipline can be absorbed as prompt policy, not architecture
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121712:- **What it does**: The README recommends a simple operator loop: recall at session start, store after decisions, delete contradictions, use global tools for cross-project preferences. Public already emits session-prime hints and startup instructions describing memory tools and retrieval modes.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121713:- **Why it matters for us**: Integration effort is low because this is mostly wording and prompt-shaping. The reusable value is the disciplined memory workflow, not any new backend mechanism.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121714:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121715:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121717:### Finding 3: First-class `project`/`global` tool aliases would be useful, but only as a thin wrapper over existing scoped retrieval
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121719:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne exposes separate recall/store tools for project and global scopes. Public already supports `retrievalLevel: local|global|auto`, scoped governance fields, and community fallback, but that power is hidden behind a denser MCP surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121720:- **Why it matters for us**: Integration effort is medium. Read-side aliases are easy, but write-side aliases must not blur governed scopes like `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, `sharedSpaceId`, and `specFolder`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121721:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121722:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121724:### Finding 4: `core=true` is only a viable shortcut if it maps cleanly onto Public’s constitutional-tier governance
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121726:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne treats `core` as a simple authoring flag. Public already has explicit importance tiers, constitutional injection, constitutional caching, and mutation-side validation around tier changes.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121727:- **Why it matters for us**: Integration effort is medium-high because this is not just a UI nicety. A `core=true` alias would have to map to constitutional or critical semantics, audit history, cache invalidation, and save/update guardrails without weakening governance.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121728:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121729:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121731:### Finding 5: A thin OpenCode-native memory plugin for Spec Kit Memory is feasible, but only as a delegation layer
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121733:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne packages five ergonomic tools as a native OpenCode plugin. Public already has richer MCP memory/recovery tooling, but it does not currently expose a similarly slim OpenCode memory facade.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121734:- **Why it matters for us**: Integration effort is medium-high. The facade itself is small, but only if it delegates to existing MCP truth instead of re-implementing search, save, health, or scope logic a second time.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121735:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
