/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121847:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121848:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121850:### Finding 4: `core=true` is only a viable shortcut if it maps cleanly onto Public’s constitutional-tier governance

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121853:- **Why it matters for us**: Integration effort is medium-high because this is not just a UI nicety. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:121991:- Why it matters: Public should not copy README marketing language into implementation plans without deciding whether the value lies in ranking math, wrapper ergonomics, or agent-facing tool guidance.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125379:### Finding 1: Adopting Mnemosyne-style compaction reminders risks duplicating Public’s existing compaction channels
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125382:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne appends a fixed memory-usage instruction block during `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public already has a transport-backed compaction note plus a separate `autoSurfaceAtCompaction()` path that injects retrieved memory/context.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125383:- **Why it matters for us**: The transfer is still attractive, but the main risk is prompt collision: we could end up with three compaction surfaces saying similar things in different voices, which weakens retrieval-policy clarity and increases prompt noise.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125384:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125385:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125387:### Finding 2: Reusing Mnemosyne’s AGENTS/startup discipline verbatim would misfit Public’s governed, file-backed memory model
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125390:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne recommends “recall at start, store after decisions, delete contradictions, use global for cross-project prefs.” Public already emits startup instructions, tool-routing hints, session priming, and automatic memory surfacing.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125391:- **Why it matters for us**: The risk is instruction drift. If we copy Mnemosyne’s wording directly, we imply cheap direct mutation semantics that Public does not have: `memory_save` is governed and file-path constrained, and deletion/update flows are more opinionated than a simple binary-backed store.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125392:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125393:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125395:### Finding 3: Thin `project` and `global` aliases are useful, but write-side aliases could blur Public’s scope boundaries
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125398:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne derives project scope from the current directory basename, special-cases `global`, and exposes separate recall/store tools. Public already supports `retrievalLevel: local|global|auto` plus governed save scope via `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, `sessionId`, and `sharedSpaceId`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125399:- **Why it matters for us**: Read aliases are low risk, but write aliases are not. A directory-basename mental model is much weaker than Public’s governed scope model and creates real collision/leak risks across similarly named repos, worktrees, or shared-memory spaces.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125400:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125401:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125403:### Finding 4: A `core=true` shortcut could erode constitutional-tier discipline if it acts like a simple tag
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125406:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne maps `core=true` to a `core` tag and describes it as always available context. Public’s “always surface” path is tied to the constitutional tier, token budgeting, cache keys, mutation hooks, and validated importance-tier updates.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125407:- **Why it matters for us**: The risk is semantic collapse. If `core=true` becomes a casual alias, operators will over-promote memories, inflate the constitutional budget, and create ambiguity between `constitutional`, `critical`, and merely “important” memories.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125408:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125409:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125411:### Finding 5: A native OpenCode memory facade is feasible, but a second authority layer would be the biggest adoption hazard
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125414:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne is a tiny OpenCode plugin that delegates everything to a local binary via argv-safe `Bun.spawn`. Public already has a richer MCP server plus OpenCode transport payloads for bootstrap/resume/compaction.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125415:- **Why it matters for us**: The biggest risk is split truth. If we add a native plugin that reinterprets save/search/scope logic instead of delegating to MCP, we will create semantic drift, duplicate failure handling, and mismatched recovery behavior. The offline-first dependency model also adds first-run latency and missing-binary failure states that Public’s current MCP surface does not require.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125416:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125417:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125457:### Finding 1: Adopting Mnemosyne-style compaction reminders risks duplicating Public’s existing compaction channels
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125460:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne appends a fixed memory-usage instruction block during `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public already has a transport-backed compaction note plus a separate `autoSurfaceAtCompaction()` path that injects retrieved memory/context.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125461:- **Why it matters for us**: The transfer is still attractive, but the main risk is prompt collision: we could end up with three compaction surfaces saying similar things in different voices, which weakens retrieval-policy clarity and increases prompt noise.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125462:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125463:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125465:### Finding 2: Reusing Mnemosyne’s AGENTS/startup discipline verbatim would misfit Public’s governed, file-backed memory model
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125468:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne recommends “recall at start, store after decisions, delete contradictions, use global for cross-project prefs.” Public already emits startup instructions, tool-routing hints, session priming, and automatic memory surfacing.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125469:- **Why it matters for us**: The risk is instruction drift. If we copy Mnemosyne’s wording directly, we imply cheap direct mutation semantics that Public does not have: `memory_save` is governed and file-path constrained, and deletion/update flows are more opinionated than a simple binary-backed store.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125470:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125471:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125473:### Finding 3: Thin `project` and `global` aliases are useful, but write-side aliases could blur Public’s scope boundaries
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125476:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne derives project scope from the current directory basename, special-cases `global`, and exposes separate recall/store tools. Public already supports `retrievalLevel: local|global|auto` plus governed save scope via `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, `sessionId`, and `sharedSpaceId`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125477:- **Why it matters for us**: Read aliases are low risk, but write aliases are not. A directory-basename mental model is much weaker than Public’s governed scope model and creates real collision/leak risks across similarly named repos, worktrees, or shared-memory spaces.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125478:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125479:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125481:### Finding 4: A `core=true` shortcut could erode constitutional-tier discipline if it acts like a simple tag
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125484:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne maps `core=true` to a `core` tag and describes it as always available context. Public’s “always surface” path is tied to the constitutional tier, token budgeting, cache keys, mutation hooks, and validated importance-tier updates.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125485:- **Why it matters for us**: The risk is semantic collapse. If `core=true` becomes a casual alias, operators will over-promote memories, inflate the constitutional budget, and create ambiguity between `constitutional`, `critical`, and merely “important” memories.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125486:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125487:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125489:### Finding 5: A native OpenCode memory facade is feasible, but a second authority layer would be the biggest adoption hazard
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125492:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne is a tiny OpenCode plugin that delegates everything to a local binary via argv-safe `Bun.spawn`. Public already has a richer MCP server plus OpenCode transport payloads for bootstrap/resume/compaction.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125493:- **Why it matters for us**: The biggest risk is split truth. If we add a native plugin that reinterprets save/search/scope logic instead of delegating to MCP, we will create semantic drift, duplicate failure handling, and mismatched recovery behavior. The offline-first dependency model also adds first-run latency and missing-binary failure states that Public’s current MCP surface does not require.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125494:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125556:- **Impact**: medium

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125641:15. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125686:- What it does: The README states that Mnemosyne combines SQLite FTS5/BM25 and sqlite-vec cosine similarity with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, while the wrapper simply forwards quoted search queries to `mnemosyne search`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125687:- Why it matters: Public should not copy README marketing language into implementation plans without deciding whether the value lies in ranking math, wrapper ergonomics, or agent-facing tool guidance.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125698:- What it does: Mnemosyne appends a compact memory-usage instruction block during `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public's existing compaction plugin injects structural transport context rather than memory-tool reminders.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125699:- Why it matters: Public may already preserve structural state across compaction, but it does not yet remind the agent of concrete memory tools in the same direct way.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:130836:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:130883:15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every meaningful finding must cite exact file paths, say whether the evidence is wrapper-confirmed or README-level, explain why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, classify the recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, and identify the affected subsystem. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:130928:- What it does: The README states that Mnemosyne combines SQLite FTS5/BM25 and sqlite-vec cosine similarity with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, while the wrapper simply forwards quoted search queries to `mnemosyne search`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:130929:- Why it matters: Public should not copy README marketing language into implementation plans without deciding whether the value lies in ranking math, wrapper ergonomics, or agent-facing tool guidance.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:130930:- Recommendation: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:130940:- What it does: Mnemosyne appends a compact memory-usage instruction block during `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public's existing compaction plugin injects structural transport context rather than memory-tool reminders.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:130941:- Why it matters: Public may already preserve structural state across compaction, but it does not yet remind the agent of concrete memory tools in the same direct way.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:130942:- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on desired runtime surface
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:130986:- each finding labeled `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:130998:- recommendation: `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131038:### Finding 1: Add a transport-owned memory reminder block at compaction time
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131040:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne injects an explicit “use memory tools” reminder into `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public’s compaction path currently carries continuity payloads and surfaced memories, not a direct tool-usage reminder.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131041:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest gap with the best fit: it improves post-compaction memory awareness without changing retrieval math or governance. Affected subsystem: OpenCode compaction transport.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131042:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131043:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131045:### Finding 2: Mnemosyne’s direct store/delete loop is incompatible with Public’s governed, file-backed mutation model
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131047:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne treats memory writes as direct CLI `add/delete` operations; Public routes saves through path validation, governed scope checks, preflight, PE arbitration, enrichment, and rollback-safe cleanup, while deletes support checkpointed folder cleanup.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131048:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the biggest semantic mismatch in the whole phase. We can reuse the discipline, but not the mutation surface. Affected subsystem: memory save/delete UX and operator guidance.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131049:- **Recommendation**: adopt now, but only as wording and workflow discipline, not as API semantics
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131050:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131052:### Finding 3: Read-side `project/global` aliases are worth prototyping, but only as thin wrappers over existing retrieval scope
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131054:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne exposes separate project/global recall tools; Public already has `retrievalLevel: local|global|auto`, but that capability is less ergonomic than Mnemosyne’s named split.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131055:- **Why it matters for us**: The read path is low-risk and high-clarity if we keep it alias-only. The write path should not copy this model because Public scope is governed, not basename-derived. Affected subsystem: memory recall ergonomics.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131056:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131057:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131059:### Finding 4: Mnemosyne’s AGENTS/startup discipline is reusable only after translating it into Public’s recovery authority
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131061:- **What it does**: The README recommends proactive recall/store/delete behavior from session start; Public already has startup instructions, bootstrap/resume authority, and routing nudges, but they are framed around MCP tools and recovery surfaces.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131062:- **Why it matters for us**: The right adoption is a rewrite, not a copy. We should teach “recall early, save after decisions, delete contradictions” using `session_bootstrap`, `memory_context`, and governed save flows. Affected subsystem: startup instructions and operator docs.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131063:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131064:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131066:### Finding 5: A direct `core=true` shortcut should be rejected on Public’s primary surface
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131068:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne maps `core` to a simple tag and treats it as always-available context; Public’s constitutional path has explicit tier validation, cache semantics, and token-budget enforcement.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131069:- **Why it matters for us**: A convenience alias would collapse an intentionally stricter hierarchy into a casual flag and encourage over-promotion. Affected subsystem: importance-tier semantics.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131070:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131071:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131073:### Finding 6: If we add an OpenCode-native memory facade, it must be delegation-only
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131075:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne proves that a very thin OpenCode plugin can be ergonomically nice, but Public already has a richer transport/MCP authority layer with startup, resume, and compaction payload planning.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131076:- **Why it matters for us**: A second implementation of save/search logic would create drift fast. The only safe version is a façade that delegates to MCP and adds zero new memory authority. Affected subsystem: OpenCode plugin integration.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131077:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE, but only as a delegation-only wrapper
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131078:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131080:### Finding 7: Mnemosyne’s offline-first binary/model stack is useful as an optional pattern, not as Public’s default deployment model
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131082:- **What it does**: The plugin assumes Bun, an installed `mnemosyne` binary, and a first-run local model download; its only runtime fallback is a “binary not found” error string.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131083:- **Why it matters for us**: The privacy story is good, but the operational cost is wrong for Public’s primary path, where MCP is already installed and governed. Affected subsystem: deployment/runtime strategy.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131084:- **Recommendation**: reject as the default adoption path
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131085:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131111:- Questions answered: Phase A adopt now: add a deduped memory-tool reminder to the existing compaction transport, and rewrite startup/AGENTS wording around `session_bootstrap` plus governed save flows. Phase B prototype later: add read-only `project/global` recall aliases that compile down to current retrieval scope, with no write aliases. Phase C hold or reject: do not add a direct `core=true` shortcut, do not replace Public’s retrieval stack with Mnemosyne, and only pursue an OpenCode-native facade if it delegates to MCP instead of becoming a second authority.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131132:### Finding 1: Add a transport-owned memory reminder block at compaction time
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131134:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne injects an explicit “use memory tools” reminder into `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public’s compaction path currently carries continuity payloads and surfaced memories, not a direct tool-usage reminder.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131135:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest gap with the best fit: it improves post-compaction memory awareness without changing retrieval math or governance. Affected subsystem: OpenCode compaction transport.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131136:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131137:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131139:### Finding 2: Mnemosyne’s direct store/delete loop is incompatible with Public’s governed, file-backed mutation model
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131141:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne treats memory writes as direct CLI `add/delete` operations; Public routes saves through path validation, governed scope checks, preflight, PE arbitration, enrichment, and rollback-safe cleanup, while deletes support checkpointed folder cleanup.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131142:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the biggest semantic mismatch in the whole phase. We can reuse the discipline, but not the mutation surface. Affected subsystem: memory save/delete UX and operator guidance.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131143:- **Recommendation**: adopt now, but only as wording and workflow discipline, not as API semantics
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131144:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131146:### Finding 3: Read-side `project/global` aliases are worth prototyping, but only as thin wrappers over existing retrieval scope
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131148:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne exposes separate project/global recall tools; Public already has `retrievalLevel: local|global|auto`, but that capability is less ergonomic than Mnemosyne’s named split.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131149:- **Why it matters for us**: The read path is low-risk and high-clarity if we keep it alias-only. The write path should not copy this model because Public scope is governed, not basename-derived. Affected subsystem: memory recall ergonomics.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131150:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131151:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131153:### Finding 4: Mnemosyne’s AGENTS/startup discipline is reusable only after translating it into Public’s recovery authority
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131155:- **What it does**: The README recommends proactive recall/store/delete behavior from session start; Public already has startup instructions, bootstrap/resume authority, and routing nudges, but they are framed around MCP tools and recovery surfaces.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131156:- **Why it matters for us**: The right adoption is a rewrite, not a copy. We should teach “recall early, save after decisions, delete contradictions” using `session_bootstrap`, `memory_context`, and governed save flows. Affected subsystem: startup instructions and operator docs.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131157:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131158:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131160:### Finding 5: A direct `core=true` shortcut should be rejected on Public’s primary surface
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131162:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne maps `core` to a simple tag and treats it as always-available context; Public’s constitutional path has explicit tier validation, cache semantics, and token-budget enforcement.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131163:- **Why it matters for us**: A convenience alias would collapse an intentionally stricter hierarchy into a casual flag and encourage over-promotion. Affected subsystem: importance-tier semantics.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131164:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131165:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131167:### Finding 6: If we add an OpenCode-native memory facade, it must be delegation-only
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131169:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne proves that a very thin OpenCode plugin can be ergonomically nice, but Public already has a richer transport/MCP authority layer with startup, resume, and compaction payload planning.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131170:- **Why it matters for us**: A second implementation of save/search logic would create drift fast. The only safe version is a façade that delegates to MCP and adds zero new memory authority. Affected subsystem: OpenCode plugin integration.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131171:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE, but only as a delegation-only wrapper
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131172:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131174:### Finding 7: Mnemosyne’s offline-first binary/model stack is useful as an optional pattern, not as Public’s default deployment model
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131176:- **What it does**: The plugin assumes Bun, an installed `mnemosyne` binary, and a first-run local model download; its only runtime fallback is a “binary not found” error string.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131177:- **Why it matters for us**: The privacy story is good, but the operational cost is wrong for Public’s primary path, where MCP is already installed and governed. Affected subsystem: deployment/runtime strategy.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131378:- Why it matters: Public should not copy README marketing language into implementation plans without deciding whether the value lies in ranking math, wrapper ergonomics, or agent-facing tool guidance.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131964:     4	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne recommends “recall at start, store after decisions, delete contradictions, use global for cross-project prefs.” Public already emits startup instructions, tool-routing hints, session priming, and automatic memory surfacing.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131965:     5	- **Why it matters for us**: The risk is instruction drift. If we copy Mnemosyne’s wording directly, we imply cheap direct mutation semantics that Public does not have: `memory_save` is governed and file-path constrained, and deletion/update flows are more opinionated than a simple binary-backed store.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131966:     6	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131967:     7	- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131972:    12	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne derives project scope from the current directory basename, special-cases `global`, and exposes separate recall/store tools. Public already supports `retrievalLevel: local|global|auto` plus governed save scope via `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, `sessionId`, and `sharedSpaceId`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131973:    13	- **Why it matters for us**: Read aliases are low risk, but write aliases are not. A directory-basename mental model is much weaker than Public’s governed scope model and creates real collision/leak risks across similarly named repos, worktrees, or shared-memory spaces.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131974:    14	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131975:    15	- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131980:    20	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne maps `core=true` to a `core` tag and describes it as always available context. Public’s “always surface” path is tied to the constitutional tier, token budgeting, cache keys, mutation hooks, and validated importance-tier updates.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131981:    21	- **Why it matters for us**: The risk is semantic collapse. If `core=true` becomes a casual alias, operators will over-promote memories, inflate the constitutional budget, and create ambiguity between `constitutional`, `critical`, and merely “important” memories.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131982:    22	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131983:    23	- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131988:    28	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne is a tiny OpenCode plugin that delegates everything to a local binary via argv-safe `Bun.spawn`. Public already has a richer MCP server plus OpenCode transport payloads for bootstrap/resume/compaction.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131989:    29	- **Why it matters for us**: The biggest risk is split truth. If we add a native plugin that reinterprets save/search/scope logic instead of delegating to MCP, we will create semantic drift, duplicate failure handling, and mismatched recovery behavior. The offline-first dependency model also adds first-run latency and missing-binary failure states that Public’s current MCP surface does not require.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131990:    30	- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131991:    31	- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:132029:    69	Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:132076:   116	15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every meaningful finding must cite exact file paths, say whether the evidence is wrapper-confirmed or README-level, explain why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, classify the recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, and identify the affected subsystem. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:132121:   161	- What it does: The README states that Mnemosyne combines SQLite FTS5/BM25 and sqlite-vec cosine similarity with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, while the wrapper simply forwards quoted search queries to `mnemosyne search`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:132122:   162	- Why it matters: Public should not copy README marketing language into implementation plans without deciding whether the value lies in ranking math, wrapper ergonomics, or agent-facing tool guidance.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:132123:   163	- Recommendation: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:132133:   173	- What it does: Mnemosyne appends a compact memory-usage instruction block during `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public's existing compaction plugin injects structural transport context rather than memory-tool reminders.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:132134:   174	- Why it matters: Public may already preserve structural state across compaction, but it does not yet remind the agent of concrete memory tools in the same direct way.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135527:### Finding 1: Mnemosyne is not a retrieval-engine upgrade for Public
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135529:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s wrapper forwards search requests to a backend whose BM25/vector/RRF internals are only README-documented, while Public already runs a visible multi-stage hybrid search pipeline.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135530:- **Why it matters for us**: The transferable value is wrapper ergonomics, not ranking replacement, so using Mnemosyne as a reason to re-architect Public retrieval would be a category error.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135531:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135532:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135534:### Finding 2: Compaction-time memory action guidance is the strongest adopt-now idea
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135536:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne preserves memory behavior across compaction with an explicit action card, while Public currently preserves continuity/state but not equally direct memory-tool verbs on the OpenCode surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135537:- **Why it matters for us**: A transport-owned, deduped memory reminder would improve post-compaction behavior without changing retrieval authority.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135538:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135539:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135541:### Finding 3: Mnemosyne’s startup/AGENTS discipline should be reused only after translation into Public terms
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135543:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne repeats “recall early, save after decisions, delete contradictions” in AGENTS and compaction, but Public’s save/delete paths are governed and more constrained.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135544:- **Why it matters for us**: The behavioral pattern is good, but the wording must point to `session_bootstrap`, `memory_context`, governed saves, and careful deletions instead of cheap direct CLI mutations.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135545:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135546:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135548:### Finding 4: Read-only `project` / `global` recall aliases are worth prototyping
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135550:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne gives simple project/global tool pairs; Public already has richer runtime scoping via `retrievalLevel` and broader fallback behavior.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135551:- **Why it matters for us**: Thin read aliases could improve ergonomics, but only if they compile down to existing scope logic and do not invent new authority.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135552:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135553:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135555:### Finding 5: Basename-derived project identity is too weak for Public’s memory authority
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135557:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne normalizes the current directory basename into a collection key and auto-inits that collection on load.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135558:- **Why it matters for us**: That is fragile across renames, sibling worktrees, and same-name repos, and it is materially weaker than Public’s governed scope model.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135559:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135560:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135562:### Finding 6: An ergonomic inline memory intake is valid only as a governed facade
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135564:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne lets the agent store concise strings directly, while Public’s durable save path is JSON-primary, file-backed, preflighted, and governed.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135565:- **Why it matters for us**: A `memory_remember`-style helper could be useful, but only if it produces the same governed save artifacts rather than bypassing them.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135566:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135567:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135569:### Finding 7: A direct `core=true` shortcut should not be added to Public’s main surface
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135571:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne collapses “always-on” behavior into a simple `core` tag, while Public reserves that behavior for explicit constitutional-tier handling and budgeted cache/query logic.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135572:- **Why it matters for us**: A shortcut would blur `constitutional`, `critical`, and ordinary memories and encourage over-promotion.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135573:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135574:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135576:### Finding 8: Mnemosyne’s blind append and hard-delete lifecycle is the wrong primary mutation model for Public
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135578:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne exposes append-plus-delete behavior at the wrapper boundary, while Public wraps updates/deletes in transactions, history, ledgering, checkpointing, and hook invalidation.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135579:- **Why it matters for us**: Public should keep its auditable mutation model and treat contradiction cleanup as structured revision, not as plain-text ID deletion.
